title: Assistant Vice President
description: **Date:** 5 May 2026 **Location:** Dublin, IE, D01 W213 **Company:** Walkers Global We are a leading international law and professional services firm providing legal, corporate and fiduciary services to global corporations, financial institutions, capital market participants and investment fund managers. With a global presence spanning the Americas, Europe, the Middle East and Asia, we advise on the laws of Bermuda, the British Virgin Islands, the Cayman Islands, Guernsey, Ireland and Jersey. We treat everyone as the intelligent professional they are. Our approach is to trust and empower our people to deliver consistently, and enable them to succeed. Diversity is our secret weapon – it’s the sheer breadth of Walkers people that makes us who we are – gathered from across the globe and fluent in languages, jurisdictions and cultures that help us to mirror our clients and keep our own thinking in tune with the world in which we operate. **Overview of role** -------------------- We are currently seeking an experienced Transaction Manager to join our dynamic and growing transaction management team in Dublin where we focus on serving the structured finance and aviation finance industry. In this role, you will be part of a busy, client facing, professionally qualified team. This is an excellent opportunity to play a key role in a highly regarded and expanding organisation which places huge emphasis on continuous professional development and career progression within a collegiate team\-based environment. **Duties and Responsibilities** ------------------------------- You will have a broad range of responsibilities including: * Act as transaction manager for your own portfolio of structured finance and aircraft leasing special purpose vehicles (SPVs); * Assist with the management of the day\-to\-day workload of junior staff; * Serve as director to a portfolio of SPVs, being the primary point of contact for all fiduciary and transaction management issues; * Demonstrate complex problem\-solving skills, anticipate issues, lead client board meetings and effectively manage a busy portfolio of clients and team members with competing deadlines and deliverables; * Build and maintain strong client relationships ensuring a high quality, responsive and professional service to our clients at all times; * Lead and manage the delivery of internal regulatory, operational and ad hoc projects and initiatives as required; * Demonstrate awareness and understanding of legal or regulatory developments and proactively seek to ensure that the team is positioned to address those challenges; and * Ensure compliance with internal policies and procedures, while actively contributing to process improvements within the team. **Knowledge\& Experience** -------------------------- * A minimum of six years' experience in a relevant legal, financial services or corporate administration role is essential; * Strong academic credentials with a university degree. A legal, company secretarial or accounting professional qualification would be an advantage; * Strong inter\-personal skills and communication skills. * Energy, enthusiasm and a proactive and solution\-driven attitude; * Ability to think on your feet, show attention to detail and be able to plan and organise work effectively; * Strong organisational, analytical and decision\-making skills with excellent attention to detail; and * A desire to work in a fast\-paced client\-facing environment where delivery of a professional service to both clients and other counterparties is of key importance. **Our Benefits** ---------------- We offer excellent progression opportunities along with a competitive remuneration package and benefits including: * Discretionary annual bonus scheme * 25 days annual leave * Hybrid Working Policy * Health insurance cover * Pension scheme * Life Assurance * Income Protection * Taxsaver and Bike to Work schemes * Professional Membership Fees * Social Committee * Employee Assistance Programme * Wellness programme Walkers global is an equal opportunity employer. Equality and diversity are key to our global identity and an integral part of our goal to continue being an employer of choice. We are committed to a work environment that supports all individuals irrespective of gender, ethnicity, nationality, race, religion, marital status, age, disability, pregnancy, sexual orientation, gender identity or any other applicable legally protected characteristics. We make every effort to ensure that employment opportunities are open and accessible to all purely on the basis of personal ability.

title: Associate, Fund Reporting Representative II
description: Associate, Fund Reporting Representative II At BNY, our culture allows us to run our company better and enables employees’ growth and success. As a leading global financial services company at the heart of the global financial system, we influence nearly 20% of the world’s investible assets. Every day, our teams harness cutting\-edge AI and breakthrough technologies to collaborate with clients, driving transformative solutions that redefine industries and uplift communities worldwide. Recognized as a top destination for innovators, BNY is where bold ideas meet advanced technology and exceptional talent. Together, we power the future of finance – and this is what \#LifeAtBNY is all about. Join us and be part of something extraordinary. We’re seeking a future team member for the role of **Associate, Fund Reporting Representative II** to join our Financial Reporting team. This role is located in Dublin, Ireland. **In this role, you’ll make an impact in the following ways:** * Prepare and review financial statements and accounts working papers for alternative investment funds on behalf of BNY clients, ensuring compliance with appropriate accounting standards (e.g. IFRS, US GAAP, Lux GAAP, etc.) and other regulations * Supervise and monitor production of financial statements and of accounts working papers files * Plan, organize and monitor the production of financial statements and ancillary information for collective investment vehicles, ensuring timely and accurate delivery to agreed timetables and SLA standards * Liaison with appropriate external and internal parties re team deliverables and information needed to produce financial statements **To be successful in this role, we’re seeking the following:** * Experienced accountant (4 years \&\#43;) with sound double entry and financial accounting knowledge including IFRS or equivalent standards exposure preferably within a fund administrator, or Big 4 firm with fund investments exposure * Accounting qualification Qualified (ACA / CIMA / ACCA) or Part Qualified (ACA / ACCA) \[preferable] * Strong accounting knowledge covering relevant accounting standards and regulations including IFRS, US GAAP and Lux GAAP, and investment fund structures (Mutual funds, hedge funds etc.) * Experienced in use of financial reporting platforms, including Confluence Unity and/or DFIN Arc Reporting, to prepare, review, and publish accurate financial reporting * Deadline driven with effective time management and ability to work under pressure BNY Newsroom BNY LinkedIn **Here’s a few of our recent awards:** * America’s Most Innovative Companies, Fortune, 2025 * World’s Most Admired Companies, Fortune 2025 “Most Just Companies”, Just Capital and CNBC, 2025 * **Our Benefits and Rewards:** BNY offers highly competitive compensation, benefits, and wellbeing programs rooted in a strong culture of excellence and our pay\-for\-performance philosophy. We provide access to flexible global resources and tools for your life’s journey. Focus on your health, foster your personal resilience, and reach your financial goals as a valued member of our team, along with generous paid leaves, including paid volunteer time, that can support you and your family through moments that matter. BNY is an Equal Employment Opportunity/Affirmative Action Employer \- Underrepresented racial and ethnic groups/Females/Individuals with Disabilities/Protected Veterans.

title: Automation Engineer
description: **Senior Automation Engineer** About the Company **Our client is a leading multinational biopharmaceutical manufacturing organisation operating a state\-of\-the\-art aseptic drug product facility. The site specialises in secondary manufacturing operations including formulation, vial and syringe filling, lyophilisation, inspection, and packaging, supporting global product supply across international markets.** **The organisation promotes a strong culture of innovation, continuous improvement, operational excellence, and compliance within a highly regulated GMP manufacturing environment.** **About the Role** The Senior Automation Engineer will join the Plant Automation Team and play a key role in supporting manufacturing operations, maintaining critical automation systems, and delivering optimisation and continuous improvement initiatives across the site. This position will act as System Owner for key automation assets, leading troubleshooting activities, lifecycle management, system reliability improvements, and automation project support within a GMP\-regulated manufacturing environment. **Key Responsibilities** System Ownership \& Reliability * Act as System Owner for critical automation systems, overseeing lifecycle maintenance, upgrades, and validation activities * Manage Change Controls, Deviations, and CAPAs associated with automation systems * Maintain system documentation, configuration management, and software version control * Support long\-term system reliability and performance initiatives **Support \& Troubleshooting** * Provide technical support to manufacturing and engineering teams during production activities, shutdowns, and technology transfers * Lead or support root cause investigations and troubleshooting of automation\-related issues * Assist with resolving system faults and minimising downtime within manufacturing operations **Continuous Improvement \& Innovation** * Implement CAPAs including software modifications and updates to SOPs and technical documentation * Review existing automation system designs and recommend improvements aligned with industry best practices and regulatory requirements * Support digitalisation and Industry 4\.0 initiatives including data integration and advanced automation technologies **Project \& Technology Integration** * Support new product introductions and technology transfer activities through engineering assessments and automation system modifications * Collaborate with cross\-functional teams, stakeholders, and external vendors to deliver projects successfully * Support engineering trials, commissioning, and implementation activities **Safety \& Compliance** * Promote a strong culture of safety and compliance across all activities * Ensure adherence to GMP, EHS, and site quality standards * Support validation and compliance activities in line with regulatory expectations **Requirements** * Bachelor’s Degree in Automation Engineering, Electrical Engineering, Computer Science, Chemical Engineering, Biotechnology Engineering, or related discipline * Minimum 5 years’ experience within a manufacturing or operations environment, ideally within pharmaceutical or biotech industries * Strong background in automation system design, installation, validation, testing, programming, and lifecycle management * Experience with one or more of the following: * Biopharmaceutical formulation or vial/syringe filling systems * Inspection or packaging automation systems * Siemens or Rockwell PLC/HMI platforms * SCADA systems including iFix, InTouch, Zenon, PCS, or Rockwell FTView * Industrial fieldbus technologies such as ControlNet, DeviceNet, Profibus, ProfiNet, AS\-I, or BACnet * Knowledge of GAMP lifecycle methodology, ANSI/ISA\-S88 and S95 standards, 21 CFR Part 11, and Annex 11 * Understanding of industrial networking technologies including TCP/IP and Firewalls * Strong problem\-solving, communication, and stakeholder management skills * Self\-motivated with the ability to work independently and within cross\-functional teams * Experience working within GMP\-regulated manufacturing environments preferred * Flexibility to travel occasionally for vendor support and training activities

title: BIM Coordinator
description: **Description** At John Paul Construction, we believe every project is an opportunity to improve on what has come before. To foster that mindset, we hire the brightest and best in the industry, encourage collaboration, and embrace innovative thinking and new technology. Due to continued expansion, we are hiring a VDC BIM Coordinator for our various projects in Dublin and Head Office. **Key Responsibilities*** Take full responsibility for the BIM process on the project. * Implement and maintain the BIM Execution Plan. * Ensure all project team members adhere to the BIM Execution Plan. * Establish BIM coordination workshops and report and record progress. * Record BIM models and their status. * Establish quality control procedures to check all models are accurate and that the level of model definition is appropriate for each project stage. * Identify and document clashes between different models using Navisworks Manage, and chair clash detection meetings with other parties to the contract, including design consultants and sub\-contractors. * Advise members of the team of significant clashes and information issues associated with the BIM aspect of the works. * Record and monitor shared data and relationships between models (grids, floor levels, and shared coordinates). * Identify and agree shared technical requirements, software package interoperability, and BIM standards to be delivered by each team member. * Monitor the uploading of information models to the Common Data Environment, ensuring that they are compliant with the BIM Execution Plan. * Co\-ordinate handover of models and data at the agreed milestones. * Carry out model generation from time to time as necessary to facilitate the development of the design on the project. * Ensure that the level of skill and expertise is kept up to date in what is a very rapidly changing environment. * All other duties associated with this role. **Specific Requirements*** Third\-level qualification in Construction / Structural / Services. * Minimum 5 years’ post\-graduate experience in a related field. * Previous experience working for a main contractor is desirable. * Proficient in Microsoft Office (Excel, Word). * Proficient in Navisworks Manage. * Working knowledge of AutoCAD, Revit MEP, and Revit Architecture. * Knowledge and understanding of BIM procedures and protocols. * Excellent communication skills. * Ability to work on own initiative. **Package** As a permanent employee of John Paul Construction, you will benefit from:* Competitive salary * Relocation allowance (where applicable) * Company laptop and phone * Pension * Life insurance and income protection * Continuous Professional Development (CPD) * Access to lifestyle benefits such as the Bike to Work scheme, Employee Assistance Programme, Sports \& Social Club, and more We are proud to be an equal opportunity employer. We believe in fostering a work environment where all individuals are treated with dignity and respect, and are given the opportunity to reach their full potential.

title: Back End Engineer
description: **Backend Engineer @The Dock** **About The Dock** The Dock is Accenture’s flagship R\&D and Global Innovation Center, based in Dublin’s Grand Canal Dock. As an incubation hub for the next generation of AI and GenAI agentic solutions, The Dock brings together a global community of business strategists, designers, engineers, data scientists, and entrepreneurs. Our mission is to solve some of the world’s hardest problems and create transformational products and services that help industries thrive in the face of unprecedented change. From shaping the future of work with intelligent systems to building breakthrough digital platforms, our team operates across the entire innovation spectrum, from exploration to real‑world deployment. At The Dock, we experiment boldly, collaborate openly, and build the future in real time. **About The Role** We’re looking for a Backend Engineering to join our team at The Dock. You’ll be part of an innovation\-driven technical team helping to design, build and deliver products with the latest technologies—from early prototypes to scalable production systems. As a backend developer, you’ll take an active role in technical hands\-on work, contributing to designing, developing and maintaining robust, secure and scalable code. You’ll be responsible for owning specific technical components of a build such as APIs layers, error handling, messaging queues or authentication layers, ensuring they are consistent with the coding standards. For this role, you must have a strong appetite for continues learning and interest in exploring technology trends and developments in your domain of expertise. Knowledge sharing and collaboration are central to this role. You’ll work closely with Designers, Product managers, Data Engineers and DevOps teams on a daily and you must be able to communicate your domain expertise to both technical and non\-technical team members. **Responsibilities:** - You must have a curious approach to problem solving, bringing a systems thinking mindset to the role and a strong sense of ownership. - Write clean, maintainable, well\-tested and documented code. - Run code reviews for more junior team members and mentor them to become more proficient developers. - Contribute to fortnightly knowledge sharing with the wider team, presenting points of view on recent projects or technologies. - Quickly assess new technologies, design patterns or approaches and asses their feasibility for use with your given project, - Lead the continuous improvement of development processes ensuring both your own code and the code of others is maintainable and extensible. - Use AI tools to accelerate engineering tasks, automate processes and improve quality and documentation where appropriate. - Own specific technical component areas and refine the business requirements into functional and non\-functional requirements. **Required experience \& skills:** - Proven experience developing software in agile teams, including defining and estimating work as part of sprint planning. - Strong object‑oriented programming expertise in at least two modern ecosystems (e.g. Python, Node.js, Java), with experience building enterprise‑grade APIs and microservices. - Hands\-on experience with cloud platforms (AWS or Azure), particularly serverless architectures, and implementing secure authentication and authorization solutions. - Solid understanding of distributed systems, data persistence (relational, NoSQL, and vector databases), and performance optimization considerations. - Familiarity with DevOps practices, including CI/CD pipelines, Git, Docker, and infrastructure‑as‑code technologies. **Additional Skills We Value** - Exposure to/ understanding of Generative AI/Agentic technologies (LLM, MCP) - Data engineering skills on Apache Spark, Azure Data factory, Apache Kafka, Data Bricks etc - Interest in system architecture, data structures, algorithms and modern software design patterns.

title: Benefits/HR Specialist - EMEA
description: **Company Description** Sandisk understands how people and businesses consume data and we relentlessly innovate to deliver solutions that enable today’s needs and tomorrow’s next big ideas. With a rich history of groundbreaking innovations in Flash and advanced memory technologies, our solutions have become the beating heart of the digital world we’re living in and that we have the power to shape. Sandisk meets people and businesses at the intersection of their aspirations and the moment, enabling them to keep moving and pushing possibility forward. We do this through the balance of our powerhouse manufacturing capabilities and our industry\-leading portfolio of products that are recognized globally for innovation, performance and quality. Sandisk has two facilities recognized by the World Economic Forum as part of the Global Lighthouse Network for advanced 4IR innovations. These facilities were also recognized as Sustainability Lighthouses for breakthroughs in efficient operations. With our global reach, we ensure the global supply chain has access to the Flash memory it needs to keep our world moving forward. **Job Description** **Payroll‑Linked Benefits Processing** * Proactively prepare monthly benefits updates for payroll, including bulk changes and required calculation adjustments. * Own the monthly benefits to payroll task list and support accurate data flow across HR, Payroll, and Finance. * Manage absence‑related benefit and payroll impacts. * Support employees with payroll‑linked benefit queries. **Vendor \& Financial Management** * Process and reconcile benefit invoices, resolving discrepancies with vendors. * Manage third‑party payments and statutory insurance requirements, including raising POs when needed. **Benefits Administration** * Support the day‑to‑day benefits operations across EMEA, including enrolments, life‑cycle changes, and leaver processing. * Manage ServiceNow benefits queries and provide clear, timely employee support. * Coordinate annual statutory health checks and handle employee questions on pensions, health insurance, and statutory benefits. * Support benefits onboarding/offboarding and keep all benefits materials—summaries, portals, forms—current and engaging. * Contribute to wellness initiatives, benefits projects, and ongoing improvements to the employee experience. * Ensure full compliance across pensions, ESPP, and statutory benefit requirements. **HR Support \& Employee Services** * Act as a Tier 2 advisor for escalated HR and benefits queries. * Collaborate closely with HR and Payroll to maintain accuracy and alignment across people processes. * Support group HR initiatives and the New Hire Orientation program. * Maintain clean, accurate Benefits \& HR records. **Process \& Compliance Oversight** * Keep SOPs and process documentation updated, clear, and efficient. * Ensure all benefits processing meets compliance, revenue, and country‑specific regulatory requirements. **Workday Systems Support** * Support Workday configuration updates and enhancements in partnership with the HR and Workday IT teams. * Run \& analyse reports to support Benefit and Payroll administration. **Qualifications** * Bachelor’sdegreeinhumanresources,businessadministration,orarelatedfield \- mandatory * Professional HR certifications\- an advantage * 3\+ years’ experience in EMEA HR and/or Benefits Administration in a similar sized international organisation * Additional language skills are a plus, especially Czech, German, Dutch, or French. * Basic knowledge of EMEA company sponsored and statutory benefits * Exceptional organisation, time management, and collaboration skills. * Demonstratingstrong attention to detail and the ability to anticipate the downstream impact of changes affecting employee benefits, communications, and cross‑functional areas such as HR, Payroll, and Finance. Absolute confidentiality is essential. **Additional Information** Sandisk thrives on the power and potential of diversity. As a global company, we believe the most effective way to embrace the diversity of our customers and communities is to mirror it from within. We believe the fusion of various perspectives results in the best outcomes for our employees, our company, our customers, and the world around us. We are committed to an inclusive environment where every individual can thrive through a sense of belonging, respect and contribution. Sandisk is committed to offering opportunities to applicants with disabilities and ensuring all candidates can successfully navigate our careers website and our hiring process. Please contact us at jobs.accommodations@sandisk.com to advise us of your accommodation request. In your email, please include a description of the specific accommodation you are requesting as well as the job title and requisition number of the position for which you are applying.

title: Biology Researcher (Masters/PhDs)
description: **Biology Researcher (Masters/PhDs) \- AI Training** **About The Role** What if your years of biology expertise could directly shape how AI reasons through the most complex problems in genetics, biotechnology, and bioinformatics? We're looking for advanced biology experts to help build and train cutting\-edge AI models — ensuring they think with the depth, rigor, and precision that real science demands. This is a fully remote, flexible contract role designed for Masters and PhD\-level biologists who want to make a meaningful impact beyond the lab. No prior AI experience required — your scientific expertise is exactly what we need. * Organization: Alignerr * Type: Hourly Contract * Location: Remote * Commitment: 10–40 hours/week **What You'll Do** * Design, solve, and critically evaluate advanced biology problems with real\-world scientific relevance * Apply your expertise in genetics, biotechnology, bioinformatics, or related disciplines to create complex, nuanced problem sets * Collaborate asynchronously with AI researchers and fellow domain experts to enhance AI model reasoning and accuracy * Assess the scientific rigor, clarity, and depth of AI\-generated biological content * Ensure outputs meet the standards expected of graduate\-level research and scholarship **Who You Are** * Master's or PhD in Biology or a closely related field — Genetics, Molecular Biology, Bioinformatics, Biotechnology, or equivalent * Strong working knowledge of genetics, biotechnology, or bioinformatics * Comfortable coding in Python or R for research or data analysis tasks * Exceptional written communicator — able to explain complex science with precision and clarity * Detail\-oriented, methodical, and committed to scientific accuracy * Self\-motivated and reliable when working independently and asynchronously **Nice to Have** * Prior experience with data annotation, data quality review, or AI evaluation workflows * Background in academic publishing, peer review, or scientific writing * Familiarity with computational biology tools or bioinformatics pipelines * Experience designing or evaluating research problems in an academic or industry setting **Why Join Us** * Work on cutting\-edge AI projects alongside the world's leading research labs * Fully remote and flexible — structure your hours around your life, not the other way around * Freelance autonomy with meaningful, intellectually stimulating work * Put your advanced degree to work in a fast\-growing, high\-impact field * Contribute to AI systems that will shape how science is understood and communicated at scale * Potential for ongoing work and contract extension as new projects launch

title: Bioprocess Associate
description: ### **Job Information** Date Opened 05/05/2026 Job Type Full time Industry Pharma/Biotech/Clinical Research Work Experience 1\-3 years City Dundalk State/Province Louth Country Ireland ### **Job Description** **About PSC Biotech** **Who are we?** PSC Biotech is a leading Biotech Consultancy firm founded in 1996, headquartered in Pomona, California, USA, with Global operations in Ireland, India, Singapore, Australia and the US, serving 350 clients in more than 23 countries worldwide. We provide cloud\-based software solutions for Quality Management and Regulatory Inspections, pharmaceuticals contract manufacturing professionals, and metrology services to our clients. **‘Take your Career to a new Level’** PSC Biotech disrupts the conventional consultancy model by aligning our EVP as one of the unique selling point which includes the opportunity to work with the most talented cohort of like\-minded professionals operating in the Pharma/ Biotech Industry. We offer a permanent contract of employment giving exposure of working in Top Pharmaceutical client sites in a diverse\-cultural work setting. **Employee Value Proposition** Employees are the “heartbeat” of PSC Biotech, we provide unparalleled empowering career development though Learning \& Development in\-house training mentorship through constant guidance to facilitate career progression. We believe in creating high performing teams that can exceed our client’s expectations with regards to quality of all scalable and business unit deliverables, staying under budget and ensuring timelines for our deliverables are being met. **Overview:** We are looking for a BioProcess Associate – to join our Drug Substance team at our state\-of\-the\-art, fully integrated vaccine facility in Ireland. As a Bioprocessing Associate, you will be a key member in our fast\-growing, dynamic startup\-working with cutting\-edge technology. ### **Requirements** **Responsibilities:** * Manufacturing processes include DS Downstream Processing (DSP) and Upstream Processing (USP) and Buffer / Media preparation * Carry out all production operations in the assigned area as per the relevant SOPs and Manufacturing Batch Records . * Assist in maintaining a safety orientated Culture, cGMP compliant work environment at all times * Write, review and revision of area Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs), On the Job Training (OJT’s), Production Batch Records (PBRs), Logbooks, Training Competencies and Work Instructions * Training and mentoring of colleagues in SOPs, process execution and equipment operation. * Support Manufacturing team to consistently deliver on specific area Key Performance Indicators (KPIs), e.g. Safety, Quality, Schedule adherence, Overall Equipment Effectiveness (OEE), and team training. * Assist with the investigation of and operations deviations through Quality System, engaging with all relevant personnel and functions as appropriate. * Lead and actively participate in shift handovers. * Liaise with Shift Lead regarding issues which may arise within the production area including highlighting of operational challenges. * Use Lean Tools as part of daily operations (Standard Work, 5S, TPM, Gemba, Method 1,) to optimize efficiency and drive the culture of Continuous Improvement (CI) * Support HAZOP and risk assessments as per requirements. * Provide assistance and/or support maintenance, engineering, quality or other colleagues as requested. * Conducts all activities that are in accordance with Company policies \& SOPs \& global regulatory guidelines (including cGMP/cGLP/cGCP), environmental guidelines, as appropriate. * Support the development, commissioning \& qualification of a new vaccines drug substance manufacturing equipment and subsequent transition to commercial manufacturing. **What skills you will need:** * Demonstrated experience in Bioprocessing, Upstream or Downstream. * Knowledge and experience of relevant cGMP, safety, and environmental regulations within the biopharmaceutical industry. * High level of adaptability working in a fast\-paced environment and champion change. * Experience with recording information in real time using electronic or manual systems. * Experience in executing operations while following SOPs, Batch Records and Manufacturing Controls Systems. * Experience of working in Grade C, D and CNC environment is an advantage. * Excellent interpersonal skills, positive influence, team orientation, attention to detail, documentation skills, problem identification and problem\-solving skills. * An ability to work independently, and as a member of a self \- managed shift team in a dynamic, fast\-paced environment that requires flexibility and initiative. **Shift Pattern:** This position will initially operate standard office hours Monday\-Friday but will be transition in Extended 7\-day shift pattern with 12\-hour shifts, designed to provide 24/7 coverage using a 4\-week repeating cycle. Two teams rotate through this schedule **Example :** * Week 1: Work Monday, Tuesday, Friday, Saturday, Sunday (12\-hour day shifts),Off Wednesday and Thursday * Week 2: Work Wednesday and Thursday only, Off the other 5 days * Week 3: Same as Week 1: Work Monday, Tuesday, Friday, Saturday, Sunday * Week 4 Same as Week 2 :Work Wednesday and Thursday only \#LI\-EL1

title: Brand & Marketing Graduate
description: Brand \& Marketing Graduate, Ballycoolin Business Park, Dublin Apply Now ***Mercury is the European leader in construction solutions.*** We build and manage complex engineering \& construction projects for the world’s leading corporations. Our solutions help deliver technologies and life changing advancements that connect people, communities, and businesses, giving them the power to achieve incredible things. Our people have the courage to be innovative. Their determination and sharp focus enable us to deliver with certainty, time and time again. We deliver our clients’ visions through leading edge construction solutions across multiple sectors, including Enterprise Data Centres, Advanced Technology \& Life Sciences, Hyperscale Data Centres, Fire Protection. At Mercury, it is our duty to encourage and back our people to realise their vision of themselves. We place them at the heart of what we do, providing challenging opportunities to develop within a great team in a supportive environment that allows them to reach their full potential. **Our Graduate Programme** We’re seeking the leaders of tomorrow to join us on our Graduate Programme \- those who are willing to share our values, live our culture and deliver beyond expectations. At Mercury we know that to achieve incredible things you must invest in your people. Our dedication to shaping the leaders of tomorrow shines through our highly regarded graduate programme and ensures we stay ahead in a competitive market. From day one you will be on a stellar career trajectory with no limits to what you can achieve, and the programme empowers you to develop the essential skills needed to excel in the fast\-paced construction industry. Since launching in 2009, we have successfully trained 360\+ graduates, many of whom have forged long\-lasting careers with us. Our comprehensive eighteen\-month programme is tailored to meet real\-world demands, featuring courses led by both external and internal subject matter experts in a focused learning environment. Graduates on our programme can anticipate working alongside a diverse and supportive team, accessing state\-of\-the\-art training and masterclasses, and gaining international experience at the leading edge of industry. An important aspect of the development programme is the completion of a group research project. Graduates are split into small groups of four or five and assigned a strategic business project to research and present their findings to Senior Management. All topics are chosen by Senior Management and are relevant to Mercury's business. **Scope of Role** The Brand and Marketing Graduate will play a pivotal role in delivering high‑impact branding and marketing initiatives that position Mercury as a European leader in construction solutions. You will support the development, implementation, and optimisation of brand and digital marketing activities across the organisation, collaborating closely with internal teams and construction sites throughout Europe. Key responsibilities include managing the Merchandise Playbook, coordinating marketing and brand support for events and teams, and ensuring consistent, high\-impact brand visuals and messaging across all marketing channels. You'll contribute to design, select premium teamwear, and explore innovative trends within the brand space. This is a fast paced, hands\-on role ideal for someone eager to learn about branding design, photography, videography, and digital marketing. You will gain valuable experience in vendor management, stakeholder engagement, and creative delivery, while helping drive marketing and brand success. **Key Responsibilities Of The Role** * Provide first\-line user support for merchandising and branding platforms, resolving issues promptly. * Support the development and maintenance of merchandise, events, and construction site playbooks. * Manage and coordinate product requests, review production files, and oversee the production and distribution of promotional materials. * Review vendor price lists and lead\-times, recommending innovative products and trends that align with brand guidelines. * Conduct product testing to ensure performance, reliability and compliance with brand standards. * Monitor and update the branding intranet, ensuring content accuracy, consistency and functionality. * Collaborate with the marketing team to create world\-class content and contribute to the optimisation of online touchpoints. * Contribute to the creation of marketing content including photography, videography, social media assets, and campaign visuals. * Ensure consistency across all communications, marketing channels, and physical environments. * Ensure timely monitoring and escalation of issues of alerts as necessary. * Demonstrate strong attention to detail in all aspects of execution and visual delivery. **Essential Criteria** * Ideally 2:1 Honours Degree / Expected 2:1 * Strong understanding of design principles and branding guidelines. * Excellent organisational and communication skills. * Proficiency in design software (e.g., Adobe Creative Suite) and an interest in content creation tools (photography, videography, editing). **Desirable Criteria** * Experience in the Construction Industry or Trade Background * Experience in branding, marketing, or graphic design related roles. ***Mercury is an equal opportunities employer.*** Apply Now

title: Business & Marketing Associate
description: Please note: This position is not eligible for employment permit sponsorship. Candidates must have the right to work in Ireland at the time of application. **About us** Future Direct is a dynamic and rapidly expanding consultancy firm specialising in Irish immigration services, serving a diverse range of B2B and B2C clients globally. We take immense pride in the life\-changing nature of our work, and we offer our team a culture of continuous learning, genuine career progression, and a supportive environment where your contributions make a real difference. **The role** We are looking for an energetic and versatile Business \& Marketing Associate to join our growing team in Dublin. This is a hybrid role, approximately 70% focused on marketing and branding, with 30% supporting business operations and is well suited to someone who enjoys wearing multiple hats in a fast\-paced, purpose\-driven environment. As Future Direct continues to grow, this role has the potential to evolve and branch out in line with business needs, offering the right candidate the opportunity to grow with us. **What you will do** **Marketing \& Branding** * Plan and execute content across social media platforms including LinkedIn, Instagram, and others * Support the development and maintenance of brand identity and visual assets * Drive marketing campaigns, email marketing, and the production of promotional materials such as leaflets and brochures * Identify and coordinate attendance at relevant industry events and networking opportunities * Produce written content including blogs, articles, and promotional copy * Support website management, SEO initiatives, and AI\-driven search optimisation **Business \& Administration** * Provide general administrative support across departments as needed * Assist with internal coordination, scheduling, and documentation * Handle invoicing and follow up on outstanding payments in a timely manner * Assist with month\-end accounts administration and financial record\-keeping **What we are looking for** * A degree or qualification in Marketing, Digital Marketing, Communications, or a related field * At least 1 year of experience in a marketing, branding, or communications role * Basic knowledge or experience of bookkeeping or accounts administration is a plus * A genuine team player who collaborates well across functions * Strong critical thinking and problem\-solving skills * A proactive, can\-do attitude with a willingness to take ownership * Good written communication skills , particularly for content and blog writing

title: Business Development Associate
description: **Business Development Assistant Manager/Manager** **Location:** Dublin **Work Visa Sponsorship:** Not available **Business Area:** Core Business Services (CBS) **Contract Type:** 12\-month FTC ****About EY**** EY is a global leader in Assurance, Tax \& Law, and Strategy \& Transaction services services. We make a difference by helping our people, our clients and our wider communities achieve their potential. Our vision is to become the leading global professional services organisation by building a better working world. At EY our inclusive work environment means that everyone’s opinion is valued. This enables us to provide better advice and ideas to our clients, which in turn helps you develop as an individual to achieve your potential and make a difference. ****Role Overview**** * Your day\-to\-day activities in Business Development will see you work primarily in the Government \& Infrastructure sector, supporting the team on all aspects of account management and formal pursuits * You will gain experience in the tendering process within EY, with responsibility for maintain all the public sector tender portals, supporting pursuit teams as they complete their bid responses and you will be responsible for ensuring that all tenders are submitted ahead of strict public sector deadlines * You will analyse performance data, create management reports and identify and publish the best\-in\-class responses that will serve to continually improve our win ratios * You will have the opportunity to support on strategic account plans, perform key sector and competitor analysis and all other aspects of our Go To Market approach * You will also support the wider Market Segment team in compiling performance reports, collating data, preparing decks and packs for markets boards and leadership, including reports on our Client Experience (Cx) programme * On occasions, you will get to work with other members of the BD function on business\-critical projects as they arise * You’ll work as part of an innovative team and make a real contribution to EY’s growth strategy **To qualify for the role, you must have:** * You have at least 5 years of relevant work experience * You must be proficient in Microsoft 365 suite (especially Teams, Outlook, Sharepoint, Dynamics CRM, Word, Excel and PowerPoint) and have experience of managing client relationship databases/tools * You demonstrate excellent writing, communication, active listening and presentation skills * You have good stakeholder management skills and experience with working directly with senior stakeholders * Professional Services experience preferable but not essential * Experience with bid management preferable but not essential * Excellent written, oral, presentation and facilitation skills * Ability to coordinate multiple projects and initiatives simultaneously through effective prioritisation, organisation, flexibility and self\-discipline. Must have demonstrated project management experience * Can work with minimal supervision, to make decisions and progress activities * Proactive committed team player ****About EY**** As a global leader in Assurance, Tax, Transaction and Advisory Services, we’re using the finance products, expertise, and systems we’ve developed to build a better working world. That starts with a culture that believes in giving you the training, opportunities, and creative freedom to make things better. Whenever you join, however long you stay, the exceptional EY experience lasts a lifetime. ****What Working At EY Offers**** We offer a competitive remuneration package. Our comprehensive benefits package includes support for flexible working and career development, and with FlexEY you can select benefits that suit your needs: covering holidays, health and well\-being, insurance, savings and a wide range of discounts, offers and promotions. Plus, we offer: * Support and coaching from some of the most engaging colleagues around. * Opportunities to develop new skills and progress your career. * The freedom and flexibility to handle your role in a way that’s right for you. All our employees are given a benefits package which they can tailor to suit their individual preferences. Our range of benefits include: * Hybrid Working * Pension/Discounted Health Insurance * Web Doctor * Purchase of Additional Annual Leave * Free Gym Membership * Travel Pass * Maternity \& Paternity Leave * Bike to Work Scheme * Referral Bonuses \& Recognition Awards * Tech MBA paid by EY EY is committed to being an inclusive employer and we are happy to consider flexible working arrangements. We strive to achieve the right balance for our people, enabling us to deliver excellent client service whilst allowing you to build your career without sacrificing your personal priorities. While our client\-facing professionals can be required to travel regularly, and at times be based at client sites, our flexible working arrangements can help you to achieve a lifestyle balance. **Career Progression** * When you join EY, you will be supported to ensure that you are enhancing your skills from day one. * Continuous learning, where you can develop the mindset and skills to navigate whatever comes next. * As you grow and develop here, you’ll discover opportunities to help customise your career journey, so that it’s as unique as you are \- success is defined by you, we will provide the tools and flexibility, so you can make a meaningful impact, your way. * Transformative leadership, we will give you the insights, coaching and confidence to be the leader the world needs. * Diverse and inclusive culture, you will be embraced for who you are and empowered to use your voice to help others find theirs. * We have embraced Hybrid working at EY adding greater flexibility and autonomy to the roles of our employees. **Inclusion \& Diversity** We hold a collective commitment to foster an environment where all differences are valued and respected, practices are equitable and everyone experiences a sense of belonging: Inclusion, diversity, and equity are part of who we are at EY. We believe that the highest\-performing teams maximize the power of different perspectives and backgrounds. These teams are both diverse and inclusive and are willing to invite and learn from other perspectives. Our ability to include various viewpoints into our mindsets, behaviours and operations is fundamental to driving innovation, building strong relationships, and delivering the best solutions for our clients. We recognise the strength that comes from having a diverse workforce and building a culture where we support all our people to achieve their potential. You’ll be embraced for who you are and empowered to use your voice to help others find theirs. As an equal opportunities’ employer, we welcome applications from people of all backgrounds. Reasonable accommodations are offered at every stage of our recruitment process. **If you can confidently demonstrate that you meet the criteria above, please contact us as soon as possible.** **Join us in building a better working world.** **That** **’s** **Why, EY.** **Apply now.** **IMPORTANT: Where Agency assistance is required, our Talent Team will engage directly with suppliers. CVs / Profiles should not be shared directly with Hiring Managers. Unsolicited CVs / Profiles supplied to EY by Recruitment Agencies will not be accepted for this role.**

title: Business Development Graduate - Summer 2026
description: **Position: Business Development Graduate** **Locations: Dublin** **Start Date: Summer 2026** **Kickstart your career in sales with a global market leader.** At **KEYENCE** , we’re looking for ambitious graduates ready to take ownership of their careers in a fast\-paced, high\-reward environment. If you thrive on challenges and want to develop world\-class commercial skills, this role is for you. **About the Role** As a **Business Development Graduate** , you’ll be the **CEO of your own territory** , managing client relationships and driving sales. Your week will be split between: * **3 field\-based days** : Visiting clients, delivering product demonstrations, and closing deals. * **2 office\-based days** : Prospecting new business opportunities and building on existing customer relationships alongside your team. You’ll start with an intensive **training programme** combining classroom learning and on\-the\-job mentoring. As you grow, you’ll continue to benefit from structured development in **sales, leadership, and management** —with every manager at KEYENCE promoted from within. **What We Offer** * **Starting Salary:** €37,300 plus €2350 per annum in Location Allowance * **Quarterly Bonus:** Guaranteed minimum bonus year 1 \= €5,700 * **Company Car:** For business and personal use * **Private Healthcare:** Includes dental, optical, audiology, and subsidised gym membership * **Pension Scheme:** Advanced company pension with Zurich * **Contribution towards Private Healthcare** * **Employee Assistance Programme** * **Fully Expensed Travel \& Accommodation** **What We’re Looking For** * A degree in any discipline * Strong motivation and a winning mindset * Must have a full driving licence, valid in Ireland * Eligibility to work in the Ireland by Summer 2026 **About KEYENCE** Founded in 1974, **KEYENCE** is a global leader in automation and control technology, providing cutting\-edge solutions in Sensors, Vision, Measurement, Safety, and Microscopes. Operating in 90% of global industries and generating over **$8 billion** in annual sales, our **Direct Sales model** and focus on innovation have driven industry\-leading profitability and growth.

title: Buyer
description: **Buyer – Retail, Front of Shop \- Hybrid in Dublin 24 (3 days in office) \- €60,000 per annum** This is not a “sit behind a desk and analyse spreadsheets” buying role. We’re partnering with a **large, fast‑moving Irish retail group (300\+ stores)** that is reshaping how it buys, ranges and launches branded products across its estate. They’re now hiring a **commercial Buyer** to take **full ownership of a Front of Shop category,** from supplier negotiation right through to in‑store activation. * **You own your category** * **You negotiate directly with global brands** * **You control promotions, space, launches and margin** * **You are accountable for the commercial number** If you’re looking **to step up in your career as a Buyer** , or are **frustrated by too many layers and not enough autonomy** , this is a genuine career‑accelerating opportunity. **What will I be doing?** * Owning the **end‑to‑end commercial cycle** for assigned categories * Negotiating with established global suppliers * Driving **promotional cycles** , JBPs, margin and sell‑through * Securing **exclusive launches, GWP activity and first‑to‑market deals** * Working closely with wholesale and retail teams to leverage buying power * Managing planograms, space allocation and range performance * Representing your category at weekly trade and commercial meetings **What experience do I need to be eligible?** * **2\+ years’ experience** in buying or assistant buying * Experience working with **branded supplier‑led categories** (e.g. Beauty, FMCG, Confectionery, Health, Electricals, Power Tools, etc.) * Comfortable **owning a P\&L number** * Can work independently in a **lean, hands‑on environment** **This opportunity is a full\-time permanent role, based in Dublin 24, hybrid (3 days onsite), paying up €60,000 per annum pro rata. To hear more about this position or to discuss your suitability, apply today and we will be in touch with more information.** **By applying here you agree to RECRUITERS storing your CV in our secure applicant tracking system (ATS; Bullhorn). As your consultants, we are dedicated to helping you find a new job in your field of expertise and, equally, to keeping your personal information secure at all times. Your CV will not be sent to any third party without your consent.**

title: CRM & Clienteling Executive
description: My client based in Dublin 2, are seeking a commercially minded CRM \& Clienteling Executive with strong experience in customer engagement, segmentation, and personalised outreach. This role is heavily focused on **clienteling** and how CRM data can be used to drive meaningful one\-to\-one customer relationships, increase engagement, and deliver measurable revenue growth. You will play a key role in turning customer data into action — supporting store teams to embed clienteling into their daily activity, while delivering targeted CRM campaigns across email and other channels. Key Responsibilities Clienteling (Core Focus) * Lead and support clienteling activity across customer\-facing teams * Promote adoption of clienteling tools and best practices in\-store * Use customer data to build targeted outreach lists for personalised communication * Support teams in using insights to improve customer engagement and repeat purchase * Monitor clienteling activity and identify opportunities to improve performance and conversion * Create and refine personalised messaging templates for one\-to\-one customer outreach CRM \& Campaign Delivery * Plan and deliver CRM campaigns focused on engagement, retention, and revenue growth * Build and manage customer segments across the full lifecycle * Deliver email marketing campaigns and support customer communication strategy * Work closely with marketing teams to ensure consistent and relevant messaging * Support key trading periods, launches, and promotions with targeted CRM activity Data \& Reporting * Pull and analyse customer data to identify trends and opportunities * Report on CRM and clienteling performance, focusing on engagement and revenue impact * Use insights to continuously improve segmentation and outreach strategies * Track customer behaviour and campaign effectiveness to inform future activity Experience Required * 2–4 years’ experience in CRM, clienteling, or customer engagement roles * Strong hands\-on experience with **clienteling / client telling tools (essential)** * Experience in retail, luxury, or customer\-facing environments preferred * Strong understanding of CRM platforms (Salesforce or similar) * Experience with customer segmentation and targeted campaign delivery * Confident working with data to generate insights and actions * Understanding of email marketing and lifecycle campaigns * Strong communication skills with ability to influence store or frontline teams Key Attributes * Strong commercial mindset with focus on sales and customer retention * Passion for customer relationships and personalised engagement * Confident working with both data and people * Ability to drive adoption of clienteling in\-store * Detail\-oriented with strong organisational skills * Comfortable working in a fast\-paced, collaborative environment * Proactive and hands\-on approach to delivery

title: Chief Executive Officer
description: **The Opportunity – Chief Executive Officer** Reporting to the Board, this is an exciting opportunity for a Senior Leader to build on the great work already completed and lead DCM through its next phase of growth. While you will be accountable for our strategic growth and governance, you will also thrive on being close to the action, leading by example and getting involved when needed, to support the team with operational issues. Please see below an outline of the key objectives and responsibilities of the role: ***Leadership*** – Lead and inspire the management team in a supportive way to deliver our vision. ***Quality*** – Deliver quality services through oversight of all regulatory compliance. ***Development*** – Influence and drive the strategic plan for DCM working to achieve the goals set out for the organisation to continue to thrive as an Older Persons Charity. ***Finance*** – Oversee annual budgets and closely monitor financial performance. **Responsibilities** **People Management** * Support an open, supportive and friendly culture across the organisation delivering on the company’s core values, vision and mission. * Ensure regular touch points with the Senior Management Team and that each role is filled with the skillset required to operate effectively. * Oversee resource management to ensure that sufficient staffing levels are maintained. **Business Development** * Build and develop relationships with the HSE and other partners. * Build brand awareness among potential new employees, residents and families through regular engagement at events and via social media. * Maintain consistency across the brand and messaging. * Deliver on agreed growth targets and strategies. **Quality Assurance** * Ensure there is effective quality assurance across all services. * Oversee and assist with the completion of a suite of reports for each subcommittee of the board. * Oversee and regularly review service level policies and procedures. * Monitor IT systems and regularly review how to improve the automation of practices to make service provision more efficient. * Ensure complaints are dealt within the policy timeframe and monitor trends. * Ensure the organisation has a robust data protection policy and procedure to meet the requirements of GDPR. **Finance** * Responsibility for budget delivery and financial performance working closing with the Finance Director and management team. * Annual (or more frequent) contribution to budget preparation and forecasting. * With the support of HR, maintain an oversight of internal pay policies and market developments/ trends. * Continually look at opportunity to bring in new income and enhance current profit margin to ensure DCM is financially viable and sustainable. **Operations / Leadership** * Be hands on as required to act as a support for the managers and staff teams across all services. * Have overall responsibility for the delivery of services to our residents / clients. * Be a visible leader that engages well with staff and service users. **Other** * Some on\-call responsibilities will be required, commensurate with a role of this level. **About You** We’re looking for a proven leader who is passionate about leading a high quality, people centred organisation. Someone who recognises that as CEO, you will provide both strategic direction and visible daily leadership across all teams to ensure we maintain our excellent standards of care. The successful candidate will be someone who finds energy in being close to the heart of the work, values strong relationships and demonstrates hands\-on leadership in services, ensuring decisions are grounded in the realities of care delivery while embracing DCM’s values and ethos. **You’ll bring:** * At least five years’ senior management experience, ideally within healthcare, social care, housing, older persons services or a related service sector. * Strong financial experience particularly in preparing and managing budgets. * Strong commercial acumen, with demonstrated experience in developing organisations to meet market opportunities. * Excellent relationship building and communication skills, with the ability to represent DCM to a range of stakeholders internal and external. * A collaborative, people focused leadership style that motivates and inspires others. * A focus on quality and governance. * A relevant qualification in management, nursing, health and social care (or related field) is desirable. **What you can expect from us:** **Summary Terms \& Conditions** * Accountable to the Board of Trustees and line managed by the Chair of Trustees. * Based between sites and the head office in Mount Tabor, Sandymount Green, with provision also for home working. * Able to work a full time 39 hours per week with flexibly as required including some evening meetings. * Able to travel between sites as required – there are no expenses paid for this travel as they are in close proximity. * Salary in the range €105\-€120K depending on experience * Eligible for 28 days annual leave plus statutory holidays. * Eligible for membership of the defined contribution pension scheme including a matching employer’s contribution up to 8%. * Eligibility for performance related bonus linked to Company performance and KPI’s. **The Process** To express your interest in this role, please send your CV and short covering letter to our Head of People Radek Warchola at: radek.warchola@dublincentralmission.ie Following review of applicants the interview process will be communicated to those shortlisted. Closing date for application is Friday 15th May 2026\.

title: Civil Defense Tech Co-Founder / CMO (100 % remote) (m/f/d)
description: We are looking to hire ambitious entrepreneurs to start and scale their own startups. We are serial entrepreneurs, for example Paul Müller (founder Adjust, €1\.2B exit) and Petter Made (founder SumUp, €8B) who are eager to support outlier personalities and serial entrepreneurs to build €1B\+ companies. Our offer: * A salary while you build your startup as you will directly be employed by us. Alternatively, you can opt for up to **€500k** in funding. * **1:1 sparring with unicorn founders** on a weekly basis * **Community**: Access to the top 0\.1% of founders, peers and investors * **Team building**: Hiring top notch talent supported through our network (over 50,000 professionals) * **Distribution**: Support in reaching product\-market\-fit and building up a sales force / marketing machine * **Funding support** for securing a multi\-million euro funding round within 12 months (on average, EWOR Fellows raise \> €2M after our Grand Pitch) One of our fellows set a record for Europe’s largest pre\-seed round by a first\-time founder, securing a €12M pre\-seed investment. **Tasks** --------- * You will own, build, and run your startup in fields such as Civil Defense Tech * You will embark on an extensive personal development journey crafted by unicorn founders and follow a fully customised programme enhancing your goal, time, and energy management * You will receive support in hiring through our network to over 50,000 professionals and advice as well as best practices from serial entrepreneurs * You will receive intensive coaching to make your startup ready to raise millions in funding * You will iterate your product with us until having reached product\-market\-fit and receive support in building up a sales force or creating a marketing engine respectively **Requirements** ---------------- * You are based in Europe or the Americas or open to relocate * You are willing to take full responsibility for your own startup and scale it to €100M\+ in revenues * You have excellent communication skills in the English language Join us and build a €1B\+ company with us!

title: Cleaner
description: Where those who love pizza can be their true self. Benefits: * Free Food on shift * Plus tips \- You'll receive tips/Tronc\* * 50% off food and drink when you’re not working * Wellbeing Support for you and your family * Referral Bonus of €450 for referring pizza chefs and €1150 for referring managers * Pension \& Life Assurance * Pay Rate is €12\.80 per hour for under 20s and €13\.50 for age 20 and over * Learning and Development Opportunities to invest in you You’ll be the heart of our team, making sure that everything in our Pizzeria is spotless and restocked. You’ll create the perfect atmosphere in which our customers can have enlivening experiences and enjoy our famous pizza. Just be yourself and we will bring the best out of you. **No Experience Required.** What you’ll be doing: Run the pot wash, clean crockery and glassware and restock Clean floors and bathrooms Working in a dynamic and energetic environment Who we are: Where those who love pizza can be their true self. If you are invited to an interview, please let your interviewer know at the time of booking if you require any reasonable adjustments to be made for you. * Tronc, or card gratuities, is a fair way to pay gratuities to our teams. All Tronc goes to you, shared between our waiting and non\-waiting teams. You’ll get this at the end of each pay period, along with your normal pay and it’s one of the best perks about working in hospitality.

title: Client Funds Accountant (ARCS)
description: Location – London/Leeds/Dublin/IOM Hybrid \- 2 days per week (Dublin only) 12\-month contract An exciting opportunity has opened up to join the Client Funds \& Reconciliations team, which is responsible for efficient control and management of \~$400m of customer cash, the safeguarding of which is an integral part of Flutter’s strategy. Reporting into the EPM ARCS Lead, the role will be to support the Client Funds team during a critical migration project. At Flutter, we are embarking on an exceptionally ambitious global finance transformation programme over the next two years. As part of this, an exciting project within the Client Funds team has arisen to help align all Client Funds reconciliation processes into one unified model. This role bridges finance operations and systems transformation, ensuring accurate reconciliation processes are transitioned from legacy systems to a unified platform The role involves developing a strong understanding of the Client Funds key control processes, building relationships across many departments, including Group Financial Control, Treasury, Global Revenue, Product and Data teams to ensure alignment and buy\-in across all stakeholders. Flutter is a growing business, so there will be exposure to projects to integrate new products, payment methods and jurisdictions and the role will also play an integral role in working on key projects to integrate and align ways of working across the Flutter Group. **What You'll Do** * Create detailed specifications for the new unified reconciliation process, ensuring all requirements are captured and validated. * Produce system architecture diagrams, process flowcharts, and user guides for BAU handover. * Maintain accurate records of process changes and maintain a centralised repository of all system documentation. * Collaborate with project stakeholders to establish timelines, milestones, and deliverables for the migration initiative. * Provide accounting expertise to inform system requirements and identify risk areas in the migration approach. * Support UAT (User Acceptance Testing) activities, including test case development and results validation. * Coordinate with technical teams and finance stakeholders to resolve issues and keep the project on track. * Contribute to project status reporting and escalation management. * Process and validate large volumes of reconciliation data across payment channels and payment processors. * Perform data cleansing, mapping, and transformation activities to ensure data integrity throughout migration. * Reconcile legacy system data to the new unified platform, identifying and resolving discrepancies * Establish data quality controls and validation procedures to ensure accuracy at scale. * Review completed month\-end reconciliations for accuracy, completeness, and compliance with accounting standards. * Verify all transactions are properly classified, recorded, and reconciled before handover to BAU * Identify and investigate reconciliation variances, implementing corrective actions where necessary. * Prepare handover documentation and training materials for the BAU team. * Serve as subject matter expert during transition to ensure continuity of controls. * Managing relationships across geographical locations, with many key stakeholders being situated in Dublin, London, the Isle of Man and Leeds. **How You'll Do It** * Qualified accountant with 3\+ years of post\-qualified experience in industry. * Experience in Finance Projects or change/transformation initiatives is essential. * Strong understanding of SOx and GAAP principles and the associated financial reporting requirements. * Advanced proficiency in Microsoft Excel and accounting ERP software (e.g. Oracle). * Ability to manage complex, multi\-step reconciliation processes, with revenue to cash experience. * Knowledge of payment processor systems and transaction flows * Enthusiasm and a willingness to learn, combined with a proactive approach to work and a desire to progress. * A working knowledge of EPM ARCS would be advantageous. * Ability to analyse and draw conclusions from large volumes of data and communicate and present results precisely. * Professional, combined with good time management, organisational and multi\-tasking skills. * High level of attention to detail and trustworthiness due to sensitive nature of customer information. * Proactivity and innovation – capacity to take the initiative to identify improvements in process and assist management in implementation. * Articulate, with the ability to communicate both within the team and to members of the wider business in a concise manner. * Flexible, enjoying a frequently changing and fast paced environment. **What’s In It For You** We are a flexible employer; whether you have personal commitments or a hobby that brings you joy, we want you to bring your best self to work and feel empowered to do so. We also like to share our success; after all you make it happen. We have an excellent benefits package that can be personalised to you: * Bonus scheme * Uncapped holiday allowance * Enhanced pension scheme * Private healthcare * Life assurance * Income protection * Hybrid working * £1,000 annual self\-development learning fund * Invest via the Flutters Sharesave Scheme * Paid volunteering days * Enhanced parental leave * Wellbeing fund (£/€250 a year) * Recognition programs * Electric car scheme, gym membership, discounts, vouchers and much more! **About Flutter** We are a world leader in online sports betting and iGaming, with a market leading position in the US and across the world. We have an unparalleled portfolio of the most innovative, diverse and distinctive brands including FanDuel, Sky Betting \& Gaming, Sportsbet, PokerStars, Paddy Power, Sisal, tombola, Betfair, MaxBet, Junglee Games and Adjarabet. With our global scale and challenger attitude, through which we excite and entertain our customers, in a safe and sustainable way. Using our collective power, the Flutter Edge, we aim to disrupt the sector, learning from the past to create a better future for our customers, colleagues and communities. We’re working to be an inclusive employer, and we encourage people from all backgrounds, ways of thinking and working to apply. Everyone brings different perspectives and experiences; you don't have to meet all the requirements listed to apply for this role. If you need any adjustments to make this role work for you let us know, and we’ll see how we can accommodate them.

title: Clinical Specialist Physiotherapist – Spinal & Orthopaedics
description: The Mater Private Network presents an exciting opportunity to lead our fast growing innovative spinal \& orthopaedic service. This is a full time, permanent position. **Details of Service:** * Leading the orthopaedic inpatient \& outpatient service, achieving associated KPI’s with this service and contributing to its growth and development. * Maintaining a daily orthopaedic inpatient and outpatient caseload. * Responsible for maintaining an inpatient orthopaedic caseload including partaking in pre\-assessment clinic for joint and spinal patients. * Work with Orthopaedic Consultants/Registrars in outpatient clinics as part of the multidisciplinary team. This involves assessment, treatment, and triage of musculoskeletal/ orthopaedic patients in the conservative, pre\-operative and post\-surgical setting. * Triaging, assessing, treating, and onward referring as appropriate, patients who attend fracture clinic. **Essential Criteria:** * Hold a degree in physiotherapy or an equivalent qualification recognized by the physiotherapists Registration Board at CORU * Be registered on the physiotherapists register maintained by the physiotherapist’s registration board at CORU and be a member of or eligible for membership of The Irish Society of Chartered Physiotherapists. * Have a proven record of clinical experience in orthopaedic physiotherapy and must hold a minimum of 5 years full time experience in the required area of physiotherapy. * Candidate must demonstrate relevant musculoskeletal and inpatient orthopaedic physiotherapy continuing professional development and it is desirable to be in the process of completing or have completed a relevant post graduate qualification at master’s level or higher from an accredited institution. * Candidates must demonstrate achievement in the areas of clinical audit, quality improvement initiatives, practice development, teaching, and research. * Candidates must have the requisite knowledge and ability (including a high standard of suitability and professional ability) for the proper discharge of the duties of the role. * Work with the physiotherapy manager in ensuring the coordination, development and delivery of a quality, client centered physiotherapy service. **Job Description Available On Request.** **MATER PRIVATE NETWORK IS AN EQUAL OPPORTUNITIES EMPLOYER**

title: Cloud AI API Integrations – Subject Matter Expert - Ireland
description: **Cloud AI API Integrations – Fully Remote Subject Matter Expert** **About Prolific** Prolific is not just another player in the AI space – we are building the biggest pool of quality human data in the world. Over 35,000 AI developers, researchers, and organizations use Prolific to gather data from paid study participants with a wide variety of experiences, knowledge, and skills. *The Opportunity* We are inviting software developers, system administrators, and other technical professionals who work with AI models via cloud platforms to participate in an upcoming research project. We want to learn about your real\-world experience integrating AI APIs into applications and workflows. This project is open to a broad range of practitioners, including: * Developers and system administrators integrating Claude (Anthropic), OpenAI (GPT models) and Google Gemini via Amazon Bedrock, Google Cloud Vertex AI, or Microsoft Azure AI * Developers and system administrators working with other leading AI APIs via cloud platforms or direct API access **Please note:** This is a "bench" model. By verifying your credentials, you gain eligibility for high\-level research tasks as they become available. Access is based on project demand and professional profile matching. *What You'll Bring* * Active experience as a software developer or system administrator working with AI APIs in a professional context * Hands\-on integration of AI models via cloud platforms (e.g. Amazon Bedrock, Google Cloud Vertex AI, Microsoft Azure AI) or direct API access * Practical knowledge of embedding AI capabilities (such as Claude, GPT, or Gemini models) into applications or automated workflows * Comfort discussing technical implementation details, challenges, and decisions in your day\-to\-day work *What You'll Be Doing* * Complete a paid screener to help us assess your background and suitability for specific research tasks * Participate in AI\-moderated interviews via Listen Labs (primary research method) * Complete qualitative surveys via Qualtrics * In some cases, take part in live interviews with the research team *Nice To Have* * Familiarity with multiple AI providers or models in the same workflow or organisation * Experience with RAG pipelines, fine\-tuning, prompt engineering, or AI agent frameworks * Involvement in evaluating or selecting AI platforms for enterprise use *Why Prolific is a great platform to join as a Participant* Joining our platform as a Prolific participant will give you the chance to influence the AI models of the future using your professional expertise. Once you pass our assessment, you can join Prolific in just 15 minutes, and start enjoying competitive pay rates, flexible hours, and the ability to work from home. We've built a unique platform that connects researchers and companies with a global pool of participants, enabling the collection of high\-quality, ethically sourced human behavioural data and feedback. This data is the cornerstone of developing more accurate, nuanced, and aligned AI systems. We believe that the next leap in AI capabilities won't come solely from scaling existing models, but from integrating diverse human perspectives and behaviours into AI development. By providing this crucial human data infrastructure, Prolific is positioning itself at the forefront of the next wave of AI innovation – one that reflects the breadth and the best of humanity. *Links to more information on Prolific* Website Youtube *Privacy Statement* By submitting your application, you agree that Prolific may collect your personal data for recruiting and global organisation planning. Prolific's Candidate Privacy Notice explains what personal information Prolific may process, where Prolific may process your personal information, its purposes for processing your personal information, and the rights you can exercise over Prolific use of your personal personal information.

title: Cloud Solutions Architect (Daily Rate)
description: **Cloud Solutions Architect** **Daily Rate Contract (1 day onsite)** My Dublin based client is seeking an experienced Cloud Solutions Architect for a daily rate contract. The Cloud Architect will support a large\-scale digital and cloud transformation programme. This is a daily rate contract role focused on designing, delivering, and optimising secure, scalable cloud infrastructure solutions across AWS and/or Azure environments. You will operate within a Cloud Centre of Excellence style function, contributing hands\-on architecture expertise, establishing best practice patterns, and accelerating cloud adoption across enterprise platforms. **Key Responsibilities** * Design and deliver secure, scalable, and cost\-efficient cloud architecture solutions aligned to enterprise and regulatory requirements * Refine and translate business requirements into robust technical designs * Provide strategic guidance on cloud adoption, platform optimisation, and architectural best practice * Drive innovation and cost optimisation across public cloud environments * Present architectural solutions to both technical and non\-technical stakeholders * Ensure security\-by\-design principles are embedded across all solutions * Develop reusable, repeatable cloud patterns to accelerate delivery and reduce platform overhead * Collaborate with engineering teams to implement, deploy, and enhance cloud capabilities * Ensure solutions meet performance, reliability, and governance standards **Required Experience** * 5\+ years’ experience designing and implementing scalable cloud solutions * Strong background in platform (AWS or Azure) * Experience designing or implementing Kubernetes solutions (preferably EKS or equivalent) * Proven ability to manage and optimise public cloud infrastructure environments *
