title: Education Consultant
description: University College Dublin’s Professional Academy is seeking a **Sales Executive/Education Consultant** to join our growing team. The UCD Professional Academy focuses on delivering shorter, career\-focused training to individuals across a wide variety of Professional Diploma and related courses. Housed within a commercial company owned by the University, it is a fast\-paced, forward\-thinking, and dynamic environment. You will be an experienced sales professional who sees the significant potential of developing this offering within Ireland’s largest University. The Professional Academy is an ambitious project which seeks to make a significant contribution to promoting skills\-based training to the workforce, while upholding UCD values by embedding quality, integrity, and accountability into every aspect of its activity. The primary purpose of the position is to establish and develop direct relationships from inbound leads to drive enrolment targets. **Responsibilities** * To consult with individuals about the best course to meet their needs and to close them as a customer and student of the UCD Professional Academy. * To maintain a clean sales pipeline through effective use of the CRM. * To manage direct relationships by providing excellent customer service. * To perform administrative tasks related to sales. * To meet and exceed sales targets and activity's. **Skills \& Requirements** * A minimum of 3 years’ sales experience in an inside sales role dealing with consultative sales. * Highly organized and efficient. * Experience working within a highly CRM centric sales organization. Hubspot experience is a plus but not required. * Excellent presentation, communication and interpersonal skills. * Demonstrated ability to meet deadlines. * High\-energy problem solver who thrives in a demanding sales environment. * Empathetic and trustworthy, treats people with respect * Excellent command of the English language (both spoken and written) * Education market experience a plus, but individuals from any consultative selling background are invited to apply **What we can offer:** * Employee Pension * Competitive Salary * Employee Assistance Program * Monthly staff recognition program * Attractive performance based bonus * Professional Development and training * Paid Maternity/Paternity/Adoptive Leave * Free access to our collection of online/in person professional diplomas * Flexible working arrangements with a commitment to facilitating remote working * 22 days of annual leave and the company closes over Good Friday and the Christmas period in addition to this time off. Does this sound like you? If you would like to learn more on this exciting opportunity please submit your CV or contact me for more information! UCD Professional Academy is an equal opportunities employer. We celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees.

title: Event Host / MC
description: Full Job Description Dublin Events is looking for a confident and experienced **Event Host / MC** to host live social events, storytelling nights, dating\-style events and other interactive event concepts. This role is ideal for someone with experience hosting events, performing, comedy, theatre, presenting or public speaking. You need to be comfortable holding a room, making people feel welcome, keeping energy up and thinking on the spot when things go quiet or don’t go exactly to plan. This is a great opportunity for a comic, performer or presenter who wants to get more live hosting experience and be part of events happening in Dublin and across Ireland. Responsibilities * Hosting live events and keeping the night moving * Welcoming guests and creating a fun, relaxed atmosphere * Making announcements and introducing different parts of the event * Thinking on the spot and filling quiet moments when needed * Supporting basic setup before the event * Helping with ticketing or check\-in where required * Using basic tech such as laptops, speakers, microphones, Spotify and presentations * Travelling for selected shows outside Dublin About You We’re looking for someone who is: * Confident speaking in front of a room * Warm, funny, quick\-thinking and friendly * Experienced hosting, performing or presenting * Comfortable making small talk with guests * Able to manage a room and keep energy up * Reliable and calm under pressure * Happy helping with basic setup and event flow * Willing to travel for some events Requirements * Event hosting, comedy, performance, theatre, presenting or public speaking experience is essential * Must be comfortable using basic event tech * Must be available for selected May and June dates * Must be comfortable travelling for some shows * Dublin\-based preferred Confirmed Dates Applicants should be available for as many of the following as possible: * Friday 22nd May * Saturday 23rd May * Thursday 11th June * Friday 12th June * Saturday 13th June * Sunday 14th June, TBC * Thursday 18th June, Cork * Friday 19th June, Cork * Friday 26th June Pay Rates: * **€95 per Dublin show (aprox 4\.5 hours)** * **€130 per show outside Dublin (travel \& accommodation covered)** Pay: €21\.00\-€26\.00 per hour Benefits: * Company events * Employee discount * Free or subsidised travel * Store discount Language: * English (required) Willingness to travel: * 75% (required) Work Location: On the road

title: Executive Assistant
description: ***🚀 We’re on a mission to make money work for everyone.*** We’re waving goodbye to the complicated and confusing ways of traditional banking. After starting as a prepaid card, our product offering has grown a lot in the last 10 years in the UK. As well as personal and business bank accounts, we offer joint accounts, accounts for 16\-17 year olds, a free kids account and credit cards in the UK, with more exciting things to come beyond. Our UK customers can also save, invest and combine their pensions with us. With our hot coral cards and get\-paid\-early feature, combined with financial education on social media and our award winning customer service, we have a long history of creating magical moments for our customers! We’re not about selling products \- we want to solve problems and change lives through Monzo ❤️ 📍Dublin \| 💰€57,000 \- €72,000 \+ Benefits * This is a 12 months fixed term contract\* **⭐ Our EA team** We’re looking for an experienced Executive Assistant to join our EA team to support our CEO, COO and CFO for Monzo EU 🇪🇺 We know that an excellent EA leads to better outcomes for our customers by enabling the performance of the Executive they support. This is why we are looking for a passionate, ambitious EA who relishes working in a fast paced environment where they have an opportunity to make a real difference. You’ll be joining a close knit community of EAs at Monzo, 16 based in London and 2 in the US, who play a critical role in keeping Monzo running smoothly and continuously raising the bar for exceptional support. **🔑 You’ll play a key role by...** * Managing our leaders’ time to ensure their diaries are accurate, free from clashes, prioritised and creating space for them to get work done. Anticipating changes and proactively supporting them to keep their plates spinning. * Supporting our leaders with their important weekly meetings whether that is with agenda, deck preparation, taking minutes or following up on actions. * Keeping your finger on the pulse of their world by proactively managing their inbox and keeping it clear of clutter and escalating important information to ensure it is addressed in a timely manner. * Relationship building across your leaders organisation and the wider company to keep your finger on the pulse of engagement. * Owning the logistics of your leaders work life and their events. This could look like arranging day to day transport and accommodation, planning away days and socials for their team or ensuring they have a meeting room for every meeting. * Working as part of an Executive Office, collaborating with the Chief of Staff and People Partner to deliver for your exec. While your focus will be on the leader that you’re supporting there will also be opportunities to get involved in project work and other initiatives across the organisation that interest you. **🤩 We’d love to hear from you if…** * You have experience working as an EA previously within a fast paced environment. (While not a requirement, previous experience in a start\-up or scale up is preferable) * You’re resilient and tenacious in your approach to getting things done and you’re not afraid to ask questions to gather the context you need to work autonomously * You are excited by the opportunity to be involved in a broad surface area and able to context switch with ease. * You have excellent communication skills with a proven ability to write and adapt style to different audiences * You enjoy work where attention to detail is a key strength * You enjoy collaborating as a team to get things done * You have prior experience using Google suite \& Slack or similar tools. Unfortunately we cannot provide sponsorship for this position. **The Interview Process** Our interview process involves 4 main stages: * Recruiter Call (30 min): One of our Hiring team will reach out to you for an introductory conversation. * Take home task * Final interviews (2 x 60 min interviews): Role Specific \& Behavioural * Final call with Monzo EU CEO **🙌 What’s In It For You** 💰€57,000 \- €72,000 📍This is a hybrid role is based in our Dublin office. Normally the Dublin team work 2\-3 days in the office per week, but this can be flexible. ⏰ We offer flexible working hours and trust you to work enough hours to do your job well, at times that suit you and your team 📚Learning budget of £1,000 a year for books, training courses and conferences ➕And much more, see our full list of benefits **here** We’ll only close this role once we have enough applications for the next stage. Please submit your application as soon as possible to make sure you don’t miss out. **Equal opportunities for everyone** Diversity and inclusion are a priority for us and we’re making sure we have lots of support for all of our people to grow at Monzo. At Monzo, we’re embracing diversity by fostering an inclusive environment for all people to do the best work of their lives with us. This is integral to our mission of making money work for everyone. You can read more in our blog, 2024 Diversity and Inclusion Report and 2024 Gender Pay Gap Report. We’re an equal opportunity employer. All applicants will be considered for employment without attention to age, ethnicity, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, family or parental status, national origin, or veteran, neurodiversity or disability status. If you have a preferred name, please use it to apply. We don't need full or birth names at application stage 😊

title: Experienced Application Tester
description: Emydex Technology is currently looking for a standout Experienced Tester who embodies all our values **\#Empowerment \#YesMinded \#Dependability \#Excellence \# Integrity \#Teamwork.** We are offering a role with a diverse reach across the business. This will entail reporting to all areas of the company from the project initiation to the customer sign off. Day to day you will be involved with scoping testing effort, test design, test execution with ongoing support for innovative ways to improve testing within Emydex. **The Candidate:** You will have a clear and approachable communication style being comfortable with testing in waterfall and agile software delivery methodologies. The ideal candidate will have all of the following skills and experience: * At least 2 years’ experience * ISTQB or equivalent experience * Understanding of C\# and .Net * SQL * Test design and execution skills * Experienced with Test management, Test execution tools. E.g. Azure DevOps * Experience with system and UAT testing **Desirable skills include** * Non\-functional testing * Test Automation * UI / UX testing * Testing in a client / server architecture * Git * Cloud experience, Azure * Scripting **Responsibilities:** * Prepare \& Execute Test Plans * Execution and reporting of manual test plans * Collaborate with the Development team \& Project Managers to understand and prioritise system testing efforts * Report bugs and errors to development teams * Reporting to Project Managers on test status * Working closely with developers to identify and mitigate risk * Identification and enforcement of quality gates * Continuous improvement of Emydex’s testing approach **Emydex Working Environment:** * Emydex value our employees and strive to create an engaging and enjoyable working environment that promotes personal growth while working with a leading\-edge tool\-set. * Encouragement, guidance and support for those who want to self\-improve and up\-skill. * Extra days’ holiday per year of service * Finish Early Fridays * Refer a friend scheme * Pension scheme eligible after probation **Conditions:** * Emydex Technology’s Headquarters is based in Blanchardstown in West Dublin, but employees have the option to work the hybrid WFH model * Salary – negotiable depending on experience * Bonus – based on achievement of objectives **About Emydex** We are an Irish Software Development company providing Software Solutions to over 100 leading Food \& Meat Processing companies in Ireland, the UK, France, Namibia, Botswana, South Africa, North America, Australia and New Zealand. To look a little further into our company profile and to see what we are all about, check out our: * The latest news and what we have been up to: https://www.emydex.com/news/ * Facebook page here with some staff event photographs: https://www.facebook.com/Emydextechnology/ * LinkedIn Profile which helps show you what Emydex is all about: https://www.linkedin.com/company/emydex\-technology Job Types: Full\-time, Permanent Benefits: * Company pension * On\-site parking * Sick pay * Work from home Application question(s): * Do you have at least 2 years of hands‑on experience testing software applications, including system and UAT testing, using tools such as Azure DevOps (or similar)? Work Location: Hybrid remote in Blanchardstown, County Dublin

title: Facilities Executive
description: Spencer Recruitment is partnering with our client, a leading sporting organisation, to appoint a Facilities Executive. This is a key operational role within a fast\-paced organisation, responsible for ensuring office facilities run smoothly and efficiently. The successful candidate will play a central part in ensuring that staff, athletes, and visitors experience a safe, well\-maintained, and welcoming environment every day. Reporting to the Chief Financial Officer, the postholder will oversee facilities operations across multiple sites, with responsibility spanning health and safety compliance, fleet management, and office and IT coordination. **About the Job** * Operational role based on\-site across multiple Dublin locations * Fast\-paced sporting organisation environment * Opportunity to take on a broad facilities remit including health \& safety, fleet, and office management **The Role** * Act as the main point of contact for staff and athletes across office facilities * Coordinate planned and reactive maintenance with external contractors * Monitor building systems and conduct regular site inspections * Act as designated Health and Safety Officer and Fire Safety Manager * Manage the domestic car fleet and related insurance policies * Oversee office space, IT coordination, mobile phones and laptop management * Contribute to sustainability initiatives and continuous improvement * Manage facilities and event budgets in a cost\-effective manner **Key Responsibilities** *Facilities Operations* * Support day\-to\-day management across multiple sites * Maintain close relationships with stakeholders at each facility * Track contractor performance and ensure work is completed to required standards * Monitor building systems (air conditioning, alarms/security, lighting, access control) * Conduct regular premises inspections to identify hazards and improvement opportunities *Health, Safety and Compliance* * Maintain compliance with health and safety regulations, including record\-keeping and reporting * Participate in fire drills, safety audits, and incident investigations * Support implementation of safety procedures, emergency plans, and risk assessments * Recruit and manage Fire Safety Officers and First Aiders * Act as first responder to alarm call\-outs outside of normal office hours *Fleet Management* * Manage the domestic car fleet and insurance policies * Act as point of contact for lease and insurance providers * Issue guidance to drivers on processes for accidental damage *Office Space and IT Management* * Assist with office layout planning, desk moves, and space utilisation * Manage stock levels of office supplies and consumables * Coordinate meeting room setup, event logistics and catering * Manage relationship with external IT helpdesk * Oversee mobile phone and laptop management for new and existing staff **About You** * Minimum 3 years' experience in a similar facilities role * Knowledge of current Health and Safety regulations * Strong organisational and multitasking abilities * Excellent communication and problem\-solving skills * Ability to build effective working relationships with key stakeholders * Energetic, reliable, and self\-motivated with a positive, can\-do attitude * Proficiency with standard office software (Outlook, Excel, Teams) * Flexibility to work outside normal office hours when required * Third level qualification in Facilities Management or Health and Safety (desirable) * Interest in or experience of working in a dynamic sports environment (desirable) **What's on Offer** * Permanent, full\-time position * On\-site role in Dublin * Opportunity to work within a high\-profile sporting organisation * Varied and dynamic role with broad operational responsibility

title: Finance & Strategy - Financial Analyst
description: *To get the best candidate experience, please consider applying for a maximum of 3 roles within 12 months to ensure you are not duplicating efforts.* Job Category Finance Job Details **About Salesforce** Salesforce is the \#1 AI CRM, where humans with agents drive customer success together. Here, ambition meets action. Tech meets trust. And innovation isn’t a buzzword — it’s a way of life. The world of work as we know it is changing and we're looking for Trailblazers who are passionate about bettering business and the world through AI, driving innovation, and keeping Salesforce's core values at the heart of it all. Ready to level\-up your career at the company leading workforce transformation in the agentic era? You’re in the right place! Agentforce is the future of AI, and you are the future of Salesforce. The Finance \& Strategy (F\&S) team serves as a trusted advisor to the business, guiding decision\-making through strong business partnership and insightful, innovative analysis. We are seeking forward\-thinking, results\-oriented individuals who thrive in a fast\-paced environment and can improve processes to help the company scale with growth. **F\&S Analyst** We are looking for forward\-thinking, goal\-oriented individuals that will succeed in a fast\-paced environment and thrive in building and improving processes that will allow the company to scale with growth. This role primary responsibilities will be in expense and headcount management, month\-end/quarter\-end management reporting with stakeholders globally, partnering across F\&S, Finance Data Office, and Controllership. **Responsibilities** * Build, maintain and update financial models to support key business decisions * Assist in preparation of monthly, quarterly and annual management reporting * Conduct variance analysis for month\-end and quarter\-end financial close. * Analyse historical and current financial data for trends, run\-rates, opportunities and risks * Collaborate with other stakeholders to gather data for insights and accuracy * Support monthly, quarterly and annual forecasting process by working across stakeholders. * Identify and recommend opportunities to improve existing financial processes, reporting and data collection methods. * Leverage across software, platforms and tools to simplify and automate current reporting and tasks. * Document financial procedures and best practices to ensure consistency and knowledge transfer. **Technical Skills** * University Degree in Finance, or relevant disciplines. * At least 1\-2 years of post\-qualification experience in an FP\&A, Finance \& Strategy, Accounting function of a multinational entity. * Strong skills in MS Excel, Hyperion, Smartview and Google Suite. Experience with SQL and snowflake a plus. * Data extraction, analysis and presentation; Tableau or other data visualization a plus. * Excellent communication and interpersonal skills. * Track record of successful and quantifiable process improvement projects. **Non\-Technical Skills** * Thrives in an environment of collaboration/teamwork but is also a self\-starter, requiring limited guidance * Get it Done attitude * Ability to deal with a fast moving dynamic environment with high degree of ambiguity * Ability to establish and manage stakeholder relationships * Clear, concise and professional communications – written \& verbal; interpersonal skills * Emotional Intelligence, empathetic mindset and humility to create space for learning * Sharp attention to details * Strong desire to process improve and drive efficiencies Unleash Your Potential When you join Salesforce, you’ll be limitless in all areas of your life. Our benefits and resources support you to find balance and *be your best* , and our AI agents accelerate your impact so you can *do your best* . Together, we’ll bring the power of Agentforce to organizations of all sizes and deliver amazing experiences that customers love. Apply today to not only shape the future — but to redefine what’s possible — for yourself, for AI, and the world. Accommodations If you need a reasonable accommodation during the application or the recruiting process, please submit a request via this Accommodations Request Form. Please note that Salesforce uses artificial intelligence (AI) tools to help our recruiters assess and evaluate candidates’ resumes and qualifications throughout the recruiting process. Humans will always make any candidate selection and hiring decisions. Please see our Candidate Privacy Statement for more information about how we use your personal data and your rights, including with regard to use of AI tools and opt out options. Posting Statement Salesforce is an equal opportunity employer and maintains a policy of non\-discrimination with all employees and applicants for employment. What does that mean exactly? It means that at Salesforce, we believe in equality for all. And we believe we can lead the path to equality in part by creating a workplace that’s inclusive, and free from discrimination. Know your rights: workplace discrimination is illegal. Any employee or potential employee will be assessed on the basis of merit, competence and qualifications – without regard to race, religion, color, national origin, sex, sexual orientation, gender expression or identity, transgender status, age, disability, veteran or marital status, political viewpoint, or other classifications protected by law. This policy applies to current and prospective employees, no matter where they are in their Salesforce employment journey. It also applies to recruiting, hiring, job assignment, compensation, promotion, benefits, training, assessment of job performance, discipline, termination, and everything in between. Recruiting, hiring, and promotion decisions at Salesforce are fair and based on merit. The same goes for compensation, benefits, promotions, transfers, reduction in workforce, recall, training, and education.

title: Finance Associate
description: Our client is a global alternative asset management firm specialising in investments across the renewable energy sector, with its headquarters based in Dublin. The ideal candidate will have three or more years’ broad accounting experience, with a strong track record in invoice management, payment processing, intercompany reconciliations, and supporting quarterly reporting within a structured finance environment. **Responsibilities:** * Process purchase orders (POs) and ensure proper documentation. * Process accounts payables and receivable transactions. * Manage invoices and payments for all group entities. * Oversee sales order management and client invoicing across multiple subsidiaries. **Requirements:** * Bachelor’s degree in finance, Accounting or related field. * 3\+ years of experience in finance administration, ideally in a multi\-entity or international environment. * Desirable experience with Oracle NetSuite, Xero or similar ERP/accounting software. * Proficiency in Microsoft office, and particularly strong Excel skills. This role is ideal for someone who has strong accounts assistant/finance administration experience and looking for a role that offers great hybrid working with 3 days in the office and flexible hours. Our client offers a strong remuneration package including Bonus, Pension and Healthcare. For more information, contact Matthew Fleming at mfleming@elevatepartners.ie or apply directly via the link provided.

title: Financial Accountant
description: **At Ayvens, progress starts with you.** Our ambitions to shape the future of sustainable mobility are powered by our talent. Join us, and get better with every move. **Financial Accountant: 12\-Mth Contract** **Location: Leopardstown, Dublin 18** **Company Background** Ayvens Insurance is part of the Ayvens group, a leading provider in mobility services with 3\.3 million vehicles under management worldwide. Euro Insurances DAC (trading as Ayvens Insurance) offers fleet insurance solutions to Ayvens operating entities with assets under management of above €1bn. The Ayvens brand was launched in 2023, following the merger of ALD and LeasePlan leasing groups to create a market leader in mobility. Ayvens Insurance incorporates legacy insurance books from the ALD and LeasePlan groups. Ayvens Insurance is part of the Societe Generale Group. **Job Purpose** The Financial Accountant will be involved in the preparation, analysis and review of financial reporting processes for monthly group reporting, regulatory reporting and statutory reporting. The Financial Accountant will report to the Financial Reporting Manager. This is a high\-impact role with significant exposure across the business, offering the opportunity to interact with multiple departments and senior stakeholders. **Responsibilities Will Include But Are Not Limited To** * Support the technical accounting and reconciliation of inputs from various operating systems into the general ledger and sub\-ledgers of Finance Reporting system (SAP). * Review and analyse data flows between operating systems and integration of data. * Prepare management accounts and reports with analysis of variances and trends. * Prepare and deliver presentations to senior management in the Company. * Prepare statutory financial statements. * Co\-ordination of statutory and internal audit processes, engagement with external auditors. * Review and monitoring of the Company’s investment portfolio ensuring investment month end closing processes are completed and accurate. * Support the planning and budgeting cycles as required. * Proactive in identifying and implementing financial system and process improvements. * Take part in ad\-hoc financial projects and process improvement activities as required. **Knowledge** * Excellent technical accounting background with IFRS17 experience an advantage. * Advanced Excel * Ability to work with sizable data sets. * Solvency II reporting experience an advantage **Skills** * Excellent analytical and problem\-solving skills. * Excellent interpersonal and communication skills with an ability to work across departmental and organisational lines. * Ability to operate withing strict reporting deadlines. * Strong time management and organisational skills with an ability to prioritise workload and multitask. **Education And Experience** * Qualified accountant preferably ACA or ACCA * At least 2 years PQE experience preferably in financial services **Ayvens Insurance is an equal opportunities employer.** **Why Ayvens?** With over 3\.4 million vehicles managed across more than 42 countries, we provide full\-service leasing, flexible subscription services, fleet management services and multi\-mobility solutions to customers of all sizes, including large corporates, SMEs, professionals, and private individuals. By leveraging our unique position to lead the way to net zero and further shape the digital transformation of the industry, we are well\-positioned to meet the evolving mobility needs of our clients and provide them with the solutions they need to thrive. At Ayvens, we believe that our success is driven by our commitment to customer satisfaction. Our team is dedicated to delivering innovative solutions and technology\-enabled services that help our customers focus on their everyday business. We’re committed to sustainable mobility and have made it a core part of our strategy. In everything we do, we’re guided by the principles of authenticity, curiosity, commitment and collaboration. We aim to foster an organisation that's diverse in people and ideas, where everyone can thrive and be themselves, no matter who they are. Join us on this exciting journey as we continue to enable the transformation towards large scale adoption of sustainable mobility and provide our customers with the solutions they need to succeed. Follow our page for the latest updates, news, and insights. * ALD Automotive \| LeasePlan are rebranding to Ayvens across all 42 countries by 2025\.

title: Financial Accountant - 12 month Fixed Term Contract
description: **OVERVIEW** The Maples Group is a standard bearer in financial and legal services, trusted by many of the world’s largest hedge fund managers, private equity firms and international corporations. Our distinction flows from our carefully curated team: 2,500\+ professionals characterised by tenacity, ethics and exacting excellence. Operating in key financial centres across the Americas, Asia, Europe and the Middle East, our international presence offers a unique springboard for career development and cross\-cultural immersion. Our side\-by\-side financial and legal services are similarly ripe for interdisciplinary learning and growth. The Maples Group looks to add a Dublin\-based Financial Accountant to our team on a 12\-month fixed term contract and invites eager and qualified candidates to apply. We are committed to diversity, inclusion and equality of opportunity as we attract, retain and develop world\-class talent. **Who We Seek** Our merit\-based culture suits professionals in pursuit of boundless careers and lives.Beyond their acumen, team members are collaborative and conscientious, bringing a healthy sense of drive and purpose to each interaction and to all aspects of their work. **About the Role** The Financial Accountant reports to the Finance Manager and supports the wider Accounts team. The team is responsible for supporting the finance function and performing all key financial processes including accounts payable, accounts receivable, working capital management, internal performance reporting and all external statutory reporting. title: Frontend Engineer
description: **Who are we?** At Newcode.ai, we're on a mission to reshape how organizations put AI to work in their day\-to\-day operations. In few months, we've moved from vision to reality—building products our clients truly love. As part of our fast\-growing and highly ambitious team, you won't just drive the future of AI—you'll help define it. **Who are we looking for?** As a frontend engineer at Newcode.ai, you'll be at the forefront of crafting the user experiences at the heart of our AI solutions. You'll work hands\-on with a modern stack — Next.js, React, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, shadcn/ui, React Query, Zustand — bringing complex AI\-powered products to life through intuitive, high\-quality interfaces. This isn't a role where you follow a spec and push pixels. You'll have real ownership over the frontend — from architecture decisions to the final interaction details. If you're someone who gets excited about trying new tools, cares deeply about craft, and wants to shape a product from the ground up, this is your place. You'll collaborate closely with product and backend teams, and have real influence over both the design and architecture of our frontend stack. We value initiative and encourage every team member to shape the product and leave a lasting mark. **Requirements** **What You'll Do** * Build and maintain intuitive user interfaces: Design, develop, and optimize frontend features that delight our users and bring our AI products to life * Collaborate across teams: Work closely with designers, product managers, and backend engineers to deliver seamless, high\-performance user experiences * Own your craft: Take full responsibility for code quality, maintainability, and the overall architecture of the frontend codebase * Innovate and iterate: Move fast, experiment boldly, and play an active role in shaping the evolution of our platform's frontend **Who You Are** * You ship and you care: You take pride in your work — clean code, smooth interactions, polished UI. Good enough isn't enough for you * You stay curious: You follow what's happening in the frontend ecosystem, try new tools, and bring ideas to the team. You don't wait to be told what to learn * You take ownership: You don't just build what's asked — you think about the why, push back when something doesn't make sense, and drive things forward * You thrive in ambiguity: Startups move fast. You're comfortable figuring things out, making decisions, and iterating quickly * You work with modern tools: You have hands\-on experience with React, TypeScript, and Next.js. Bonus if you've worked with Tailwind CSS, Zustand, React Query, or component libraries like shadcn/ui * You leverage AI as a tool, not a crutch: You know how to use tools like Claude Code, Cursor, or Copilot to move faster and ship better code — but you understand what you're building and why. AI slop doesn't pass your own code review * You're frontend\-first, but not frontend\-only: You're most at home building great user experiences, but you're comfortable navigating backend code when needed. You don't need a backend engineer to unblock you for every API change **Benefits** **Why Newcode.ai?** * Join a collaborative, high\-energy team where your ideas are heard and your impact is real * Help design, build, and launch products shaping the future of artificial intelligence * Work flexibly, in English, from anywhere in the EU At Newcode.ai, you don't just see the future—you help create it. Ready to shape what's next? Apply today and join us in building a smarter, more efficient world powered by AI.

title: Fund Accounting & Oversight - Associate
description: Location: **Dublin, Leinster** Team: **PFS** Job Requisition \#: **R263808** Date posted: **May. 05, 2026** **Job description** ------------------- **About this role** Elevate your career by joining the world's largest asset manager! Thrive in an environment that promotes positive relationships and recognizes outstanding performance! We know you want to feel valued every single day and be recognized for your contribution. At BlackRock we strive to empower our employees and actively engage your involvement in our success. With over USD $11 trillion of assets we have an outstanding responsibility: our technology and services empower millions of investors to save for retirement, pay for college, buy a home and improve their financial well\-being. Team Overview Position within the Alternatives Accounting and Product Controllers team being responsible for BlackRock’s Alternative's business. As an experienced professional, this position supports a complex and fast\-paced alternative investment fund business in a dynamic and challenging environment. Alternative Investments is a strategic focus for the firm and this position will be part of a core accounting team in Dublin, Ireland. The position offers significant growth opportunities both organically as the firm expands our product offerings, as well as opportunities to be involved in ongoing platform wide projects. Role Responsibilities * Demonstrate complete ownership of the day to day accounting and administrator oversight to ensure timely and accurate delivery of reporting to LPs * Interact with multiple internal and external partners over the course of the year, requires strong communication skills and ability to understand third party requests and deliver solutions to meet those partner needs * Ensure a robust control environment exists both at the fund service providers and internally to ensure risk of error is mitigated * Oversee regulatory compliance with SEC, NFA, and other regulations for product’s under the individual’s purview * Recalculate management fee and carried interest in accordance with fund legal documents and report associated revenue to corporate controllers team * Responsible for engaging with audit team around complex accounting topics and successful planning, coordination and completion of audited financials each year * Communicate performance results to investors and resolve investors’ questions. * Participate in investor due diligence meetings, as required for product set, and explain the controls in place to ensure accurate reporting to clients is achieved * Field custom reporting requests and projects for investors and/or business specific needs and develop reporting to deliver back to clients/partners * Drive increased efficiency and innovation through continuous review of operating procedures and planning / evaluating technology enhancements. Experience * 5\+ years of investment / fund accounting / fund operations / public accounting (audit) work experience or equivalent, including review experience for at least a portion of that time. * Bachelors’ degree in Accounting (strongly preferred) or Finance. CPA or equivalent a plus. * Excellent attention to detail and written and verbal communication skills * Proper and effective accounting skills including knowledge of GAAP, financial statements, performance fees and carried interest. * Sound analytical and problem solving skills. * Works effectively to plan and prioritize responsibilities including ongoing and ad hoc projects simultaneously; * Fluent in Microsoft Office products, emphasis on Excel. **Our benefits** To help you stay energized, engaged and inspired, we offer a wide range of employee benefits including: retirement investment and tools designed to help you in building a sound financial future; access to education reimbursement; comprehensive resources to support your physical health and emotional well\-being; family support programs; and Flexible Time Off (FTO) so you can relax, recharge and be there for the people you care about. **Our hybrid work model** BlackRock’s hybrid work model is designed to enable a culture of collaboration and apprenticeship that enriches the experience of our employees, while supporting flexibility for all. Employees are currently required to work at least 4 days in the office per week, with the flexibility to work from home 1 day a week. Some business groups may require more time in the office due to their roles and responsibilities. We remain focused on increasing the impactful moments that arise when we work together in person – aligned with our commitment to performance and innovation. As a new joiner, you can count on this hybrid model to accelerate your learning and onboarding experience here at BlackRock. **About BlackRock** At BlackRock, we are all connected by one mission: to help more and more people experience financial well\-being. Our clients, and the people they serve, are saving for retirement, paying for their children’s educations, buying homes and starting businesses. For additional information on BlackRock, please visit @blackrock (opens in new window) \| Twitter: @blackrock (opens in new window) \| LinkedIn: www.linkedin.com/company/blackrock (opens in new window) BlackRock is proud to be an Equal Opportunity Employer. We evaluate qualified applicants without regard to age, disability, race, religion, sex, sexual orientation and other protected characteristics at law. **Job Requisition \#** R263808

title: GTM Engineer (RevOps) (f/m/x)
description: *Personio's intelligent HR platform helps small and medium\-sized organizations unlock the power of people by making complicated, time\-consuming tasks simple and efficient. Our team of 1,500 Personios is building user\-friendly products that delight our 15,000\+ customers and their 1\.5 million employees. Ready to make an impact from day one?* **This role requires office attendance in Dublin / Munich / Berlin / Madrid** The GTM Engineering team sits within Revenue Operations and is the builder layer of Personio's AI\-Powered GTM programme. We design and deploy automated workflows, AI agents and data enrichment systems that make our go\-to\-market teams faster, smarter and more effective. We are looking for a Revenue Operations Manager / Analyst, GTM Engineering (RevOps) to join the team. This is a senior individual contributor role: you will own complex end\-to\-end workflows, lead key planning workstreams and become a go\-to subject matter expert on GTM systems and automation at Personio. **Role Responsibilities: What you'll do** * Own and lead the design, build and optimisation of automated GTM workflows across Salesforce, Clay, Gong, LLMs and Workato, connecting enrichment, scoring and outbound into unified systems * Build AI\-powered agents and assistants (e.g. LLM agents, Gong\-powered research briefs) that augment rep productivity at scale * Build and iterate on Clay tables and workflows for automated lead enrichment, account research and personalised outreach * Own meetings end\-to\-end: set the agenda, lead discussions and drive decisions with GTM stakeholders * Identify process inefficiencies and drive innovative improvements through "out of the box" thinking * Become a company source of reference on a key topic or process within GTM Engineering * Effectively set project workplans, anticipate red flags and proactively guide peers * Coach and onboard new team members; provide constructive feedback to team and cross\-functional partners * Communicate findings and recommendations across several stakeholders in high\-impact written material (PPT, email, dashboards) * Document every system you build so partner teams can operate it long\-term, and lead first\-cycle enablement with reps **Role Requirements: What you need to succeed** * 4\+ years in a technical GTM, RevOps, Sales/Marketing Ops or GTM Engineering role in a B2B SaaS environment * Proven ability to lead complex, cross\-functional projects end\-to\-end with minimal guidance * Deep hands\-on expertise with at least three of: Salesforce, Clay, Gong, HubSpot, Outreach, Workato/Zapier/Make * Experience with AI/LLM tools, prompt engineering and agentic workflows; ability to evaluate and implement new tooling * Strong data fluency: able to build robust analyses, dashboards and reports in Sheets/Excel, Tableau or BI tools * Demonstrated ability to influence stakeholders and drive alignment across multiple teams * Strong communication skills: you can adapt your style to technical and non\-technical audiences in both written and verbal formats * Experience building agents (e.g. via AWS bedrock) a plus * Track record of applying 80/20 prioritisation to deliver maximum impact at scale ‌ **Why Personio** Personio is an equal opportunities employer, committed to building an integrative culture where everyone feels welcomed and supported. We embrace uniqueness and understand that our diverse, values\-driven culture makes us stronger. This role is office\-based, with 3 days per week in your contracted office. The remaining days can be worked from home. You will also have 20 Flex Days per year to work remotely from other locations. **Aside from our people, culture, and mission, check out some of the other benefits that make Personio a great place to work:** * Receive a competitive reward package – reevaluated each year – that includes salary, benefits, and pre\-IPO equity * Enjoy 28 days of paid vacation, plus an additional day after 2 and 4 years * Make an impact on the environment and society with 1 (fully paid) Impact Day * Receive generous family leave, child support, mental health support, and sabbatical opportunities * We enjoy gathering for meals, cultural initiatives, and events like local Summer Sessions and year\-end celebrations. There's also healthy snacks, drinks, and a weekly catered lunch

title: Governance & Executive Support Officer
description: **Governance \& Executive Support Officer** Our client, a well\-established not\-for\-profit professional membership organisation based in Dublin 2, is looking for an experienced Governance \& Executive Support Officer to join their small, close\-knit team on a permanent basis. This is a newly created role, and a genuine opportunity to own the governance function of a respected organisation that plays an important role. With a significant strategic agenda ahead, the senior leadership team need a trusted, capable person to take full ownership of the governance \& executive support function. If you have a background in board support, governance administration or executive support within a not\-for\-profit, membership body, charity, public body or similar organisation, and you thrive working independently in a small team, this could be a really rewarding role. **What’s On Offer** * Salary: €50,000 – €60,000 depending on experience * Permanent, full\-time contract \- 4\-day week considered for the right candidate * 22 days annual leave plus additional days over the Christmas period * Employer pension contribution of 12% (following probation, on minimum 4% employee contribution) \- well above market standard * Annual performance bonus of up to 6% * Death in service and income protection scheme (following probation) * Hybrid working 2 days in office / 3 days from home, with further flexibility an option once probation has been completed. * City centre location, Dublin 2 * A stable, supportive working environment where your contribution genuinely matters **About the Organisation** This is a respected, long\-established professional membership body with a large and active community it serves across Ireland. The internal team is small which means your work is visible, valued and makes a real difference day to day. As a regulated professional body with statutory responsibilities, significant membership obligations and a busy governance calendar, the organisation carries a busy governance workload. Board and committee meetings, statutory filings, policy frameworks, AGMs, compliance programmes all needs to be managed. **The Role** You’ll be the person who keeps the governance function running smoothly \- from preparing board papers and taking minutes, to drafting policies, managing statutory filings and supporting the executive team. It’s a broad, varied position that requires someone who is organised, discreet and comfortable working with minimal direction. This role is newly created, which means there’s real scope to look at how things are done and shape them going forward. If you see a better way of doing something, you’ll be encouraged to say so. You’ll need to be confident working independently \- knowing when to run with something and when to check in. You won’t be hand\-held, but you will be well supported. **What You’ll Be Doing** **Board, Committee \& Executive Support** * Organising and preparing agendas and papers for Board and key committee meetings * Taking accurate minutes, drafting resolutions and following up on actions * Supporting AGM preparation and coordination * Providing administrative support to senior management and working groups * Coordinating insurance renewals and managing key deadlines **Governance \& Compliance Administration** * Managing statutory registers and filings with the Companies Registration Office (CRO) and Register of Beneficial Ownership (RBO) * Leading on developing and refining internal policies and governance documents — pulling together existing information, identifying gaps, incorporating feedback and producing clear, practical outputs * Supporting the development of and ensuring compliance with membership rules and procedures * Overseeing compliance monitoring programmes — tracking requirements, managing audits and advising on obligations * Supporting complaints and conduct procedures * Managing regulatory submissions, statutory returns and correspondence with external bodies * Supporting risk monitoring and reporting on internal controls **What We’re Looking For** * Experience developing and refining policies and governance documents \- pulling together existing information, identifying gaps and producing clear, practical outputs * Experience preparing board or committee papers and taking formal minutes Beyond those, we’re looking for: * A background in a not\-for\-profit, membership body, professional organisation, charity, public body or similarly regulated environment. * Minimum 5 years’ experience in a governance, board support, company secretarial or senior executive support role * Someone who takes initiative when given high\-level direction \- you make considered decisions, state your assumptions and move forward rather than waiting to be told * Exceptional organisational skills and attention to detail. * High level of integrity and discretion \- confidential matters will be part of the day\-to\-day * Strong written communication \- you produce clear, professional documents to a high standard * Comfortable working independently in a small team without large systems or support structures around you * A pragmatic, solutions\-oriented approach \- you see what needs to be done and you do it * Proficiency in Microsoft 365 \- including Outlook scheduling, shared calendars and an openness to AI\-assisted tools such as Copilot

title: Graduate - Operations Technology Analyst
description: **Overview** The Operations Tech Analyst Programme (OA) is a 9\-month rotational programme for soon to be and recent graduates. This programme provides a blend of education, skill building, and exposure to Susquehanna and the financial services industry. It will prepare you for a full\-time position where you will be designing and developing applications and processes for the operations department. **Our Programme** As an Operations Tech Analyst, you will be exposed to the full life cycle of Susquehanna’s trading activity and develop a fundamental understanding of the industry and trading environment which Susquehanna operates in. Trading Operations work at the intersection of trading, technology, and external counterparties. You will work within the trading operations teams to decompose problems into their logical components and develop efficient technology solutions which will be released to the desks. You and the team will be responsible for the full lifecycle (Design, Build, Test and Maintain) of each application the Trading Operations Tech team takes ownership of. Working alongside our team you will have an in\-depth exposure to all the different trading desks and business areas at Susquehanna. Each rotation will incorporate hands\-on experience and real\-time tech projects to add value. Throughout the rotations you will work with our inhouse technology development teams as well as working closely with our tactical teams. If you have a keen interest in the financial markets, technology and collaborating with traders then this could be the role for you. **What’s In It For You** * Education: Susquehanna’s in\-house education programme is widely recognised as being the best in the industry. You will attend regular seminars to learn about in\-house business, products, and tech (Excel, SQL, Python etc.). * Support: You will be assigned a mentor from our trading operations team for each of your rotations. You will also be assigned a buddy who was a previous Operations Tech Analyst to guide you along the way. * Real Impact: You will develop solutions that will be immediately implemented by the teams. Here, you'll see the real impact that your problem\-solving skills can have on the firm. * Culture: Our non\-hierarchical culture allows employees of every level to thrive. We buck the trend in the Finance world. The environment is casual, collaborative and we focus on continuous development, no matter how long you’ve been in Susquehanna. * Perks: Based in the heart of Ireland’s Financial Services Centre, and a 10\-minute walk from Dublin’s city centre, our employees avail of free onsite catering, a games room, and a large in\-house gym, to name but a few of our favourite perks. **What We're Looking For** * Passion for Tech: You’re eager to apply your tech skills by working with Python, SQL, VBA or other programming languages where appropriate. This role is up to 80% coding so it is essential you have a flare for development work. * Problem Solver: You enjoy solving puzzles and love when you discover a solution to difficult problems. You’re not afraid to discuss ideas and work with others to find the optimal solution. * Communicator: You enjoy building relationships with those you work closely with. You enjoy sharing ideas, expressing your thoughts and listening to the views of others. * Ambitious: You constantly set yourself goals in life and give your all to achieve them. Be it in sports, music, academics, or another passion you have, you’re not afraid to put in the effort to reach your full potential. * Numerical \& Logical: When it comes to decision making, you use clear and sound reasoning. You’re rational and like to use numbers and data to come up with sensible solutions. * Interest in Finance: Interest in the financial markets and gaining exposure to a true front office trading environment. * Curious: You’re eager to find out more about what it’s like to work in the Finance sector, and specifically in an Operations role within a true front office environment. You have an identifiable interest in the financial markets and current affairs. * Soon\-to\-be or recent graduate: Our programme starts in early September 2026 **Interview Process** Apply \-\> Recruiter Interview \-\> Technical Interview \-\> Final Interview \-\> Offer Note: We hire for this programme on a rolling basis, therefore interested candidates are strongly encouraged to apply early. **About Susquehanna** Susquehanna is a global quantitative trading firm powered by scientific rigor, curiosity, and innovation. Our culture is intellectually driven and highly collaborative, bringing together researchers, engineers, and traders to design and deploy impactful strategies in our systematic trading environment. To meet the unique challenges of global markets, Susquehanna applies machine learning and advanced quantitative research to vast datasets in order to uncover actionable insights and build effective strategies. By uniting deep market expertise with cutting\-edge technology, we excel in solving complex problems and pushing boundaries together. If you're a recruiting agency and want to partner with us, please reach out to recruiting@sig.com. Any resume or referral submitted in the absence of a signed agreement will not be eligible for an agency fee.

title: Graduate Journalist / Content Researcher (Full-Time)
description: About the Role At energyefficiency.ie, we are on a mission to demystify home energy upgrades for Irish homeowners. With energy costs rising and climate targets becoming more urgent, our audience needs clear, trustworthy, practical information they can act on. We are looking for a **Graduate Journalist / Content Researcher** to join our growing editorial team. This is an ideal role for a recent journalism, communications, English, media, or related graduate who is curious, detail\-oriented, and excited about producing high\-quality researched content. You will help create deep\-dive guides, explainers, interviews, practical articles, and evidence\-led content that makes complex topics easier to understand. We want someone with journalistic instincts: a person who can find reliable sources, ask good questions, verify information, and turn research into clear, useful, engaging content. You might be researching topics such as the cost of solar panels in Ireland, how SEAI grants work, what homeowners should know before upgrading insulation, or how new technologies are changing the way people manage energy at home. Your job will be to make these subjects accessible, accurate, and genuinely helpful. **Key Responsibilities** * **Research and reporting:** Conduct detailed research using credible sources, including government guidance, expert commentary, data, reports, and interviews where relevant. * **Content creation:** Write high\-quality articles, guides, explainers, and practical resources for Irish homeowners. * **Simplifying complex topics:** Turn technical, policy\-heavy, or data\-led information into clear, reader\-friendly content without losing accuracy. * **Fact\-checking:** Verify figures, claims, grants, regulations, and technical details before publication. * **Editorial collaboration:** Work closely with the Editor to develop ideas, shape briefs, improve drafts, and maintain high editorial standards. * **Audience focus:** Create content that answers real reader questions and helps people make informed decisions. * **Content improvement:** Help update and improve existing articles so they remain accurate, useful, and current. **The Ideal Candidate** * You are a recent graduate or early\-career journalist/content writer with strong research and writing skills. * You have excellent attention to detail and understand the importance of accuracy. * You know how to identify reliable sources, verify information, and structure a clear narrative. * You can explain complicated subjects in simple, engaging language. * You are curious, self\-motivated, and willing to learn quickly. * You have a strong interest in Irish current affairs, public policy, consumer issues, sustainability, homes, technology, or practical service journalism. * You are comfortable taking feedback and improving your work through editing. * Previous writing, student journalism, internship, newsletter, blog, or portfolio experience is welcome, but not essential. **What We’re Looking For** * A strong writer with journalistic instincts. * Someone who enjoys research as much as writing. * A clear communicator who avoids jargon. * A fast learner who can get to grips with unfamiliar topics. * A person who wants to create content that is genuinely useful, not just content that fills space. **Why Join energyefficiency.ie?** * **Meaningful work:** Your content will help people understand their options, reduce energy costs, and make better\-informed decisions. * **Real impact:** Your work will be published, read, and used by thousands of people across Ireland. * **Growth opportunities:** We are part of a wider, rapidly expanding group, with room for high performers to grow into senior editorial, strategy, or management roles. * **Remote flexibility:** The role is remote, with hybrid options available depending on location and business needs. Job Type: Full\-time Pay: €28,000\.00\-€30,000\.00 per year Application question(s): * have you ever had any content published before? (helpful but not a deal breaker) Education: * Bachelor's (required) Work authorisation: * Ireland (required) Work Location: In person

title: Graduate Landscape Architect (Dublin)
description: **Company Description** **At AECOM, we’re delivering a better world.** We believe infrastructure creates opportunity for everyone. Whether it’s improving your commute, keeping the lights on, providing access to clean water or transforming skylines, our work helps people and communities thrive. Our clients trust us to bring together the best people, ideas, technical expertise and digital solutions to our work in transportation, buildings, water, the environment and new energy. We’re one global team – 47,000 strong – driven by a common purpose to deliver a better world. **Here, you will have freedom to grow in a world of opportunity.** We will give you the flexibility you need to do your best work with hybrid work options. Whether you’re working from an AECOM office, remote location or at a client site, you will be working in a dynamic environment where your integrity, entrepreneurial spirit and pioneering mindset are championed. You will help us foster a culture of equity, diversity and inclusion – a safe and respectful workplace, where we invite everyone to bring their whole selves to work using their unique talents, backgrounds and expertise to create transformational outcomes for our clients. We will encourage you to grow and develop your career with us through our technical and professional development programs and diverse career opportunities. We believe in leadership at all levels. No matter where you sit in the organization you can make a lasting impact on the projects you work on, the teams and committees you join and our business. We offer competitive pay and benefits, well\-being programs to support you and your family, and the development resources you need to advance your career. When you join us, you will connect and collaborate with a global network of experts – planners, designers, engineers, scientists, consultants, program and construction managers – leading the change toward a more sustainable and equitable future. Join us and let’s get started. **Job Description** AECOM is seeking a talented Landscape Architect at the start of their careers to join and support the growth of our Landscape Architecture team. As part of one of the UK\&I’s largest and most diverse landscape practices, you will undertake high\-profile regional and global projects across various sectors including urban regeneration, residential developments, environmental enhancement schemes, and energy projects. Our portfolio includes collaborations with local authorities, private clients, and global developers, delivering innovative and sustainable design solutions that support progress towards net zero and deliver wider environmental and social benefits. This role offers the opportunity to deliver transformative projects, within a talented team, and contribute to AECOM’s reputation for design excellence. With a focus on sustainability, innovation, and collaboration, this position is ideal for a self\-motivated individual seeking to start their career in a dynamic and experienced team. **About AECOM’s Landscape Architecture Team** Our Landscape Architecture team is renowned for excellence and multidisciplinary collaboration. We work across all design stages, blending strengths in design and planning to deliver imaginative solutions that enhance communities and environments. Recent projects include energy transition, public realm, active travel, resorts, and new neighbourhoods that shape cities and regions. **Why Join AECOM?** * **Freedom to Grow** : Take control of your working life with flexible working options, including hybrid working and part\-time hours. Work the hours that suit you. * **Career Development** : Help shape landscape architecture at AECOM with opportunities for training and mentoring. * **Inclusive Culture** : AECOM supports diversity and competitive life\-style benefits. **Job Responsibilities** * **Project Involvement** : * Work collaboratively with colleagues to produce drawings, visualisations and technical documents, including concise reports across all stages of planning, and design projects * Contribute to meetings, workshops and presentations developing creative and practical ideas to help solve complex design challenges. * Assist leads with project management, including financial and programme responsibilities. * **Team Development** : * Develop and share knowledge, supporting a culture of continuous growth and development. * Actively engage in working towards the Irish Landscape Institute (ILI) or UK Landscape Institute (LI) professional exam. * **Client Engagement** : * Assist with nurturing strong relationships with clients, stakeholders, and collaborators. * Facilitate proactive client engagement and business development. * **Technical Excellence** : * Prepare and develop designs and technical materials from initial feasibility through to production of construction information. * Support contract administration to ensure high standard of project delivery. * Produce high\-quality reports and drawings with the necessary level of detail for the various stages of development, using appropriate software. * **Collaboration** : * Work iteratively within multidisciplinary teams to deliver cohesive and integrated design solutions. **Qualifications** **Minimum Requirements** * **Experience** : * Graduates of ILI or LI accredited Landscape Architecture degree. * Demonstrable experience of creating placemaking projects that address key drivers of climate resilience, nature recovery and social inequity. * Knowledge and understanding of the Irish planning system. * Understanding of project management, including financial control, reporting, and programme management. * **Technical Skills** : * Proficient in AutoCAD, Adobe Creative Cloud, and Microsoft Office Suite. * Experience with Rhino, Revit and/or Civil 3D is highly desirable. * Skilled in preparing clear graphics and technical reports. * Strong communication skills, including fluency in written and spoken English. * **Other** : * Full Irish, EU/EEA or UK driving licence. **Preferred Qualifications** * ILI or LI\-accredited Bachelors’ degree, Diploma, and/or Masters’ degree. **Additional Information** * Applicants are encouraged to apply as early as possible since CVs will be reviewed, and candidates selected on a rolling recruitment basis for either immediate or advanced offer start. * Please include a covering letter with your application and provide all predicted and/or obtained academic grades and details of the dates you are able to work to/from and well as locations you would be interested in. * You must have the right to work in the UK. **About AECOM** AECOM is the world’s trusted infrastructure consulting firm, delivering professional services throughout the project lifecycle – from advisory, planning, design and engineering to program and construction management. On projects spanning transportation, buildings, water, new energy and the environment, our public\- and private\-sector clients trust us to solve their most complex challenges. Our teams are driven by a common purpose to deliver a better world through our unrivaled technical and digital expertise, a culture of equity, diversity and inclusion, and a commitment to environmental, social and governance priorities. AECOM is a Fortune 500 firm and its Professional Services business had revenue of $16\.1 billion in fiscal year 2025\. See how we are delivering sustainable legacies for generations to come at aecom.com and @AECOM. **Freedom to Grow in a World of Opportunity** You will have the flexibility you need to do your best work with hybrid work options. Whether you’re working from an AECOM office, remote location or at a client site, you will be working in a dynamic environment where your integrity, entrepreneurial spirit and pioneering mindset are championed. You will help us foster a safe and respectful workplace, where we invite everyone to bring their whole selves to work using their unique talents, backgrounds and expertise to create transformational outcomes for our clients. AECOM provides a wide array of compensation, benefits and well\-being programs to meet the diverse needs of our employees and their families. We’re the world’s trusted global infrastructure firm, and we’re in this together – your growth and success are ours too. Join us, and you’ll get all the benefits of being a part of a global, publicly traded firm – access to industry\-leading technology and thinking and transformational work with big impact and work flexibility. As an Equal Opportunity Employer, we believe in each person’s potential, and we’ll help you reach yours. We are a Disability Confident Employer and will offer an interview to applicants who have a disability or long\-term condition, who meet the minimum/essential criteria for the role. Please let us know using this email address ReasonableAccommodations@aecom.com if you would like to apply through the Disability Confident Interview Scheme. All your information will be kept confidential according to EEO guidelines. **ReqID:** REF59091J **Business Line:** B\&P \- Buildings \& Places **Business Group:** DCS **Strategic Business Unit:** Europe \& India **Career Area:** Design **Work Location Model:** Hybrid

title: Graduate Management Trainee - Drogheda
description: **Overview** We’re Enterprise Mobility. A family\-owned, global mobility leader with a $39 billion turnover, nearly 90,000 team members, and operations in 95 countries. Led by CEO Chrissy Taylor, the third generation of the Taylor family, we’re built on a legacy that gives us the stability to focus on the long\-term success of our people, our customers and our business. **Why join the Graduate Management Training Programme?** As a Graduate Management Trainee, you'll have the freedom and support to explore your leadership potential \- and the opportunity to become a branch manager in one of our retail operations in as little as two years. In fact, most of our senior leaders began their careers in this very role \- including our current CEO. From day one, we’ll invest in you. You’ll be in a supportive environment where you’ll take on real responsibilities and gain invaluable hands\-on experience in customer service, sales, marketing, finance, operations, and more. **We work hard and reward hard work** Your work will be recognised with performance\-based incentives and opportunities for continued promotion. Our unique promote\-from\-within culture means you can keep your career moving forward without having to change organisations. **Award\-winning training and development** Whether you’re building on existing strengths or developing new ones, your growth is our priority. Through classroom learning, on\-the\-job training, and mentorship, you’ll have the tools and support to take the next step – and the one after that. **Our doors are open** As a Graduate Management Trainee, your experience at Enterprise Mobility will be as unique as you because you shape who we are as much as we shape your skills, path and potential. We pride ourselves on opening our doors to a variety of voices, giving way to an experience that’s both inclusive and that inspires diversity of thought. **Responsibilities** From your very first day, you’ll be trusted with real responsibility and exposed to all areas of our business. You’ll develop skills in: **Customer Service:** deliver exceptional experiences by confidently handling reservations, resolving enquiries, and building rapport with a diverse range of customers. **Sales and Marketing:** connect with local business partners, grow your network and develop lasting relationships. **Financial Control and Profitability:** understand the financial mechanics of a successful business, including cost control and interpreting profit and loss statements. **Operations and Logistics:** learn how to manage a fleet, plan strategically, and drive performance. **Leadership and Development:** take the lead in mentoring, training, and developing your own team \- with the opportunity to manage and promote others as you grow. **Qualifications** * A Bachelor’s degree is preferred; however, professional experience can be substituted if applicable. * You must have a full manual Republic of Ireland, UK or EU driving licence, but we do make accommodations for applicants who don’t drive due to a disability. * No drug or alcohol related offence on driving record within the last five years is permitted. **Additional Information** * In accordance with current employment permit regulations, we are unable to support an employment permit application for this role, as it is not eligible under the current system. * Regardless of your socio\-economic background, university attended, subject studied, and degree attained, we will always look at how you perform against our competencies and will judge you on that alone. * Please let us know about any accommodations you may need to participate in our recruitment process. * Please limit your application to only one job posting based on where you live and/or plan to work. Applying to multiple locations will delay your application being processed. * This job posting is for applications within the following location(s): Drogheda

title: Graduate Support Specialist
description: **Job description** As we continue to grow and develop advanced solutions for the retail technology space, we have an exciting graduate role available for a Tech **Support Specialist** to join our team in Dublin. This is a fantastic opportunity to be part of a fast\-paced, forward\-thinking company that thrives on data, innovation, and continuous improvement. This hybrid role requires 2 days per week onsite at our Dublin office. **ROLE RESPONSIBILITIES:** * Work closely with the onboarding team during new customer sensor onboarding. * Understand and create workflows around customer onboarding requirements. * Calibrate and configure IoT sensors to ensure data accuracy and reliability across retail environments. * Understand and analyse large\-scale, anonymised shopper data sets. * Manipulate and restructure data from one configuration to another to support various client onboarding needs. * Monitor live data feeds and dashboards, proactively identifying and flagging anomalies or irregular behaviour in datasets. * Respond to customer queries and data requests, providing insights and clear communication on findings. * Collaborate cross\-functionally with technical and commercial teams to support product and customer development. **ROLE REQUIREMENTS:** * Graduated in a technical field preferably computer science, project management, business, or engineering. * Fluent **English** with excellent written and verbal communication skills. * Strong **Excel** capabilities (including formulas, pivot tables, data modelling, and dashboard creation). Excel skills will be tested during the interview process. * Comfortable working with cutting\-edge technologies and modern data environments. * A proactive, problem\-solving mindset with a strong attention to detail. * Ability to work independently and collaboratively in a hybrid team structure. * From time to time this role will require working outside of standard working hours. * **Required** in Python and SQL. Job Types: Full\-time, Graduate Pay: €33,000\.00\-€37,000\.00 per year Benefits: * Additional leave * Bike to work scheme * Company events * Food allowance * Private medical insurance * Sick pay * Work from home Ability to commute/relocate: * Dublin, County Dublin: reliably commute or plan to relocate before starting work (required) Education: * Advanced/Higher Certificate (required) Experience: * Python: 1 year (required) Language: * English (required) Work authorisation: * Ireland (required) Work Location: Hybrid remote in Dublin, County Dublin

title: Graduate Technology Programme 2026
description: **Build the future of connected intelligence. From day one.** Cubic³ is an Irish tech scale\-up at the centre of one of the most exciting transformations in global industry \- software\-defined vehicles, satellite connectivity, and AI\-driven services that keep the world moving. We work with the world's leading OEMs, MNOs, and technology partners. Our technology is in millions of vehicles. And we're just getting started. We're looking for a number **graduates** to join our Dublin HQ as part of our inaugural **Graduate Technology Programme** \- and we want people who are genuinely curious, technically sharp, and ready to make an impact from day one. **What The Programme Looks Like** This is a 24\-month, full\-time programme embedded within one of three specialist engineering teams: * Software Engineering \- building the platforms, applications, and services that power connected devices at scale * Core Network Engineering \- designing and operating the network infrastructure that underpins global connectivity * SIM Engineering \- working at the intersection of hardware, software, and telecoms on the technology that makes seamless connection possible This isn't a rotation scheme. You'll go deep. You'll be a genuine member of your team, contributing to production\-grade work on technology that's live in the real world. **What We're Looking For** You'll be a final\-year undergraduate or master's student in a technology\-focused discipline, think Computer Science, Software Engineering, Mathematics, Electronic Engineering, or similar. **Beyond The Degree, We're Looking For People Who** * Think logically and love solving problems that don't have obvious answers * Communicate clearly, with engineers, with customers, with leadership * Are adaptable. You'll be moving between teams, challenges, and contexts * Have genuine curiosity about how connectivity and software are reshaping industries * Want to grow, not just learn. There's a difference **Where you'll work** Our Dublin office. We're a genuinely global company \- with teams in Germany, Korea, Japan, Brazil and beyond \- but this programme is rooted in Dublin, where the majority of our engineering and product teams are based. **Why Cubic³** * A rare opportunity to join an Irish scale\-up at a genuinely cutting\-edge moment * Work on technology that's in real products, used by real people, worldwide * A learning culture that invests in you, from structured L\&D to mentorship and buddy systems * A team that's warm, diverse, and seriously good at what they do * Award\-winning brand. Silver at the Transform Awards Europe 2026\. Board member of the 5G Automotive Association. This is a company going places. **Sound like you?** We'd love to hear from you. Applications are open now, and our process is designed to be transparent, fast, and genuinely worth your time. We assess how you think, not just what's on your CV. **\#CubicGrad2026** \| **\#Cubic3** \| **\#DublinTech** *Candidates must have a permanent, unrestricted right to work in the Republic of Ireland. Unfortunately, we are unable to offer visa sponsorship for this programme.* *This is 100% onsite in our Dublin Sandyford HQ. We are not in a position to offer relocation, so all applicants should be currently located in Ireland, or be in the process of relocating to Ireland.*
