title: Level 2 Service Engineer
description: **Company Description** Every minute of every day, Smiths Detection’s threat detection and security screening technology helps to protect people and infrastructure, making the world a safer place. Smiths Detection, a global leader in the development, manufacture and management of security and detection solutions designed to make the world a safer place. Our technology provides threat detection and screening solutions for customers in our key markets: aviation, ports and borders, defence, and urban security. Our expertise spans 21 global offices, seven manufacturing sites and five R\&D centres, with a global network of 3,000 dedicated colleagues contributing towards over 40 years at the frontline of advances in safety and security. This results in careers built on variety and the opportunity to work on a range of state\-of\-the\-art solutions, where you can develop your knowledge and experience every day. **Job Description** We are looking for a skilled and motivated Shift Engineer to support the maintenance, troubleshooting, and optimisation of advanced electromechanical and non\-intrusive detection systems at Dublin Airport (with occasional support to Cork Airport) This is a hands on role within a fast\-paced operational environment, where you will play a key part in ensuring system reliability, performance, and customer satisfaction. 12 hour shifts, structured as follows: two consecutive days of work, followed by two consecutive nights of work, followed by four consecutive days off. 2 days 2 nights 4 off. Days is 0700\-1900, Nights is 1900\-0700\. **Key Responsibilities** * Respond promptly to service, repair, and maintenance requests to maximise system uptime * Perform installation, troubleshooting, repair, and preventative maintenance on site * Deliver customer training and provide technical support where required * Maintain accurate service logs, reports, and documentation * Review service activity to identify recurring issues and drive improvements * Manage spare parts lifecycle (ordering, usage, returns) efficiently * Ensure all tools and test equipment are maintained and calibrated * Analyse equipment performance and recommend enhancements * Collaborate with senior engineers, product specialists, and wider teams * Support service sales activities and revenue\-generating initiatives * Participate in site surveys, pilot programs, and special projects * Ensure compliance with health, safety, and environmental regulations * Maintain flexibility for travel, overtime, and shift rotation **Qualifications** * ONC / HNC / Associate Degree in Engineering (or equivalent experience) * Strong experience in electrical, electronic, or electromechanical systems troubleshooting and repair * Experience working in a field service or on\-site engineering role * Good IT skills including MS Office (Word, Excel, PowerPoint) * Experience using diagnostic tools, handheld tools, and test equipment * Strong communication skills with the ability to explain technical information clearly **Desirable** * Experience with mechanical and air conditioning systems * Knowledge of SAP or similar service/reporting systems * Experience in airport environments or detection/screening technology **Additional Information** **We offer…** Join us and we’ll help build your career, with excellent training and opportunities for career growth across the business, both locally and globally. You’ll experience an inclusive environment, with strong leadership and a focus on safety and wellbeing. You’ll also have the flexibility to choose from a wide range of benefits to suit your lifestyle, offering you and your family support from a health and wellbeing, financial and lifestyle perspective. **Join us and work for a world\-leader, with the benefits and training to reward your dedication and skills. Be part of a team where we are making the world a safer place.** We believe that different perspectives and backgrounds are what make a company flourish. All qualified applicants will receive equal consideration for employment regardless of color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, economic status, disability, age, or any other legally protected characteristics. We are proud to be an inclusive company with values grounded in equality and ethics, where we celebrate, support, and embrace diversity. At no time during the hiring process will Smiths Group, nor any of our recruitment partners ever request payment to enable participation – including, but not limited to, interviews or testing. Avoid fraudulent requests by applying jobs directly through our career’s website (Careers \- Smiths Group plc)

title: Logistics Associate
description: #### **About \&Open** We’re the global gifting platform for companies that care. Our platform sends gifts globally at scale, while our team guides the process with taste, thought and attention to detail. As a B Corp\-certified company, we’re all about less waste, more impact. At the end of the day, it’s not how much you send, it’s how you make people feel that matters. Our customers include OpenAI, Hubspot, Revolut and Intercom. Come and grow with us.#### **About the team** Our Logistics team is growing! We’re obsessed with enabling the delivery of gifts to people across the globe, and work to create the best possible solutions for our customers and the people they wish to gift. To make this all happen, we collaborate closely to understand each client's needs and find the most effective methods to surprise and delight the lucky gift recipients. We care deeply about the journey of our gifts, carefully planning every step from source to doorstep. #### **About the role** Come and join our logistics team to support fulfilment operations in our Dublin warehouse. This is an active role that combines hands\-on warehouse work — sorting, packing and managing stock — with system\-side responsibilities such as monitoring our WMS, coordinating delivery exceptions and supporting wider operational initiatives. #### **What you'll do?** * Pick, pack and dispatch outbound shipments accurately, across a range of sizes, formats and gift types. * Receive, check and book inbound shipments into the Warehouse Management System (WMS), ensuring accuracy and timely stock availability. * Work as a team to ensure all orders are shipped within the SLA and contribute towards the successful achievement of targets. * Manage the flow of goods through our Dublin studio warehouse. * Support the planning and execution of large operational initiatives such as stock moves, warehouse changes and ongoing process improvements across the logistics function. * Monitor and analyse in orders across our fulfilment partners to identify discrepancies, delays and fulfilment risks. * Coordinate delivery exception for shipments in transit by identifying issues and supporting investigations and solutions. * Perform system and administrative updates to ensure data accuracy across operational tools. * Assist with other ad\-hoc tasks required to support the day\-to\-day running of Logistics operations. * Support the wider Logistics team by providing cover during holidays, busy periods and team absences. #### **What you'll need** * Fluent written and spoken English is essential. * Experience of working in a customer focused environment. * 1–3 years of experience in a warehouse, fulfilment, or logistics environment. * Comfortable with the physical demands of the role: standing for extended periods, lifting and moving boxes, and working in an active warehouse environment. * Experience working with a WMS or similar inventory/order management system is a strong plus. * Exceptional attention to detail, a positive can\-do attitude, time management, and organisational skills. * Ability to work in a fast\-paced environment to tight timelines. #### **Benefits** * Competitive Salary * Company Pension * Healthcare cover * 25 days Annual Leave \+ 2 Company Days * Maternity and Paternity Paid Leave * Exciting experience in a fast\-paced start\-up environment * Employee Stock Options Plan #### **Location** Head Office: Dublin, Harold's Cross #### **All Welcome Here Statement** We believe diverse teammates, opinions and backgrounds generate a larger global impact and more considerate acts of care. \&Open is an equal opportunity employer and we do not discriminate on the basis of race, religion, colour, national origin, gender, sexual orientation, age, marital status, veteran status or disability status.

title: Logistics Team Member
description: **Role Title:** Logistics Team Member **Location:** Naas Road **Contract:** 9 month fixed term contract Vhi Health \& Wellbeing DAC currently has a 9 month fixed term contract role for the position of Logistics Team Member This role will contribute to the operations of the logistics area in line with company policy. To ensure excellent logistic support to the Vhi Hospital@Home clinical team and the Corporate LiveWell team **About Us** Our purpose at Vhi is to help our customers and patients live longer, stronger and healthier lives. A big part of delivering this purpose is to create a workplace culture where all colleagues feel connected, informed and motivated to do their best work. At Vhi, we have a set of shared values that drive the kind of behaviour we want and that will support colleagues to grow, develop and play a key part in our success. Leading the way in private healthcare, we now offer an extensive range of healthcare services, including: urgent care, planned care, primary care, health screening, hospital @ home, women’s health, digital health, out of hours emergency dental care, and nurseline 24/7\. The efficient running of these services is made possible by our teams of highly skilled healthcare professionals. **What We Offer** In addition to offering a competitive basic salary, the offer comes with the following benefits: * Bonus * Health Insurance * Pension * Income Continuance * EAP In addition, Vhi offers a range of services and programs designed to support your physical, mental, and emotional health. Services include the Vhi 360 health centre with lifestyle and complementary therapies, an **What Will You Do** * Liaise with the Logistics Team Lead to ensure smooth running of the logistics area and ensure company policy is adhered to particularly in relation to infection control and Dangerous Goods Safety Advisor (DSGA) policy. * Order and rotate approved stock as requested by the Logistics Team Lead. * Fulfil daily orders from the Clinical Staff for consumables and liaise with the clinical staff regarding collections and delivery of stock. * Liaise with pharmacy daily to deliver prescriptions and collect supplies as requested. * Liaise with Vhi Hospital@Home patients and their relatives in a professional manner to arrange delivery of stock to their homes. * Assist patients with placement of stock in the home. * Report back to Logistics Team Lead any issues regarding stock and be proactive in ensuring there is sufficient stock in the store taking lead\-times into consideration. * Monitor movement of medical electronic equipment to patient homes, assist with weekly swap\-outs and assist with weekly quality control (QC) of equipment. * Ensure all returned medical equipment is decontaminated as per infection control policy, labelled and stored as per company policy. * Ensure all chemicals are managed safely as per DGSA policy. * Ensure all Carriage of Dangerous Goods by Road (ADR) documentation is completed daily and submitted in line with company policy. * Ensure all Infection Control (IPC) policies are adhered to and carry out monthly self\-audit of logistics area in conjunction with IPC Nurse and Logistics Team Lead. * Ensure on a daily basis the logistic area is temperature controlled and report any variances to the Logistics Team Lead. * Liaise with clinical staff regarding collection of clinical waste, ensure transportation is within DGSA guidelines and ensure the clinical waste room is maintained in line with company/DGSA policy. * Always ensure for patients receiving medication an anaphylaxis kit is included with the supplies and tracker is completed. * Report any issues to the Logistics Team Lead. * Assist the Logistics Team Lead with the annual calibration programme by rotating equipment to staff and entering calibration updates on the Q pulse system. * Be flexible in times of emergency or adverse weather conditions where extended working hours will be required. Blood Collection Responsibilities * Co\-ordinate all blood collections with nursing and medical staff to ensure specimens are collected and transported to the lab on time and in line with DGSA policy. * Plan an efficient route daily to ensure the urgent bloods are collected earlier in the day. * Liaise with the laboratory daily to ensure all samples are checked in and receipts returned. * Send a daily report to Vhi Hospital@Home doctors regarding which bloods were sent to the laboratory. * Ensure all documentation is completed prior to and when the blood arrives in the laboratory. * Ensure there is a supply of blood collection equipment and forms available for staff and stores area. * Record all non\-conformances with the laboratory on Q Pulse. Doctor Transport * Safely transport doctors and medical supplies to and from patient homes using the most time efficient route. * Identify locations to be visited and develop an effective route plan to meet the daily journey requirements. * Advise doctors of efficient routes based on work plan for the day to maximise time and reduce fuel consumption. * Report to Logistics Team Lead if medical staff are using inefficient routes to see patients. * Liaise with patients in advance of doctor visit to agree a suitable arrival time for the doctor. * Ensure the vehicle is in good working order and daily vehicle checklist has been completed * Ensure there is and spill kit in the vehicle and be familiar with its indications and use. * Ensure the emergency equipment and doctor’s equipment is checked and ready for use. * Transport doctor’s emergency and drug bag from the Safe to car daily. * Check expiry dates of consumables as part of daily emergency checklist. * Report any non\-conformances to the logistics Team Lead. * Ensure medical drug bag is removed from the vehicle daily and locked away in the Safe. * Assist the doctor in the event of a cardiac arrest of patient or other emergencies as outlined in Basic Life Support (BLS) training. * Complete and submit weekly medical utilisation reports. Corporate LiveWell Logistics * Work with the Logistics Team Lead when assigned to the Corporate LiveWell team to ensure there is efficient delivery and collection of all supplies to the corporate site. * Liaise with the dedicated HR person in the corporate site to gain access to the LiveWell rooms for delivery and collections. * Work with the on\-line LiveWell planner to ensure correct stock ready for each site. * Assist with the monthly Quality Control (QC) of the point of care equipment. * Rotate cholesterol equipment into Harcourt Street for monthly QC in conjunction with the screening lead nurse. * Undertake such other duties as may be assigned from time to time by the Logistics Team Lead or Operations Manager. **What you need to be successful Education \& Experience** * Full clean driving licence. * Previous experience working in a logistics department. * An aptitude for or previous experience in a medical or pharmaceutical environment is desirable * Good analytical skills. * Excellent IT skills. **Interpersonal Skills** * Good written and oral communication skills * Prioritising and organisational skills are essential. * Customer focused with caring and helpful attitude is essential. * Demonstrated alignment to Vhi values **Sustainability** At Vhi, we believe that the health of people and the health of the planet are interconnected. That’s why we’re on a journey towards an integrated model of healthcare that puts people and the planet at its heart. **Our Commitment to Inclusion and Accessibility** At Vhi, we are on a continuous journey to build an inclusive and diverse workplace where everyone feels valued and respected. We welcome applications from individuals of all backgrounds, experiences, abilities, and perspectives We are committed to providing reasonable accommodations at every stage of the recruitment process for candidates with disabilities, neurodivergence, or medical conditions. If you require an accommodation, please contact recruitment@vhi.ie, and a member of our recruitment team will be in touch via email. Any information you provide will be treated confidentially within the recruitment team and used solely for the purpose of determining and providing appropriate accommodations during the application and recruitment process. **Vhi is an equal opportunities employer** **Ready to start your journey? Apply now.**

title: Maintenance Engineer
description: **Electrical Maintenance Engineer (Shift\-Based \| 4\-Day Work Week)** **Be part of something being built, not just maintained.** This is a great opportunity to join high\-volume manufacturing operation entering an exciting phase of growth and investment. As part of this, a **new Electrical Maintenance Engineer role** has been created. If you’re someone who enjoys solving problems, improving systems, and being trusted to make an impact, this is a role where you won’t just “keep things running”… you’ll help define how they run in the future. **What You’ll Be Doing** * Diagnosing and resolving faults across production and site equipment * Supporting the reliability of automated machinery (e.g. conveyors, packaging systems, processing equipment) * Carrying out planned preventative maintenance and condition monitoring * Responding quickly to breakdowns to minimise downtime * Contributing to continuous improvement and reliability initiatives * Performing root cause analysis and implementing long\-term fixes * Maintaining accurate maintenance records and system updates * Working collaboratively with production and leadership teams * **Playing a key role in the installation, testing, and optimisation of new equipment** **What We’re Looking For** * Qualified in an electrical discipline (time\-served Craft Electrician or equivalent, ) * At least 2 years’ experience in a similar industrial or manufacturing environment * Strong fault\-finding and problem\-solving ability * Comfortable working hands\-on with both electrical and mechanical systems * A proactive mindset you don’t wait to be told, you take ownership * Flexible and team\-oriented approach to shift work * Good communication skills and attention to detail **What’s On Offer** * **€70,000 \+ DOE** * Pension Contribution 5% * 40\-hour week across a 4\-day shift pattern * Annual medical * Full PPE and your own professional toolbox provided **Working Hours \& Shift Pattern** * 4\-day working week (4 shifts over 7 days) * 4 x 10 hour shifts \-3 days off back to back * Start times rotate between: * 4:00am – 2:00pm * 6:00am – 4:00pm * 12:00pm – 10:00pm **Why Apply?** This isn’t a backfill role, you’ll be joining at a time of **real investment and change** . If you want to move beyond reactive maintenance and **get exposure to projects, installs, and improvements** , this is the right opportunity.

title: Mechanical Engineer - Dublin or Cork
description: **MECHANICAL ENGINEER \- DUBLIN OR CORK** **RED are an expanding international M\&E consultancy, with a proven track record in low energy design, focused on providing excellent design solutions to clients.** Through our market leading technical expertise, we enable the world’s digital infrastructure and develop the built environment whilst also helping clients realise ambitious plans for zero carbon\-built environments. Our approach is centred around listening to our clients and interacting energetically with our fellow project stakeholders. You will have experience in the delivery of European data centres and mission\-critical projects, along with other sector experience including process pharmaceutical, healthcare, commercial, labs and/or clean rooms would be desirable. **Role Responsibilities** * Responsible for the running of small projects. Researches, develops, designs and assumes responsibility for coordination and monitoring of engineering project services for Mechanical or Electrical installations, systems, equipment, facilities etc. * Develops and specifies applications of controls, instruments, and operating systems for all building engineering services purposes * Surveys tests and reports on existing electrical/mechanical installations * Specifies activities to ensure that manufacturing, construction, installation, and operational testing confirm to functional specifications and customer requirements * Represents Company at technical meetings with customers, professional teams and contractors teams * Preparation of specification modifications (word or excel) * Ensure engineering calculations and drawings meet required robust industry and legal standards * Responsible for validation of Intermediate \& Graduate Engineer input into designs * Support the delivery of projects in accordance with the expectations of the RED Project Delivery Process from a Project Management, Financial Management, QA/QC and Technical Review perspective **ROLE REQUIREMENTS** * HND or degree qualified or equivalent experience * Working towards or have achieved MCIBSE/MIEE or equivalent * Working towards Chartered Eng status * Experience of specification design/drawing for at least one building type * Sound knowledge of key Health and Safety issues and legislation * Knowledge of MS Office applications word, excel etc * Ability to use IT based calculation and spreadsheet software * Good understanding of BS standards * Ability to prioritise own workload * Ability to work to deadlines * Managerial Skills * Act in an ethical manner and follows the principles of the Ethics Charter and Ethics in Practice Guidelines * Background in consulting or construction preferred * Good understanding of international working and business operations of a global consultancy * Act in an ethical manner and follow the principles of the Ethics Charter and Ethics in Practice Guidelines of the Group **BENEFITS** RED offers a variety of benefits, including but not limited to: * 23 days annual leave rising to 28 days annual leave over 5 years’ service * Holiday purchase and buy back scheme * Hybrid Working * Contributory company pension scheme * Healthcare Scheme via salary sacrifice (Company funded cover is dependant on level) * Non\-contractual bonus awards * Training sponsorship * Charity volunteering leave * Wellness days/ initiatives. * Salary commensurate with experience. * Our people work in friendly teams with a collaborative approach and mentoring support from talented leaders. * We offer a comprehensive benefits package and are an equal opportunities employer. * This role will ideally suit those with the right to live \& work in Ireland without restrictions, as we are unable to offer sponsorship at the present time. RED is a company of Tractebel. Business Unit: Tractebel Division: Tractebel Div2 Legal Entity: TRACTEBEL ENGINEERING IRELAND LIMITED Professional Experience: Skilled ( \>3 experience \<15 years) Education Level: Bachelor's Degree Company Name: RED

title: Mid-Level Full Stack Java Engineer
description: Solance is redefining financial services for the B2B market, with a dedicated focus on family offices and corporate service providers—sectors often overlooked by traditional banks. We are currently in the process of obtaining an EMI (Electronic Money Institution) licence in Ireland, with a long\-term vision to evolve into a fully licensed bank. Our mission is to combine the technological advantages of a neobank with the personalised, high\-touch service of a private bank—delivering exceptional, tailored support to the specific needs of our Clients. **About this role** This is an opportunity to join Solance Europe Limited as an early\-stage engineer in our Dublin city centre based team. You'll report to the CTO and collaborate with the Principal Engineer and the rest of the Product and Tech team members to build a greenfield, cloud\-native banking and payments platform using a modern Java and React native based stack. This is a **hands\-on engineering role** focused on delivering high\-quality features, improving reliability and developer experience, and growing your ownership over services over time. You'll help build a resilient, scalable and secure platform that improves the experience for our **Clients** . You will contribute to building core services, APIs, UI flows, and automated pipelines in a regulated environment—learning best practices in security, resilience, and quality along the way. **Requirements** **Experience** * Track history in deep technical and business curiosity and initiative to deliver value and make things better for their Customers * 2\-4 years of professional experience in software engineering (full stack or strong backend with solid frontend capability). * Experience delivering production software in an agile environment. * Practical experience with NoSQL databases (preferably DynamoDB), including understanding of partition/sort keys, indexing strategies * Strong communicator who collaborates effectively with other engineers and cross\-functional stakeholders. * Proactive, curious, and motivated to learn—able to take ownership of well\-defined areas and expand scope over time. **Technical Skills** * Solid proficiency in **Java** backend development and building **RESTful APIs** . * Practical experience with **React** Native (or closely related modern frontend frameworks). * Working knowledge of **AWS** (deploying/operating services, or building on managed cloud services). * Comfortable with **Git** , code reviews, and collaborative development workflows. **Quality / Operations** * Experience writing automated tests and contributing to reliable release practices. * Familiarity with CI/CD concepts and an interest in automation (build/test/deploy). * Basic understanding of observability (logs/metrics/alerts) and production support practices. **Nice to have** * Exposure to fintech domains such as **payments, onboarding, KYC/AML, wallets, FX, cards** , or regulated environments. * Familiarity with security fundamentals (OWASP, least privilege, encryption in transit/at rest). * Some experience with Python or React Native (helpful but not required). **Benefits** * Join a startup with a bold vision to redefine B2B financial services. * Work on a modern greenfield platform with strong technical guidance and high standards. * Build in a regulated environment and develop valuable experience in security, resilience, and operational excellence. * Make a meaningful impact within the Tech team while growing your own career quickly as part of our early\-stage team.

title: Mobile Mechanic
description: **Mobile Technician – Full Time** Dublin (Field\-Based) United Tyres Autoservice Job Type: Full\-time, Permanent **Purpose of the Role** Carry out mobile jobs safely and professionally, represent United Tyres Autoservice to a high standard in the field, and make good decisions on when work should be escalated to the workshop or recovery. **Why This Role Matters** Mobile work is often the **first real experience customers have with United Tyres Autoservice**. title: Multiple Openings (Associate), Fund/Client Accounting
description: **We welcome you to apply!** When applying to this general posting, our expert BNY Talent Acquisition Team may also review your resume for consideration across other open roles within the company. At BNY, our culture allows us to run our company better and enables employees’ growth and success. As a leading global financial services company at the heart of the global financial system, we influence nearly 20% of the world’s investible assets. Every day around the globe, our 50,000\&\#43; employees bring the power of their perspective to the table to create solutions with our clients that benefit businesses, communities and people everywhere. Together, we help make money work for the world – and this is what \#LifeAtBNY is all about. Join us and be part of something extraordinary. We’re seeking a future team members for the role of **Fund Accountants (Associate)** to join our **Fund Accounting team** . This role is located in Dublin, Ireland. In this role, you’ll make an impact in the following ways: * Perform accurate and timely delivery of daily NAV calculations in a fast paced and rapidly evolving environment and act as point of escalation for the team for senior / team lead positions * Maintain open lines of communication with key internal teams to ensure accurate delivery of NAV critical inputs, in line with internal service delivery agreements * Critically analyse fund movement and act as point of contact for queries or escalation items from the team within the NAV production environment * Investigate and ensure full close out of operational and audit queries, from both internal and external stakeholders * Manage daily operational requirements across the team, including but not limited to team allocation of duties, annual leave, team performance etc. * Provide key considerations and feedback for initiatives to support change and improvements in alignment with the firm's strategy * Ensure escalation of items requiring urgent resolution to Management Team in a timely manner To be successful in this role, we’re seeking the following: * 3\-7\&\#43; years’ Fund Accounting experience within Operations * Prior experience using Enterprise Invest One is highly preferred * Knowledge of the components of daily NAV calculations and complex financial instruments * Knowledge of Fund Accounting requirements for UK OEICs and/or Luxembourg SICAVs an advantage * Skilled in critically analysing all aspects of NAV Production to identify items requiring further investigation or escalation for senior level positions * Third level degree qualified, ideally in Accounting or Business * Excellent communication skills At BNY, our culture speaks for itself, check out the latest BNY news at: BNY Newsroom BNY LinkedIn Here’s a few of our recent awards: America’s Most Innovative Companies, Fortune, 2024 World’s Most Admired Companies, Fortune, 2024 Human Rights Campaign Foundation, Corporate Equality Index, 100% score, 2023\-2024 Best Places to Work for Disability Inclusion, Disability: IN – 100% score, 2023\-2024 “Most Just Companies”, Just Capital and CNBC, 2024 Dow Jones Sustainability Indices, Top performing company for Sustainability, 2024 Bloomberg’s Gender Equality Index (GEI), 2023 Our Benefits and Rewards: BNY offers highly competitive compensation, benefits, and wellbeing programs rooted in a strong culture of excellence and our pay\-for\-performance philosophy. We provide access to flexible global resources and tools for your life’s journey. Focus on your health, foster your personal resilience, and reach your financial goals as a valued member of our team, along with generous paid leaves, including paid volunteer time, that can support you and your family through moments that matter. BNY is an Equal Employment Opportunity/Affirmative Action Employer \- Underrepresented racial and ethnic groups/Females/Individuals with Disabilities/Protected Veterans. This is a talent pipeline posting and does not represent any one particular job opening. By applying to this pipeline requisition, your interest will be reviewed for multiple potential openings based upon your background and disclosed work preference.

title: Named Account Executive
description: *To get the best candidate experience, please consider applying for a maximum of 3 roles within 12 months to ensure you are not duplicating efforts.* Job Category Sales Job Details **About Salesforce** Salesforce is the \#1 AI CRM, where humans with agents drive customer success together. Here, ambition meets action. Tech meets trust. And innovation isn’t a buzzword — it’s a way of life. The world of work as we know it is changing and we're looking for Trailblazers who are passionate about bettering business and the world through AI, driving innovation, and keeping Salesforce's core values at the heart of it all. Ready to level\-up your career at the company leading workforce transformation in the agentic era? You’re in the right place! Agentforce is the future of AI, and you are the future of Salesforce. The biggest banks and insurance companies in Poland are under pressure to modernise — and they need a trusted partner to help them do it. That's where you come in. At Salesforce, we're not looking for someone to manage accounts. We're looking for someone to *transform* them. As a Strategic Enterprise Account Executive for Financial Services in Poland, you'll sit at the heart of some of the most consequential technology decisions in the market — shaping 3–5 year transformation agendas for Tier 1 and Tier 2 financial institutions, and building the kind of executive relationships that last. This is a role for people who are energised by complexity, not intimidated by it. **What You'll Be Doing** * Owning a portfolio of Poland's top banking and insurance institutions — not managing it, *owning* it * Building and executing account strategies that go way beyond pipeline hygiene — think multi\-year transformation roadmaps * Leading high\-value, multi\-stakeholder sales cycles with CIOs, CTOs, COOs, and Heads of Digital, Risk, and Distribution * Positioning Salesforce's full platform — Sales, Service, Marketing, Data, and AI — as the answer to real business problems: growth, cost efficiency, regulatory pressure, and customer experience * Orchestrating world\-class internal teams (Solution Engineering, Customer Success, Industry Advisors, Partners) to win big * Landing, expanding, and growing accounts at scale — with pipeline that's real, qualified, and predictable **What You Bring** * 10\+ years in enterprise software or SaaS sales — with a track record of 7\-figure\+ deals * Proven experience selling into banking and/or insurance in Poland *(this one's non\-negotiable)* * Confidence operating at C\-level — you challenge thinking, reframe problems, and hold your ground * A deep understanding of what keeps financial institutions up at night: legacy transformation, regulation, digital channels, cost pressure * High ownership mindset — you don't wait for resources, you pull them in * Fluency in Polish and English **Even better if you have:** * Experience with platform sales (CRM, data, cloud, AI) * An existing network within Polish financial institutions * The ability to spot an opportunity inside an RFP — not just respond to one **What Success Looks Like Here** You're consistently delivering against multi\-million quotas. You're the trusted advisor your clients call before they call anyone else. You're expanding accounts from a single use case into enterprise\-wide adoption. And your forecast? It's one you're proud to stand behind. **Why Salesforce** We're the global \#1 in CRM, Data, and AI — and in Poland's financial services market, our brand opens doors. You'll have access to the broadest platform in the industry, the credibility to walk into any boardroom, and the genuine opportunity to shape large\-scale digital transformation in one of Central Europe's most dynamic markets. We're also a company that believes business is the greatest platform for change — and that the best work happens when everyone feels seen, valued, and empowered to bring their whole self to work. Unleash Your Potential When you join Salesforce, you’ll be limitless in all areas of your life. Our benefits and resources support you to find balance and *be your best* , and our AI agents accelerate your impact so you can *do your best* . Together, we’ll bring the power of Agentforce to organizations of all sizes and deliver amazing experiences that customers love. Apply today to not only shape the future — but to redefine what’s possible — for yourself, for AI, and the world. Accommodations If you need a reasonable accommodation during the application or the recruiting process, please submit a request via this Accommodations Request Form . Please note that Salesforce uses artificial intelligence (AI) tools to help our recruiters assess and evaluate candidates’ resumes and qualifications throughout the recruiting process. Humans will always make any candidate selection and hiring decisions. Please see our Candidate Privacy Statement for more information about how we use your personal data and your rights, including with regard to use of AI tools and opt out options. Posting Statement Salesforce is an equal opportunity employer and maintains a policy of non\-discrimination with all employees and applicants for employment. What does that mean exactly? It means that at Salesforce, we believe in equality for all. And we believe we can lead the path to equality in part by creating a workplace that’s inclusive, and free from discrimination. Know your rights: workplace discrimination is illegal. Any employee or potential employee will be assessed on the basis of merit, competence and qualifications – without regard to race, religion, color, national origin, sex, sexual orientation, gender expression or identity, transgender status, age, disability, veteran or marital status, political viewpoint, or other classifications protected by law. This policy applies to current and prospective employees, no matter where they are in their Salesforce employment journey. It also applies to recruiting, hiring, job assignment, compensation, promotion, benefits, training, assessment of job performance, discipline, termination, and everything in between. Recruiting, hiring, and promotion decisions at Salesforce are fair and based on merit. The same goes for compensation, benefits, promotions, transfers, reduction in workforce, recall, training, and education.

title: Network Architect (Azure) contract
description: Senior Network Architect (Azure) Role Overview We are seeking an experienced Network Architect with deep expertise in Microsoft Azure to design, implement, and optimize scalable, secure, and high\-performance network solutions. This role will be responsible for shaping cloud network strategy, ensuring best practices, and supporting complex hybrid and multi\-cloud environments. Key Responsibilities * Design and architect enterprise\-grade network solutions within Microsoft Azure, including hybrid connectivity. * Lead the development of network architecture standards, policies, and best practices. * Implement and manage Azure networking services such as Virtual Networks (VNets), VPN Gateway, ExpressRoute, Azure Firewall, Load Balancers, and Application Gateway. * Design secure connectivity between on\-premises infrastructure and Azure environments. * Optimize network performance, scalability, and cost\-efficiency across cloud environments. * Collaborate with security teams to enforce Zero Trust architecture and compliance requirements. * Provide technical leadership and mentorship to network engineers and cloud teams. * Troubleshoot complex networking issues across hybrid and cloud\-native environments. * Evaluate emerging technologies and recommend improvements to existing architecture. * Produce and maintain detailed architecture documentation and diagrams. Required Qualifications * 8\+ years of experience in network architecture, engineering, or related roles. * Strong hands\-on experience with Microsoft Azure networking services. * Deep understanding of TCP/IP, routing protocols (BGP, OSPF), DNS, and network security principles. * Proven experience designing hybrid cloud networking solutions. * Experience with firewalls, network security groups (NSGs), and micro\-segmentation. * Familiarity with Infrastructure as Code (IaC) tools such as Terraform or ARM templates. * Strong troubleshooting and analytical skills. Preferred Qualifications * Microsoft certifications such as: * Azure Solutions Architect Expert * Azure Network Engineer Associate * Experience with multi\-cloud environments (AWS, GCP). * Knowledge of SD\-WAN and network virtualization technologies. * Experience with DevOps practices and CI/CD pipelines. * Scripting skills (PowerShell, Python). Key Competencies * Strategic thinking and architectural design * Strong communication and stakeholder management * Problem\-solving and decision\-making * Attention to detail and documentation rigor * Leadership and mentoring capabilities

title: Network Engineer
description: About Us Diacom is a rapidly growing Managed IT Service Provider, delivering enterprise\-grade IT solutions across networking, managed IT services, and voice telecommunications. Our tech stack includes HPE Aruba \& Fortinet networking, Microsoft Windows, Microsoft Azure, and Microsoft 365, and an industry\-leading cybersecurity platform in the MSP IT space. Our growth means we need specialists who can maintain the rapid response times our clients depend on. You’ll be joining a team that values curiosity, has autonomy, and demands technical excellence. The Role The Networks Engineer (L2\) supports the delivery of Diacom’s networking projects, solutions, and services across new and existing customer environments. The role is responsible for the configuration, deployment, maintenance, and troubleshooting of network infrastructure, while ensuring continuity of service, customer satisfaction, and ongoing revenue for the networks vertical of the business. The position also contributes to the development of new service lines, supports the helpdesk with escalations, and maintains high\-quality documentation and technical standards. What You'll Do • Lead the design and deployment of enterprise\-grade network solutions for new and existing customer environments. • Provide expert guidance on network strategy, capacity planning, segmentation, scalability, and security design. • Oversee the maintenance, performance, and reliability of customer network infrastructure, including firewalls, switches, wireless access points, and supporting systems. • Ensure best\-practice configuration, deployment, and lifecycle management of: o Switching platforms (HPE Aruba, Fortinet) o Wireless solutions (HPE Aruba, Fortinet) o Firewalls (Fortinet/FortiGate, Palo Alto) o NAC/AAA platforms (Aruba ClearPass) • Plan, coordinate, and execute scheduled firmware upgrades, maintenance windows, and infrastructure refresh activities. • Act as the senior escalation point for complex and high\-impact network incidents from the Diacom Helpdesk and TAC teams. • Lead root\-cause analysis (RCA), remediation planning, and implementation of long\-term resolution strategies. • Produce detailed reports for internal and external stakeholders covering network health, performance trends, and incident outcomes. • Collaborate with VoIP, Managed Services, and Cybersecurity teams on cross\-functional projects and integrated solutions. • Provide technical input and subject\-matter expertise to sales and account management teams, including solution design and pre\-sales activities. • Provide technical leadership, mentoring, and knowledge\-sharing to junior network engineers. • Contribute to internal technical training, standards development, and process improvement initiatives. • Participate in the after\-hours support roster for critical infrastructure and customer\-impacting events. • Provide availability for overtime during major project deployments or urgent customer requirements as required. What We're Looking For • Exposure to/or experience with enterprise networking technologies (Aruba, Fortinet, Palo Alto). • Full Driver’s License • Strong troubleshooting and diagnostic skills across wired and wireless networks. • Understanding of NAC principles and AAA frameworks. • Familiarity with network monitoring tools. • Strong communication and customer service orientation. • Ability to produce accurate technical documentation. Nice to Have • Basic understanding of networking concepts, operating systems, and common security controls • Strong analytical, troubleshooting, and communication skills • Ability to work effectively in a team environment and follow established procedures

title: Newly Qualified Accountant
description: **NEWLY QUALIFIED ACCOUNTANT OPPORTUNITY** \- Balbriggan, County Dublin We are seeking a Newly Qualified Accountant (ACA / ACCA or equivalent) who is ready to step into a client\-facing role with real responsibility from day one. This is an opportunity to move beyond training and take ownership of a diverse client portfolio within a dynamic and supportive environment. Role purpose You will support the client engagement team in delivering high\-quality accounting and advisory services while managing your own portfolio of clients. The role combines technical accounting, client relationship management, and exposure to advisory work. **Key responsibilities** * Take ownership of a client portfolio, including year\-end accounts, management accounts, budgets and forecasts for selected clients * Ensure all client deadlines and deliverables are met, working efficiently and to agreed budgets * Manage and develop client relationships, ensuring high levels of satisfaction and identifying opportunities to provide additional support * Deliver end\-to\-end accounting work, from initial preparation through to final accounts sign\-off * Oversee the completion of accounts and ensure all client queries are dealt with promptly and professionally * Support and deliver specialist advisory and project work, providing clear analysis, options and recommendations * Maintain up\-to\-date technical knowledge, including relevant legislation and accounting standards * Assist with tenders and proposals for new business opportunities * Support the development and training of junior team members * Work effectively with cloud accounting systems, including Xero, and other digital tools **Requirements** * Recently qualified ACA, ACCA or equivalent professional qualification * Previous experience in a similar accounting or client\-facing role * Experience managing or supporting a client portfolio * Strong knowledge of GAAP and accounting standards, particularly FRS 102 1A * Experience in technical compliance and advisory work * Familiarity with cloud accounting software such as Xero * Experience in mentoring or training junior staff * Strong communication skills and a proactive, solutions\-focused approach This role offers the opportunity to take full ownership of client work, develop strong advisory skills, and progress in a modern, technology\-driven accounting environment where responsibility and client impact are central to the role.

title: PET CT Clinical Specialist
description: Alliance Medical currently have an exciting opportunity with an excellent remuneration package available for a PET\-CT Clinical Specialist to join our centre in Co Dublin! The PET\-CT Clinical Specialist will lead the PET\-CT unit, focusing on service delivery, quality assurance, and staff management. This role involves a combination of expert clinical duties, such as ensuring the correct protocols are followed and manage the day\-to\-day operations of the PET\-CT service, and leadership responsibilities, like training staff and supervising service delivery. **Key Responsibilities:** * In conjunction with the PET\-CT Clinical and Administrative staff, provide a safe \& efficient PET\-CT service, while adhering to all legislative regulations and company policies. * Accountable for the quality of the service provided, including customer service \& technical aspects. * Developing and implementing operational policies, protocols \& guidelines. * Maintain up to date knowledge of clinical, technical and radiographic developments in relation to the PET\-CT department and promote awareness of new developments. * Support the Unit Manager in the implementation of initiatives aimed at on\-going service development and improvement. * Promote \& maintain a safe environment for staff and patients. Assist in the development and implementation of risk management and health and safety strategies in association with the Quality Department. * Participate fully with all internal and external audits and Accreditation. * Quality Assurance: Investigate \& take appropriate action in accordance with Company policy in relation to complaints, accidents and incidents. * Ensure that all Company policies \& relevant up\-to\-date information is understood by staff and complied with. * Ensure all Company policies on infection control, IV policy and health and safety etc. are understood by staff and complied with. * Maintain a quality assurance programme in the PET/CT department. * Assist the Radiation Protection Officer to ensure that all principles and policies of radiation protection, are adhered to. * Be familiar with all legislation and directives regarding the safety standards for the protection of health workers and the general public against the dangers arising from Ionising Radiation. * Follow all Radiation Protection practices as determined by the Radiation Protection Committee, Radiation Protection Advisor and Radiation Protection Officer **Key Requirements:** * BSc (Hons) Diagnostic Radiography or equivalent * Active CORU Registration * IV Cannulation * Post\-Graduate Qualification in PET\-CT (desirable) but not mandatory. **Why choose Alliance Medical?** * Competitive Salary * Relocation Allowance (t\&c's) * Comprehensive Induction \& training programme * Family Friendly Working Arrangements * 27 Annual Leave Days (including 2 discretionary days) * Full paid private Health Insurance * Paid Maternity Leave * Wedding Day Leave * Paid Sick Leave * Fertility Leave * Excellent Pension Scheme * Educational Assistance * Wellbeing Initiatives * Excellent overtime rates \#AMDI

title: Part Time Admin Assistant
description: We are looking for a highly organised, proactive, and tech\-savvy Admin Assistant to support an award\-winning multi\-business owner and her manager across three growing businesses. This is a part\-time, in\-person role based between Dublin 7 and Dublin 2, working for approximately 10–20 hours per week for a 12\-month period. About the Role This role is ideal for someone who genuinely enjoys organisation, systems, and helping businesses run smoothly behind the scenes. You’ll be involved in a mix of structured administrative support and ad hoc tasks, helping improve day\-to\-day operations and overall efficiency across multiple businesses. Responsibilities * General administrative support across three businesses * Organising files, information, schedules, and workflows * Setting up and improving systems and operational processes * Supporting project coordination and day\-to\-day business tasks * Using tools such as Notion to manage information and workflows * Exploring and implementing AI tools and smart automations to improve efficiency * Assisting with various ad hoc admin tasks as needed We’re Looking For Someone Who: * Is highly organised and detail\-oriented * Enjoys creating systems and improving processes * Is comfortable learning and using technology and apps * Has experience with (or interest in) tools like Notion and AI platforms * Can work independently and take initiative * Is reliable, adaptable, and proactive * Has strong communication skills and a positive attitude Details * Part\-time: 10–20 hours per week * In\-person role based in Dublin 7 and Dublin 2 * Initial 12\-month commitment * Potential for longer\-term opportunity This is a great opportunity to work closely with a dynamic entrepreneur across multiple businesses and play an important role in helping operations run smoothly and efficiently. Pay: €17\.50 per hour Ability to commute/relocate: * DUBLIN 7, County Dublin: reliably commute or plan to relocate before starting work (required) Education: * Bachelor's (preferred) Experience: * System administration: 1 year (required) Language: * English (required) Work authorisation: * Ireland (required) Work Location: In person

title: Part Time Trainee Cook/Chef
description: We are looking for an applicant who enjoys cooking and working with food. Experience is not essential and full training will be provided where required. The successful candidates primary role will be to prepare meals for our Hot Fresh Food Service counter. The role's normal hours will be mornings 7am \- 3/4pm. We are always keen to hear from Chefs of all grades interested in joining our kitchen team. Candidates should have the ability to work well under pressure. Salary \- competitive and negotiable **The ideal candidate will have:** * Excellent communication skills * Be organised and flexible * The ability to use their own initiative and work as part of our Team * Enjoy cooking \& working with food * A passion for seasonality and for using local and home grown produce * Must have the ability to multi\-task in a fast\-paced environment \& pressurised environment * Ensure excellent food hygiene practice is observed from storage through to preparation and service. * Ensure HACCP compliance within the kitchen. * Must be courteous and focused on providing a consistently high standard of customer service Job Types: Part\-time, Permanent Application question(s): * Please briefly outline your availability for work Education: * Leaving Certificate (preferred) Work Location: In person

title: Policy Specialist
description: Spencer Recruitment is partnering with our client, a specialist public affairs and research consultancy, to appoint a Policy Specialist/Account Manager. This is a focused, purposeful role for a driven professional ready to transition from broad project management into deep subject\-matter expertise. The successful candidate will immerse themselves in the technology and healthcare sectors, mastering the policies, frameworks, innovations, and parliamentary movements that shape them at an Irish and EU level. **About the Job** * Specialised research role embedded within a dynamic public affairs consultancy * Hybrid working model * Opportunity to develop into a recognised Subject Matter Expert in tech and health policy at an Irish and EU level **The Role** * Drive rigorous, proactive policy intelligence across the technology and healthcare sectors * Utilise, train, and refine advanced AI monitoring tools to track parliamentary debates, committee activity, and legislative drafts * Transform complex policy documents and datasets into sharp, actionable intelligence and strategic narratives * Take full ownership of research output \- from initial data extraction through to final, polished insights **Key Responsibilities** * Conduct ongoing research into regulatory shifts, legislative changes, and emerging public affairs trends in tech and health * Monitor and analyse parliamentary debates, committee work, and legislative developments at Irish and EU level * Produce clear, high\-quality written reports and briefings for internal and client use * Manage the end\-to\-end research process with accuracy and strong attention to detail * Maintain and refine AI\-powered monitoring workflows to improve intelligence gathering * Contribute to the development of strategic narratives and client\-ready policy outputs * Stay ahead of sector developments and proactively flag relevant emerging issues **About You** * 2\-4 years' experience in policy, public affairs, research, PR, or a related consultancy or agency environment * Genuine interest in how public policy, parliamentary action, and technological innovation intersect * Highly organised with a proven ability to manage projects and deliver to deadlines * Comfortable working with new software and AI\-driven monitoring tools * Entrepreneurial mindset \- proactive, comfortable with ambiguity, and driven to find answers * Excellent written and verbal communication skills; able to present complex legislative findings clearly and confidently * Analytically minded with a natural aptitude for research and synthesis * Thrives in a startup or fast\-moving environment **What's on Offer** * Highly competitive base salary relative to experience, designed to attract top\-tier talent * Annual performance bonus tied to individual impact and company success * Clear Subject Matter Expert career trajectory in tech and health public affairs * Travel opportunities to attend key industry conferences, policy briefings, and stakeholder events * Supportive, entrepreneurial team environment with real ownership and autonomy

title: Porter
description: We are looking for someone to join our small team, working in the store and showroom of an auction house in Shankill/Bray area. The work includes setting up the floor for monthly auctions, taking items into the store for auction, helping buyers with collection of items. Completing some paperwork when items are being delivered. Organising the store for efficiency. Pay: Up to €15\.00 per hour Benefits: * On\-site parking Work Location: In person

title: Power Systems Engineer, Network Development
description: **Position Description** Our Networks for Net Zero Strategy sets out ESB Networks’ role in enabling the delivery of the Government’s Climate Action Plan and supports the decarbonisation of electricity by 2040, which will enable the achieving of Ireland's net zero ambition no later than 2050\. Climate change is one of the greatest challenges facing humanity and it is widely accepted that rapid action is required to limit greenhouse gas (GHG) emissions to avert the worst consequences of climate change and protect both current and future generations. Through the rapid growth and development of renewable power, Ireland’s electricity system has been significantly decarbonised over the last decade, which had utilised much of the capacity for generation connections on the distribution system to date, with accelerating progress now needed to continue to build increased momentum towards achieving a net zero society. The scale of demand growth driven by electrification requires investment in capacity for demand connections, domestic, farming, industrial and commercial, across all voltage levels. ESB Networks’ role is to deliver the electricity distribution network that will empower all our customers to decarbonise their energy consumption. Our vision is to connect a clean electric future together and our customers and stakeholders will be at the heart of this transformation. We are hiring Powers System Engineers/Network Planners to join or Network Development Team in ESB Networks. Our Network Planners are responsible for planning the medium and long\-term reinforcement of the high voltage, medium voltage and low voltage distribution network to enable the Climate Action Plan targets and our Networks for Net Zero Strategy with a particular focus on the areas of electrification of heat and transport; connection of large demand customer; connection of renewables and development of policies and standards. The roles involve the power system analysis of the LV, MV, 38 kV and 110 kV distribution network to determine medium and long\-term strategic investment requirements, the optimum method of connection for new customers and interaction with various internal and external stakeholders. Network Planners work closely with other functions within ESB Networks in the design and delivery of network plans. The role requires engagement with customers, the transmission system operator, and other stakeholders to ensure development is well founded, and meets requirements. **Key Responsibilities** * Power System Analysis of the 110 kV, 38 kV, MV and LV distribution network and 220 kV bulk supply points in accordance with defined planning standards based on future scenarios. * Use load flow software (Synergi, PSS Sincal, etc.) to carry out studies to assess the impact of embedded generation, RES, flexibility, demand customer connections \& reinforcement options on the distribution system and to determine the Least Cost Technically Acceptable connection method. * Complete Study Documents, Investment Appraisals \& Technical Approvals as required and ensure that each CAPEX proposal has all supporting documentation for the business cases in place. * Ensure all connection applications are processed in line with CRU\-approved policies and ESBN Distribution Planning and Security of Supply Standards * Verify the accuracy and outputs of other team members studies in line with the agreed verification and approval procedure as appropriate. * Respond to customer queries and disputes on the proposed customer connection method as required. * Project manage innovation\-led distribution planning improvement projects from initial scoping and development of terms of reference through to implementation of the recommendations. * Preparation, development, and implementation of planning related standards, policies and tools as required. * Engage with a broad range of stakeholders within industry to listen to what is happening in other jurisdictions and what can be learned from best practice in other utilities. **Experience And Qualifications** * Strong technical capability. * 5\+ Years relevant experience required * Honours Degree in Electrical Engineering or equivalent work experience is essential. * Excellent communication and report writing skills. * Proven track record of delivery of projects on time and to the highest technical standards. * Self\-motivated with ability to project manage and prioritize own work. * Ability to work and share knowledge and competencies within a team structure. * Proven track record of success contributing to the training and development of technical professionals. * Ability to make quality decisions and judgments, with a logical, yet imaginative approach to problem solving. * Full clean drivers license * Minimum engineering experience of five years in the area of Power System Analysis with broad understanding of distribution network, working in any of the areas listed below, preferably including at least two years of experience at an advanced level (checking/verifying work of junior staff, training staff and developing work processes, tools and templates). title: Power Systems Engineer, Network Development
description: Vacancy Number: 28377 Category: Engineering \- Electrical Job Type: Full\-time **Position Description** Our Networks for Net Zero Strategy sets out ESB Networks’ role in enabling the delivery of the Government’s Climate Action Plan and supports the decarbonisation of electricity by 2040, which will enable the achieving of Ireland's net zero ambition no later than 2050\. Climate change is one of the greatest challenges facing humanity and it is widely accepted that rapid action is required to limit greenhouse gas (GHG) emissions to avert the worst consequences of climate change and protect both current and future generations. Through the rapid growth and development of renewable power, Ireland’s electricity system has been significantly decarbonised over the last decade, which had utilised much of the capacity for generation connections on the distribution system to date, with accelerating progress now needed to continue to build increased momentum towards achieving a net zero society. The scale of demand growth driven by electrification requires investment in capacity for demand connections, domestic, farming, industrial and commercial, across all voltage levels. ESB Networks’ role is to deliver the electricity distribution network that will empower all our customers to decarbonise their energy consumption. Our vision is to connect a clean electric future together and our customers and stakeholders will be at the heart of this transformation. We are hiring Powers System Engineers/Network Planners to join or Network Development Team in ESB Networks. Our Network Planners are responsible for planning the medium and long\-term reinforcement of the high voltage, medium voltage and low voltage distribution network to enable the Climate Action Plan targets and our Networks for Net Zero Strategy with a particular focus on the areas of electrification of heat and transport; connection of large demand customer; connection of renewables and development of policies and standards. The roles involve the power system analysis of the LV, MV, 38 kV and 110 kV distribution network to determine medium and long\-term strategic investment requirements, the optimum method of connection for new customers and interaction with various internal and external stakeholders. Network Planners work closely with other functions within ESB Networks in the design and delivery of network plans. The role requires engagement with customers, the transmission system operator, and other stakeholders to ensure development is well founded, and meets requirements. **Key Responsibilities*** Power System Analysis of the 110 kV, 38 kV, MV and LV distribution network and 220 kV bulk supply points in accordance with defined planning standards based on future scenarios. * Use load flow software (Synergi, PSS Sincal, etc.) to carry out studies to assess the impact of embedded generation, RES, flexibility, demand customer connections \& reinforcement options on the distribution system and to determine the Least Cost Technically Acceptable connection method. * Complete Study Documents, Investment Appraisals \& Technical Approvals as required and ensure that each CAPEX proposal has all supporting documentation for the business cases in place. * Ensure all connection applications are processed in line with CRU\-approved policies and ESBN Distribution Planning and Security of Supply Standards * Verify the accuracy and outputs of other team members studies in line with the agreed verification and approval procedure as appropriate. * Respond to customer queries and disputes on the proposed customer connection method as required. * Project manage innovation\-led distribution planning improvement projects from initial scoping and development of terms of reference through to implementation of the recommendations. * Preparation, development, and implementation of planning related standards, policies and tools as required. * Engage with a broad range of stakeholders within industry to listen to what is happening in other jurisdictions and what can be learned from best practice in other utilities. **Experience and Qualifications*** Strong technical capability. * 5\+ Years relevant experience required * Honours Degree in Electrical Engineering or equivalent work experience is essential. * Excellent communication and report writing skills. * Proven track record of delivery of projects on time and to the highest technical standards. * Self\-motivated with ability to project manage and prioritize own work. * Ability to work and share knowledge and competencies within a team structure. * Proven track record of success contributing to the training and development of technical professionals. * Ability to make quality decisions and judgments, with a logical, yet imaginative approach to problem solving. * Full clean drivers license * Minimum engineering experience of five years in the area of Power System Analysis with broad understanding of distribution network, working in any of the areas listed below, preferably including at least two years of experience at an advanced level (checking/verifying work of junior staff, training staff and developing work processes, tools and templates). Experience in two or more of these areas is an advantage, however applicants with specialised / expert knowledge in one area will also be considered. + System Planning and Grid Connection Studies + Transmission and Distribution System Master Plans + Power Quality Measurements and Studies + Development of Policies and Standards for Electricity Distribution System Planning **Desirable** ------------- * Prior experience in a Network Planning role * Can prepare Investment Appraisal documents setting out the drivers and methodology for assessing suitability and preference of technical and financial options using defined criteria. * Proven experience of analysing networks using modelling software to determine network parameters under various conditions. * Can perform financial analysis using lifetime cost projections for various connection proposals and network reinforcement proposals. * Experience in coding languages such as Python, SQL, etc. * Published Technical Papers at reputable international conferences. * Active involvement in international engineering organisations such as CIGRÉ, IEEE and IET (e.g. participation in Working Groups) * Experience of any of the following specialised power system studies software packages: PSS SINCAL, Synergi **Location** Location will be ESB Networks’ office in Leopardstown Road \- Dublin, Wilton – Cork, or Rosbrien – Limerick, but the role will require a certain amount of travel (often at short notice) both within and outside Ireland. Smart Working at ESB is designed to make ‘in\-person’ time together as purposeful as possible while also enabling the effective use of digital tools and hybrid arrangements to preserve our commitment to flexibility. The successful candidate may work flexibly within the jurisdiction where they have been employed, with attendance at the workplace for in\-person collaboration at least two days per week. All Smart Working arrangements are subject to regular review based on the operational requirements of the role, the team, and the business. **Reporting To** The successful candidate will report to one of the Network Development Team Leaders, who in turn report to the relevant manager in Network Development Group. **Why Work with Us?** * Opportunity to lead Ireland’s transition to net zero carbon future * Career development through mentoring and training * Corporate Social Responsibility Opportunities * Sports and Social Clubs * Networking opportunities * Credit Union * Generous Pension * Access to staff well\-being programmes * Generous parental leave entitlements * Strong values\-based and inclusive culture * Strong commitment to diversity, equity and inclusion * Great team environment working to our Core Values: Courageous, Caring, Driven and Trusted **Salary** €59,000 \- €70,000 per annum. **Closing Date** *19th of May, 2026* Your application will be held in reserve for 6 months should you be suitable for the role. **Diversity, Equity and Inclusion Statement** ESB is committed to being an equal opportunities employer. We welcome applications from all sections of society and ensure that no one is discriminated against on the grounds of race, religion or belief, ethnicity or nationality, disability, age, citizenship, marital status, domestic or civil partnership status, sexual orientation or gender identity, or any other basis as protected by law. Applicants who anticipate requiring assistance or reasonable accommodations for any part of the application or interview process may contact, in confidence, recruitment@esb.ie. **About ESB** Join us in our mission to achieve a net\-zero electricity system by 2040\. ESB is Ireland’s leading energy utility, with activities spanning electricity generation, transmission and distribution, energy supply, energy services and international consultancy. Operating in Ireland, Northern Ireland and Great Britain, we invest over €1 billion each year to deliver a new energy future based on reliable, affordable zero\-carbon electricity.

title: Pre-Sales Solutions Architect
description: **At Poka, we're transforming how frontline teams operate, learn, and solve problems.** **Our connected worker platform is purpose\-built for manufacturing, empowering frontline workers to share knowledge, collaborate in real\-time, and drive continuous improvement. Think of it as the missing digital link, bringing the human interface into the stack of the Smart Factory, generating the right data and insights that drive entirely new scales of process improvements.** **We're proud to support global leaders like Nestlé, Bosch, Mars, and Danone in their digital transformation journeys and as part of the IFS family, our reach and impact continue to grow globally.** ### **Architect the future of industrial enterprises \- where AI, data, and frontline operations truly connect.** We're looking for a Senior/Principal Solution Architect who can operate confidently from whiteboard to boardroom. This is a strategic, customer\-facing role where deep technical expertise meets business transformation, helping global enterprises simplify complex ecosystems, unlock the value of their data, and accelerate AI adoption. You'll engage directly with C\-level stakeholders across IT and business functions, shaping how organizations modernize their architecture and embed next\-generation capabilities like agentic systems and AI\-driven workflows into their operations. ### **What you'll be doing** **Designing enterprise architectures that scale and evolve** * Design end\-to\-end architectures that integrate seamlessly into complex ecosystems (ERP, MES, IoT, cloud, data platforms, etc.) * Define scalable, secure, and AI\-ready solutions aligned with long\-term business and technology strategies * Position how Poka simplifies, rationalizes, and enhances existing technology landscapes while enabling new capabilities **Leading executive conversations and strategic alignment** * Act as a trusted advisor to C\-level stakeholders (CIO, CTO, COO, CDO, CAIO) and enterprise architects * Translate architectural decisions into clear business outcomes \- ROI, efficiency gains, risk reduction, and agility * Lead executive workshops, value\-driven discussions, and architecture reviews * Help customers align technology investments with broader transformation goals **Driving AI and agentic system innovation** * Design architectures leveraging AI, agentic layers, and autonomous systems * Shape and apply agent\-to\-agent communication models and intelligent workflow orchestration * Turn emerging AI and distributed system concepts into practical, enterprise\-grade deployments * Guide customers in adopting AI responsibly, securely, and at scale **Connecting architecture to business value** * Link solution design decisions to operational performance, financial outcomes, and key business KPIs * Provide domain expertise across manufacturing, supply chain, and operations environments * Develop value\-driven reference architectures, reusable patterns, and solution blueprints **Collaborating to influence outcomes across the business** * Partner with Product Management and R\&D to influence roadmap based on customer and market needs * Support Sales and Customer Success teams in strategic deals and large\-scale transformation programs * Act as a bridge between technical teams and business stakeholders ### **What you bring** **Experience** * 15\+ years in industrial software, enterprise architecture, or solution engineering * Proven success working with global, top\-tier enterprise organizations * Extensive experience engaging and influencing C\-level stakeholders, including business leadership **Technical expertise** * Strong foundation in enterprise architecture (cloud, data, integrations, APIs, microservices) * Experience with industrial systems (MES, SCADA, IoT platforms, ERP integrations) * Solid understanding of AI/ML concepts, agentic architectures, or distributed intelligent systems **Business and communication skills** * Ability to translate complex technology into clear business value and strategic impact * Strong storytelling and executive communication skills * Deep understanding of how technology drives operational excellence, cost optimization, and revenue growth **Leadership and collaboration** * Experience working cross\-functionally with Product, Engineering, Sales, and executive stakeholders * Strong facilitation skills across workshops, co\-creation sessions, and executive engagements ### **What success looks like** * C\-level stakeholders clearly understand both the architectural fit and the business value of Poka * Solution architectures drive simplification, scalability, and measurable business outcomes * AI and agentic capabilities are embedded into core enterprise workflows * Strong credibility and trust established across both IT and business leadership * Demonstrable impact on customer efficiency, innovation, and strategic transformation ### **Why Poka** You won't just be designing architectures \- you'll be shaping how some of the world's largest manufacturers \& industrial companies think about AI, data, and the future of work. This role sits at the center of innovation across Poka and IFS, with real influence on product direction, customer strategy, and how agentic systems come to life in industrial environments. If you're motivated by complexity, executive impact, and building what's next, this is where you can do it at scale. **What we offer:** * Dynamic and collaborative work environment. * Generous vacation policy upon arrival. * Remote work policy. * A truly flexible schedule that allows you to adapt your work hours. * Benefits Plan offered from day one. * MacBook Pro and €500 allowance for your home office. * Generous referral bonus. * The tools, support, and opportunities you need to keep learning and progressing in your career. *Want to learn more about our culture? Click* *HERE**.* **Please note:** An understanding of English is essential at Poka, as it is the language used during company\-wide meetings and many internal communications, ensuring effective collaboration and decision\-making. In addition, as Poka operates in an international environment, English is the primary language for communicating with our clients and partners, enabling us to better serve them and represent Poka in a professional manner.
