title: Strategic Finance Analyst
description: As a Strategic Finance Analyst, you will be an active contributor within Strategic Finance, partnering with Enterprise and regional stakeholders to support financial planning, performance analysis, and decision\-making. Reporting to the Senior Manager, Strategic Finance, you will independently own defined areas of analysis and planning, applying sound judgment to translate business activity into actionable financial insights. We are looking for candidates who demonstrate strength across the following: Strategic Thinking, **Data \& Insight, People \& Influence and Tactical Execution** This role is based in our Dublin City Centre office and will be based on site 2 days a week. **Responsibilities** **Business Partnering \& Influence** * Build effective working relationships with stakeholders across Enterprise functions * Partner with business leaders to support financial planning and decision\-making within your coverage area * Communicate insights clearly and influence decisions through structured, data\-driven recommendations **Financial Planning \& Ownership** * Own financial models, forecasts, and KPI tracking for assigned areas * Support the annual planning process (SPF), including budgeting and forecasting across global functions * Deliver accurate and timely reporting, identifying opportunities to improve processes and outputs **Analysis \& Insight** * Translate business performance into financial insights, identifying key drivers, risks, and opportunities * Apply analytical rigor to validate data, assess trends, and improve the quality of reporting * Present insights in a clear and compelling way to stakeholders at multiple levels **Strategic Thinking \& Problem Solving** * Apply business acumen to connect financial results to broader business strategy * Identify problems and opportunities within your coverage area and propose actionable solutions * Select and apply appropriate analytical approaches based on context and business needs **Cross\-functional Collaboration** * Work closely with global and regional finance teams to align on assumptions, reporting, and planning approaches * Contribute to team initiatives aimed at improving tools, processes, and financial capabilities **Required Qualifications** * 3\+ years experience in Strategic Finance, FP\&A, or Finance Business Partnering * Bachelor’s degree in Finance, Accounting, Economics, or related field * ACA/ACCA (or equivalent) with relevant post\-qualification experience * Strong financial modeling and analytical skills * Ability to work independently within defined areas and manage multiple priorities * Demonstrated ability to communicate insights clearly and work effectively with stakeholders * Ability to translate business activity into structured financial analysis and recommendations **Desired Qualifications** * Experience with finance systems (e.g., OneStream, Oracle, Hyperion) * Experience improving reporting, planning processes, or financial tooling * Exposure to working in a global or cross\-functional environment For this role, you’ll find success through craft expertise, a collaborative spirit, and decision\-making that prioritizes your fellow Rioters, who are the customers of your work. Being a dedicated fan of games is not necessary for this position! At Riot Games, we put players first. That mission drives every decision in our quest to create games and experiences that make it better to be a player. Whether you’re working directly on a new player\-facing experience or you’re supporting the company as a whole, everyone at Riot is part of our mission. And just like in our games, we’re better when we work together. Our goal is to create collaborative teams where you are empowered to bring your unique perspective everyday. If that sounds like the kind of place you want to work, we’re looking forward to your application.

title: Summer Logistics & Transport Administrator (Part-Time)
description: **Great opportunity for students in Logistics / Supply Chain** **!!! Please note: this role is specifically suited to candidates studying or experienced in Logistics, Supply Chain or Transport Operations. This is NOT a general administration position. !!!** **About Us** Global Fruit Company Ltd. is a well\-established fresh produce supplier based in Dublin 11, operating within a fast\-paced international supply chain environment. We work closely with growers, suppliers, and transport partners to deliver fresh products efficiently across Ireland. **The Opportunity** We are looking for a motivated student or recent graduate to join our transport team for the summer. This is an excellent opportunity to gain hands\-on experience in a real logistics operation, working closely with a small, experienced team. You will be exposed to the day\-to\-day running of transport planning, supplier coordination, and administrative processes within a supply chain business. **What You’ll Be Doing** * Supporting transport operations with day\-to\-day administrative tasks * Processing and checking transport invoices * Updating collection schedules and transport plans * Preparing and organising transport documentation * Communicating with drivers and suppliers * Assisting the team during busy operational periods **What You’ll Gain** * Practical experience in transport and logistics operations * Exposure to real\-world supply chain processes * Opportunity to work closely with an experienced team * Insight into planning, coordination, and documentation in logistics * Potential for future opportunities within the business **What We’re Looking For** * Currently studying (or recently completed) logistics, supply chain, or a related field * Strong attention to detail and organisational skills * Comfortable using Excel and general office systems * Reliable, proactive, and willing to learn * Good communication skills **Working Pattern** 20–25 hours per week 4 days a week (including Friday – essential), 5\-6 h per day **Contract Duration** May to August 2026 Possibility to extend depending on performance and business needs **If you’re looking to gain valuable experience in logistics and supply chain this summer, we’d love to hear from you.** Job Types: Part\-time, Fixed term Contract length: 4 months Pay: From €14\.15 per hour Expected hours: 20\.0 – 25\.0 per week Benefits: * Employee assistance program * On\-site parking * Sick pay Work Location: In person

title: Systems Administrator
description: **🚀 Elevate Your Career – Become Our Cyber Security IT Systems Administrator!** Are you ready to lead the charge in securing and scaling enterprise infrastructure? We’re looking for a **technical powerhouse** to drive security‑driven projects in a mission‑critical 24/7 environment. This is your chance to shape the future of IT operations while working at the cutting edge of Microsoft technologies. **What You’ll Do:** * Take the reins as **technical lead** on cyber security infrastructure initiatives. * Design, build, and harden complex Microsoft environments with resilience and scalability in mind. * Own **vulnerability management \& patching** to keep systems airtight. * Implement **high‑availability \& disaster recovery strategies** for critical applications. * Mentor junior staff and collaborate across teams to deliver secure, high‑performing solutions. * Drive continuous improvement with automation, monitoring, and failover planning. **What You Bring:** * 5\+ years senior experience in Microsoft enterprise environments. * Certifications like **MCSA, MCP, CCNA** – plus **CompTIA Security\+** or Microsoft Security Associate. * Mastery of **Windows Server 2019/2022/2025** , **Active Directory \& Group Policy** , and network protocols. * Strong skills in backup solutions (NetBackup bonus), VMware, storage, and Linux (desirable). * Proven ability to solve problems, document processes, and thrive under ITIL‑driven methodologies. * Bonus points for automation chops with **Python, Ansible, REST APIs** . **Why Join Us?** You’ll be the **bridge between Cyber Security and IT Operations** , shaping infrastructure that meets business goals, regulatory expectations, and service‑level demands. This is more than a role—it’s a chance to **lead, innovate, and protect** at scale.

title: TEAM MEMBER
description: At Sprout, our Team Members are the heart of what we do. You're the face of the company — the one customers come back to see, as much as they come back for the food. From delivering warm, human interactions to helping the team keep the place humming, this is a role for someone who cares about people, pace, and good food. This is more than just a job — it’s a chance to grow, have fun, and be a BIG part of a close\-knit team that loves what they do. **Reporting to:** General Manager **Contract:** Full\-time and part\-time available **Pay:** €14\.15 per hour ****What You’ll Be Doing**** * Versatile Team Player – You’ll jump into different parts of the day, from helping prep fresh ingredients to setting up the service line and keeping the restaurant tidy. * Hospitality Heartbeat – Bring good energy to every interaction and make customers feel genuinely welcome and looked after. * Restaurant Ready – Keep the place sparkling clean, safe, and set up for success throughout the day. * All\-Rounder – Get hands\-on experience across our service line: till, toppings, mix – you’ll learn it all. * Detail\-Oriented – You’ll follow checklists to make sure every dish looks and tastes great. * Menu Confident – Learn our menu so you can guide customers and recommend dishes with confidence. * Safety Champion – Help the team stay on top of food safety, hygiene, and health \& safety standards. ****Who You Are**** * Hospitable – You love making people smile and creating good energy. * Polite \& Kind – Holds the door, jumps in to help, and treats people with respect. * Motivated – You care about doing a good job, no matter what the task is. * Curious – You’re eager to learn and grow. You ask questions and don’t pretend to know it all. * Positive Energy (Oomph!) – You bring great energy into the room. Excited to be here. * Team Player – You’re happiest when you’re helping others. You believe more joy comes from giving than getting. * Values\-Driven – You show up with a good attitude and live the Sprout values. ****What We’re Looking For**** * You don’t need experience for this role, we’ll teach you everything you need to know. What matters most is your attitude and willingness to learn. Here’s what we’re looking for: * Good Level of English \- You can understand instructions and communicate confidently with customers and the team. * Curious \& Willing to Learn – You take feedback well and are keen to grow. * Reliable – You turn up on time and take pride in doing a good job. ****Why Work at Sprout?**** * Great food for you on every shift * Growth opportunities \- we love to promote from within * Team discount across all locations * Well\-being and mental health support * Regular team socials and celebrations * A fun, friendly team that genuinely care. If you’re someone who thrives in a team, loves a bit of buzz, and wants to grow in a company that values people and positivity, we’d love to hear from you.

title: Tactical Communications Co-Founder / COO (100 % remote) (m/f/d)
description: We are looking to hire ambitious entrepreneurs to start and scale their own startups. We are serial entrepreneurs, for example Paul Müller (founder Adjust, €1\.2B exit) and Petter Made (founder SumUp, €8B) who are eager to support outlier personalities and serial entrepreneurs to build €1B\+ companies. Our offer: * A salary while you build your startup as you will directly be employed by us. Alternatively, you can opt for up to €500k in funding. * 1:1 sparring with unicorn founders on a weekly basis * Community: Access to the top 0\.1% of founders, peers and investors * Team building: Hiring top notch talent supported through our network (over 50,000 professionals) * Distribution: Support in reaching product\-market\-fit and building up a sales force / marketing machine * Funding support for securing a multi\-million euro funding round within 12 months (on average, EWOR Fellows raise \> €2M after our Grand Pitch) One of our fellows set a record for Europe’s largest pre\-seed round by a first\-time founder, securing a €12M pre\-seed investment. **Tasks** * You will own, build, and run your startup in fields such as Tactical Communications * You will embark on an extensive personal development journey crafted by unicorn founders and follow a fully customised programme enhancing your goal, time, and energy management * You will receive support in hiring through our network to over 50,000 professionals and advice as well as best practices from serial entrepreneurs * You will receive intensive coaching to make your startup ready to raise millions in funding * You will iterate your product with us until having reached product\-market\-fit and receive support in building up a sales force or creating a marketing engine respectively **Requirements** * You are based in Europe or the Americas or open to relocate * You are willing to take full responsibility for your own startup and scale it to €100M\+ in revenues * You have excellent communication skills in the English language Join us and build a €1B\+ company with us!

title: Technical Business Analyst – 6 Month Contract
description: **Technical Business Analyst – 6 Month Contract, Hybrid, Dublin** **What’s involved** * Analyse and document end\-to\-end data flows across SAP S/4HANA reporting and downstream systems * Review and validate interfaces, data mappings, and reconciliation processes * Work closely with finance, reporting, and IT teams to define clear functional and technical requirements * Identify data integrity issues, breaks in reporting flows, and process inefficiencies * Support delivery across multiple change initiatives, ensuring alignment between business needs and technical solutions * Contribute to Agile ceremonies (sprint planning, backlog refinement, stand\-ups) and manage requirements through delivery lifecycle **What’s needed** * Banking or financial services experience (essential) * Technical BA background with strong experience in data flows, interfaces, and system integrations * Hands\-on experience with SAP S/4HANA, particularly reporting and finance data structures * Strong understanding of reconciliations, controls, and data quality in financial environments * Proven experience working in Agile delivery environments * Ability to engage stakeholders, challenge requirements, and translate business needs into clear technical outputs **What’s on offer:** * Competitive Daily Rate Vantage Resources is an equal opportunity employer and is committed to creating a diverse working environment by recruiting people from different backgrounds and cultures. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for engagement and/or employment without regard to race, color, religion, gender, gender identity sexual orientation, national origin, culture, disability or age. An inclusive and diverse workforce is an essential part of the development of our organisation’s culture which we believe enhances both our working environment and the service we provide to our customers.

title: Technical Consultant
description: When you join KPMG Ireland, you are joining a community of over 4,200 employees who collectively work together, both in person and virtually, to achieve their full potential. We are known as an organisation who want you to come as you are and do work that truly matters. Joining KPMG Ireland is an opportunity to enhance your career, not only in the work that you enjoy doing, but equally through the global connections that you make, the lifelong learning that you can accomplish and the chance to make your mark. Are you our next **Technical Consultant?** **Location: Dublin** **Grade: Senior Technology Consultants at Manager and Senior Consultant level** **Contract type: Perm** **Role** We are currently seeking to hire **Senior Technology Consultants at Manager and Senior Consultant level** who will join our Management Consulting practice on a permanent basis. The role demands that the individual be capable of advising client organisations on a wide range of IT topics. On a day\-to\-day basis our Technology Consultants will work with our clients delivering, architecting and designing innovative, flexible and cost\-effective technology and infrastructure capabilities. **We ideally need you to have some of the following experience:** * Experience across some or a range of Technology Transformation areas such as IT and Digital Strategy, Project Management, IT Architecture, Solution Design \& Development, Testing, IT Operating models and Cost Management * Ability to think and engage both “big picture” and detailed levels * Driven to learn, innovate, and integrate new thinking or the latest technology trends in everything you do * Experience using the standard tools and techniques for research, analysis, and problem solving * Highly adept developing requirements, producing reports, completing assessments, or supporting technology transformation and delivery initiatives * Driven to find new efficiency and value\-add opportunities through detailed analysis * Experience of modern Digital techniques such as DevOps etc. **Responsibilities** As a Technology Consulting Practitioner, you’ll help our clients transform their technology landscape. You will work alongside Business and IT stakeholders, leading the planning, design and implementation of key IT Change Programmes and advise clients on how to maximise the efficiency and effectiveness of their IT operations. **Examples Of Projects Our Technology Consultants Work On Are** * Defining and delivering clients IT strategies and IT operating models * Assisting clients by leading technology transformation programmes/projects * Assisting clients on the recovery from major incidents such a Cyber Ransomware attacks * Help clients define their IT sourcing strategies and advise on appropriate services and service level regimes * Advising and implementing efficient and effective IT governance and operations processes e.g. Agile and Traditional SDLC, ITIL, COBIT etc, * Helping clients navigate its journey into the cloud (IAAS, SAAS, PAAS) * Advising clients on the modernisation of legacy IT platforms and End User environments * Advising our clients on emerging technologies such as Artificial Intelligence, Robotics, Analytics and Blockchain. **Your Team** **Discover How KPMG Can Enrich Your Life Beyond Your Career** **Benefits Tailored For You** At KPMG, we understand that life is multifaceted. That’s why we offer a flexible benefits package designed to support your unique lifestyle. Our benefits include: * Discounted Health Insurance: Prioritise your well\-being with our health insurance options. * Additional Annual Leave: Purchase extra leave to spend more time with friends and family or to travel. * Enhanced Family Leave: Enjoy more time with your family with our generous leave options. * Emergency Childcare Cover: For times when your regular childcare plans fall through * Social Events: Connect and network with colleagues through our engaging social events. * Early Finish Fridays: Start your weekends early on bank holidays and during the summer. * Relocation Assistance: Smooth your transition if you’re moving to Ireland. * Learning \& Development: Access our extensive supports, including LinkedIn Learning, to grow your skills. We also offer a variety of flexible working options to help you balance your professional and personal life. **Career Progression with Continuous Support** From the moment you join KPMG, you’ll receive dedicated support from your performance manager to enhance your skills. Our award\-winning Business School provides continuous learning opportunities to deepen your expertise. We stand out as the only Big Four firm to announce promotions four times a year, offering you frequent opportunities for career advancement. **Commitment to Inclusion \& Diversity** Inclusion, diversity, and equity are at the heart of KPMG. We celebrate diverse backgrounds, experiences, and perspectives, and we’re committed to fostering an inclusive and equitable environment at every level. Our goal is to attract and retain the best talent by building a culture where everyone can achieve their potential. Learn more about our commitment here \- https://kpmg.com/ie/en/home/about/inclusion\-diversity\-equity.html **Ready to Join Us?** If you meet most of the criteria, we encourage you to apply by submitting your CV. KPMG is an equal opportunities employer, welcoming applications from people of all backgrounds. We are happy to provide reasonable accommodations during the recruitment process if needed.Necessary arrangements can be made at interview stage to accommodate the needs of candidates with disabilities. For any specific queries about the role, please contact Michelle Breen For a glimpse into life at KPMG, follow \#ExperienceKPMG on LinkedIn, Instagram, TikTok, and X. **Please note that agency assistance is not required for this position.** By clicking apply to this role your application will not only be considered for this position, but your details will be stored on our database and may be considered for similar future positions that may arise. This means that the personal data contained in your application and provided to KPMG as part of the application process may be retained by us for up to 18 months from the date of your application and you hereby agree to that. If you do not wish your details to be stored on our recruitment database, please email careers@kpmg.ie stating this and we will ensure that such information is not stored other than for the purposes of this application.

title: Technical Revenue Operations Analyst
description: 📝 **Company Mission** *Our mission is to give the world access to the best products by empowering great businesses to reach their growth potential.* 💼 **About Wayflyer** Today's SMBs need a capital provider that keeps pace with their growth ambitions. Traditional financing options are slow, cumbersome and often out of reach. That's why we built Wayflyer. Our unique technology allows us to assess businesses in minutes, generate financing offers that reflect their growth potential and send funds in as little as 24 hours. Since launching in April 2020, we've deployed over $5bn to thousands of businesses worldwide, backed by Tier 1 banks like J.P. Morgan. We've become a trusted financing partner for some amazing brands, like True Classic, Little Words Project and Kekoa Foods. Teams at Wayflyer are truly cross\-functional. You'll be collaborating with ambitious colleagues from around the world \- all striving to deliver on a huge opportunity. Check out this video to hear directly from them. 💛 **Culture \& Values at Wayflyer** At Wayflyer, we value being sound people, excellent operators, and ambitious overachievers, working together with integrity, creativity, and bold optimism to deliver exceptional results. To learn more, please visit our website. 🚀 **Your Role at a Glance:** *The key ways you'll bring value to the team* * Integrations \& Technical Ownership: you'll build, maintain and enhance systems, internal automations \& workflows to increase sales rep productivity and effectiveness. * Lead Routing: Take ownership of lead routing including maintaining and optimizing lead allocation, enrichment, and proactively identifying inefficiencies in the inbound lead flow. * Data Analytics \& Problem Solving: You will use self\-sourced data to analyse specific process performance, conduct root cause analysis to propose and build solutions. You will measure results against existing KPIs and embed a continuous improvement mentality in your work. * Cross\-Functional Collaboration \& Project Ownership: You will collaborate actively with teams such as Sales, Partnerships, Marketing and Finance to gather requirements and build solutions that will improve overall efficiency and productivity. * Support revenue data hygiene, including enrichment, and process improvements to ensure data integrity across systems. * Driving Operational Excellence: This is a high impact role where you'll have the opportunity to support the implementation and scaling of automated solutions. **🔨 What Makes You a Great Fit** * 2\+ years of experience in RevOps, MarOps, consultancy or similar roles * Excellent analytical skills and solid technical fluency, with the ability to convey complex requirements between technical and non\-technical teams. Comfortable with Excel, GSheet, SQL or Python (Pandas, Numpy, Seabourn, MatPlotLib) * Exposure to automation platforms (Zapier, Pipedream, n8n, UIPath) would be highly beneficial * Experience configuring and administering HubSpot and other GTM\-function technologies, including CPQ (Configure, Price, Quote), CLM (Contract Lifecycle Management), sales enablement, engagement \& orchestration, and customer\-success platforms would also be desirable * Exceptional verbal and written communication skills to drive adoption, training, and change management initiatives. * Robust problem\-solving capabilities and comfort navigating ambiguity, evidenced by a history of delivering successful outcomes in complex, fast\-paced environments. * Highly organised with strong project management skills and the ability to coordinate cross functionally. 🤝 **What to Expect After You Apply** You can expect to hear from a member of our Talent Acquisition team soon. If we believe you're a good fit, the first step will be a conversation with a recruiter who will guide you through the next stages of the process. The full interview process, which includes meeting the Hiring Manager and completing a case study or interview loop, typically takes approximately 3\-4 weeks. 🏖️ **The Perks of Being at Wayflyer** 🌴 **Time Off That Matters** Recharge with 25 days of paid annual leave, plus public holidays. 🧘 **Your Wellbeing** Access support and resources to support your wellbeing, including our *Wellbeing @ Wayflyer* ERG. 🫀 **Comprehensive Health \& Protection** Stay covered with private healthcare, life insurance, and critical illness cover. 👵 **Secure Your Future with Our Pension Plan** Our pension plan helps you build a strong foundation for tomorrow, starting today. 🍼 **Family\-First Policies** We provide a generous parental and adoptive leave program for both Primary and Secondary caregivers, ensuring you have the dedicated, fully paid time you need to focus on your family. 💰 **Share in Our Success** With our equity scheme, you're not just an employee; you're a stakeholder in our journey. 🌴 **Work From Abroad!** Enjoy the freedom to work remotely from anywhere in the world for up to 60 calendar days a year. **📍Global Offices and Working Policy** 🇮🇪 **Dublin, Our Buzzing HQ** Our headquarters operates a hybrid model. The number of in\-office days depends on your team, but the majority of teams and our SLT are typically in 3\-4 days per week, giving you valuable exposure to senior leadership. 🇬🇧 **London, One Big Family** Home to around 30 employees, our brand\-new London office also runs on a hybrid model. You’ll often find our CEO working alongside the team with regular visits. 🇦🇺 **Sydney, Small but Mighty** Our close\-knit Sydney office has around 20 employees and follows a flexible hybrid model, fostering strong collaboration. **🇺🇸 Charlotte, our new US hub** We’ve opened our doors in the heart of Uptown Charlotte, with a brand new space designed to foster in\-person collaboration, while supporting hybrid flexibility. 🌍 **Remote, Across the US \& Europe** The majority of our Tech org works remotely across the UK and Europe, coming together throughout the year for off\-sites, company kick off and technical kick off. We also have a growing number of remote team members across the US. 💡 **How We Handle Your Personal Data** By submitting your application, you acknowledge that Wayflyer Limited will process your personal data for the purpose of evaluating your suitability for the role. Such processing is based on the need to take steps prior to entering into a potential employment agreement. To learn more about how we handle your personal data, you can contact our privacy team at privacy@wayflyer.com or review our privacy notice at https://wayflyer.com/privacy\-notice.

title: Trainee Industrial Spray Painter
description: **Company Profile:** Suretank Group has been one of the leading Off\-Site Manufacturing Organisation (OSM) for over 30 years, offering solutions into the Offshore sector to include Tanks \& Specialist Containers and Modular Electrical and Plantroom Solutions into the Data Centre and Life science Sectors. Our target sectors include the Energy, Marine, Infrastructure \& Industrial sectors. Established in 1995, Suretank Group operates from its headquarters in Ireland, with additional manufacturing facilities in the UK, USA, Poland and other global locations. As we continue to expand, we are committed to delivering excellence and innovation while fostering a culture of collaboration and growth. **Job Summary:** The Trainee Industrial Spray Painter will support the day\-to\-day activities of the Finishing Department while developing practical skills and experience as an industrial spray painting. **Key Responsibilities:** * Assist with preparation of surfaces prior to painting, including cleaning, masking, and sanding. * Support the application of paint and coatings under supervision, ensuring quality standards are met. * Follow all Health \& Safety, Quality, and Environmental procedures in line with company and ISO standards. * Assist in the handling, storage, and organisation of paints and materials, ensuring they are stored correctly when not in use. * Maintain cleanliness and organisation of the Finishing Department work area at all times. * Ensure compliance with all relevant regulations and ISO standards for Health \& Safety, Quality and Environmental. * Support basic paint tasks such as touch\-ups and completion of test plates under guidance. * Assist with updating internal systems (e.g. SAP) and completing relevant documentation as required. * Work closely with team members to ensure project deadlines are met. * Follow instructions from supervisors and senior team members to complete tasks efficiently. * Assist with basic maintenance and cleaning of painting equipment. * All paints stored in correct manner when not in use. * Touch \-up of units where required. * Paint Boards updated on a daily basis. * Waste products noted and if required, collection of waste product organised and informed to production management. * All paperwork/paint reports completed in a timely manner and in accordance with ISO standards and procedures of work. * Support other departments when required, particularly during quieter periods in the Finishing Department. * Participate in training and actively develop knowledge of painting processes, tools, and materials. **Candidate Skills:** * Willingness to learn and develop practical skills. * Good attention to detail and ability to follow instructions accurately. * Strong work ethic with a positive and proactive attitude. * Good communication and teamwork skills. * Basic awareness of Health \& Safety practices * Flexibility to work overtime when required. * Reliability and good timekeeping. **Company Benefits:** * Career development opportunities * Income protection * Death in service cover * Employee referral bonus program * Employee Assistance Program (EAP) * Free on\-site parking

title: Trainee Service Advisor
description: Colm Quinn BMW is seeking a motivated and customer\-focused **Trainee Service Advisor** to join our busy Aftersales Department. This is an excellent opportunity for someone looking to build a career within the motor industry, gaining hands\-on experience at the heart of the customer journey. As a Trainee Service Advisor, you will support our experienced Service Advisors in delivering a seamless, professional, and premium service experience to our customers. You will develop your skills in customer engagement, service coordination, and workshop operations while upholding the high standards associated with both the BMW and Colm Quinn brands. **Key Responsibilities:** * Assist in welcoming customers to the Aftersales Department and supporting initial queries. * Support the coordination of vehicle service bookings, repairs, and customer follow\-ups. * Learn to provide clear and accurate updates to customers regarding service progress and timelines. * Work closely with Service Advisors, technicians, and workshop controllers to ensure efficient workflow. * Maintain accurate records and assist with administrative duties in line with dealership standards. * Identify opportunities to enhance the customer experience under guidance from senior team members. **What We’re Looking For:** * Previous experience in customer service, retail is advantageous but not essential. * A strong interest in the motor industry and desire to build a career in Aftersales. * Excellent communication and interpersonal skills with a customer\-first approach. * Strong organisational skills and attention to detail. * A proactive attitude with willingness to learn and grow within a team environment. * Passion for delivering a premium service aligned with the BMW brand. **What We Offer:** * A competitive entry\-level remuneration package. * Structured training and clear career progression into a Service Advisor role. * The opportunity to work with a prestige automotive brand in a modern, award\-winning facility. * A supportive team environment within a growing and dynamic motor retail group. If you are dedicated to customer satisfaction, enjoy working as part of a busy team, and take pride in representing a premium brand, we’d love to hear from you. Job Types: Full\-time, Permanent Work Location: In person

title: Transport Planner
description: **Working in partnership with M\&S, at Gist we deliver industry\-leading logistics and supply chain transformation.** **We value, celebrate, and believe in the power of individuality and strive to create a work environment where everyone can be themselves. We want you to join us, bring your authentic self to work and unlock your full potential.** As a **Transport Planner** you'll play a key role at the centre of our operation, where every action you take contributes to our customers enjoying their favourite M\&S food. Our operation in **Clonshaugh** are looking for candidates who can commit to a **Sunday\-Thursday shift pattern, working 40 hours per week between the hours of either 07:00 to 15:00 or 09:00 to 17:00\.** **What’s in it for you?** Gist is an inclusive, dynamic, exciting, and evolving business built on our core values. Here are some of the benefits we offer that make working for Gist just that little bit more special… * 43,589\.76 per annum * 20% M\&S discount on most things from furniture, fashion and food * Discounted rates on healthcare cash plan * Auto Enrolment Pension or competitive Defined Contribution Pension Scheme and life assurance (up to 4 times salary) * Amazing perks and discounts via our App to major restaurants and retailers * Access to a wide range of wellbeing support – including our Employee Assistance Programme * A first\-class welcome with a tailored induction and a wide range of training schemes to help with your learning and development * Cycle to work scheme * Supportive Team: Join a close\-knit community where collaboration is key **What Will You Do \& What Will You Need** As part of your role, you will work with customers to meet current and anticipated requirements to enable the Site to achieve service expectations. Address customer concerns immediately and escalate where appropriate in a timely manner. Manage transport resources in line with operational and budgetary requirements including shift rota management to maintain service levels. Plan and manage operational resources to optimise the utilisation of labour and equipment to effectively manage forecasted volumes. Ensure effective communication links between colleagues and other shifts to ensure operational consistency and drive high standards and flag opportunities for process improvement. Ideally, you will have previous experience of working in a multi customer centric operation and planning operational resources. In depth knowledge of drivers’ rules, regulations and WTD and have a national/international CPC. **Who Are We And Where Will You Be** At Gist we are united by our core values; with safety and people at the heart of everything do, we are progressive, accountable, collaborative, and ethical. Gist will play an integral part in the M\&S journey as it pushes boundaries to lead the retail industry into a greener, speedier, more inspiring era. **Apply today and Join us on the road to success** Upon application if successful, you will be contacted by a member of the team and booked onto an initial introduction call to discuss the role at hand and next steps. We reserve the right to close a vacancy before the closing date in the event of an overwhelming response or a change in business priorities. Gist aims to be an inclusive organisation, trusted and admired by our colleagues, customers, and suppliers. If you consider yourself to require reasonable adjustments to any part of our recruitment process, we invite you to share those requirements with us when completing your application. We will make every effort to ensure your needs are met to provide a fair and transparent process of assessment. If it’s meaningful to you, it’s important to us.

title: Turn Your Experience Into a Recruitment Career

title: UKI Business Consulting - Capital Projects - Infrastructure Controls Specialist, All Levels
description: **Job Title:** UKI Business Consulting \- Infrastructure \& Capital Projects \- Infrastructure Controls Specialist, All Levels * **Location:** Ireland * **Available for Work Visa Sponsorship:** No * **Business Area:** UKI Business Consulting * **Contract Type:** Full\-Time – Permanent **The team and the opportunity** To continue the successful growth of our Infrastructure \& Capital Projects team within EY’s Consulting practice, we are looking for experienced and motivated Project Controls Specialists with a background in delivering complex, high‑value capital programmes. The successful candidate will be responsible for overseeing and managing all aspects of project controls within the infrastructure sector, ensuring projects are delivered on time, within budget, and in line with quality and governance requirements. This role requires meticulous attention to detail, strong analytical capability, and the ability to communicate and collaborate effectively with a broad range of stakeholders. Our Infrastructure \& Capital Projects (I\&CP) team sits within the Transformation Delivery competency of EY’s Irish Consulting practice. Our I\&CP team is committed to delivering exceptional client services across a range of sectors including energy, utilities, transport, water, gas, telecoms, housing, and healthcare. Our ambition is to become the Trusted Advisor for our clients in the procurement, construction, and operational phases of major capital programmes by leveraging: * Our people and their extensive industry experience * Existing partnerships and relationships * Our global EY experience across the Infrastructure \& Capital projects lifecycle lifecycle **Your key responsibilities** To support the continued growth of EY’s I\&CP business, we are currently recruiting Project Controls Specialists with an engineering, technical, or construction‑related background. * Work directly with EY’s infrastructure clients across our focus sectors (energy, water, transport, gas, telecoms, housing, and healthcare) to develop innovative insights and solutions to meet their needs. * Develop and implement project control processes and procedures to effectively manage scope, schedule, cost, and quality. * Collaborate with project managers, engineers, contractors, and other stakeholders to establish project baselines and monitor performance against established metrics. * Create and maintain detailed project schedules, including critical path analysis and resource allocation, to optimise project timelines and identify potential risks and opportunities. * Monitor project costs and expenditures, track budget variances, and provide timely analysis and reporting to support informed decision\-making. * Identify and mitigate project risks through proactive risk assessment, analysis, and mitigation strategies. * Lead or support regular progress meetings and presentations to update project stakeholders on project status, key milestones, and performance metrics. * Utilise project management systems and tools to enhance project controls processes and enhance project visibility and transparency. * Provide mentorship and guidance to junior project controls staff, fostering a culture of continuous improvement and professional development within the team. * Support the preparation of project proposals, bids, and cost estimates by providing accurate and reliable project controls data and analysis. * Ensure compliance with relevant industry standards, regulations, and best practices in project controls and management. **Internal Responsibilities** * Support client account management activities, including project resourcing, financial management, and quality and risk management. * Build and maintain strong relationships with clients and EY colleagues to support business develop across EY. * Communicate effectively with EY senior management and support the development, management and motivation of high\-performing teams. * Develop a strong understanding of EY service offerings and proactively identify opportunities to enhance client value. * Support team development activities, including: + Coaching, and mentoring. + Conducting performance reviews and contributing to feedback. + Supporting recruitment, retention and training initiatives. + Maintaining continuous technical and professional development. + Understanding, applying, and communicating workplace policies and procedures **To qualify for the role, you must have** * 3rd level educational qualification in Engineering, Construction, or a related field. * Minimum of 5 years of experience in project controls or project management within the infrastructure sector, with a proven track record of supporting or managing large\-scale programmes. * Ideal candidates will have:\- Experience in consulting or similar client facing role. + Multidisciplinary experience including project management and engineering feasibility and design. + Excellent stakeholder management and communication skills with a track record in managing complex programmes. + Strong analytical and problem\-solving skills, with the ability to analyse complex data sets and develop actionable insights. + Excellent communication and interpersonal skills, with the ability to effectively collaborate with diverse stakeholders at all levels. + Demonstrated leadership capability with a proactive, delivery‑focused approach. + Knowledge of industry\-specific regulations, standards, and best practices in infrastructure development and construction. + Additional professional qualifications, such as PRINCE2® or PMP, are desirable. **What working at EY offers** We offer a competitive remuneration package. Our comprehensive Total Rewards package includes support for flexible working and career development, and with FlexEY you can select benefits that suit your needs, covering holidays, health and well\-being, insurance, savings and a wide range of discounts, offers and promotions. Plus, we offer: * Support and coaching from some of the most engaging colleagues around * Opportunities to develop new skills and progress your career * The freedom and flexibility to handle your role in a way that’s right for you All our employees are given a benefits package which they can tailor to suit their individual preferences. Our range of benefits include: * Pension * Maternity \& Paternity leave * Discounted health insurance * Bike to work Scheme * Web Doctor \- Free unlimited online GP consultations for you and your family * Recognition Awards * The purchase of additional annual leave * Cash incentives for referrals * Hybrid Working * Work Mobile * Free Gym membership * TECH MBA paid by EY * Travel Pass * Wellness rooms Available in some offices EY is committed to being an inclusive employer and we are happy to consider flexible working arrangements. We strive to achieve the right balance for our people, enabling us to deliver excellent client service whilst allowing you to build your career without sacrificing your personal priorities. While our client\-facing professionals can be required to travel regularly, and at times be based at client sites, our flexible working arrangements can help you to achieve a lifestyle balance. **Career Progression** * When you join EY, you will be supported to ensure that you are enhancing your skills from day one. * Continuous learning, where you can develop the mindset and skills to navigate whatever comes next. * As you grow and develop here, you’ll discover opportunities to help customise your career journey, so that it’s as unique as you are \- success is defined by you, we will provide the tools and flexibility, so you can make a meaningful impact, your way. * Transformative leadership, we will give you the insights, coaching and confidence to be the leader the world needs. * Diverse and inclusive culture, you will be embraced for who you are and empowered to use your voice to help others find theirs. * We have embraced Hybrid working at EY adding greater flexibility and autonomy to the roles of our employees. **Inclusion \& Diversity** We hold a collective commitment to foster an environment where all differences are valued and respected, practices are equitable and everyone experiences a sense of belonging: Inclusion, diversity, and equity are part of who we are at EY. We believe that the highest\-performing teams maximize the power of different perspectives and backgrounds. These teams are both diverse and inclusive and are willing to invite and learn from other perspectives. Our ability to include various viewpoints into our mindsets, behaviours and operations is fundamental to driving innovation, building strong relationships, and delivering the best solutions for our clients. We recognise the strength that comes from having a diverse workforce and building a culture where we support all our people to achieve their potential. You’ll be embraced for who you are and empowered to use your voice to help others find theirs. As an equal opportunities’ employer, we welcome applications from people of all backgrounds. Reasonable accommodations are offered at every stage of our recruitment process. To help create an equitable and inclusive experience during the recruitment process, please inform us as soon as possible about any disability\-related adjustments or accommodations you may need. **Are you ready to shape your future with confidence? title: UX Researcher
description: **We are looking for our future UX Researcher to join our** **P** **roduct** **team** **s** **in Dublin and our Global** **Customer Experience (CX)** **team!** **The role is offered as hybrid with 2 to 3 days per week onsite, in our Sandyford office.** As a UX Researcher on the Experience Insights (EXI) team (which is part of the larger Customer Experience team ) , you will work closely with designers, product managers and other stakeholders to create and refine digital experiences that improve the lives of our users. Using mixed\-methods research, you’ll support teams by bringing clarity to ambiguity with user insights throughout the product development process, from early\-stage discovery to launch and evaluation. The role is essential in delivering seamless and intuitive user experiences that are grounded in user needs. **What the role entails :** *Product Team Influence* * Develop research plans with clear objectives, participant screeners, moderator guides, recruiting strategies, and timelines * Share research findings with product, design, and executive teams in ways that support decisions * Provide regular project updates, including status, blockers, and open questions * Recommend appropriate research methods and tools based on stakeholder goals * Work with Product Managers to forecast and prioritize research needs *Organizational Influence:* * Join cross\-functional initiatives, representing the user voice and helping to drive continuous improvement * Build user empathy across the organization through readouts and direct feedback *UXR Influence:* * Stay current on industry trends and best practices in UX research **What you bring to the role** *Empathy and User Advocacy :* * You center users in your work. You understand their experiences, emotions, and motivations, and you make sure others understand them too. You use interviews, surveys, and usability tests to uncover what matters most. *Analytical Thinking :* * You are confident in both qualitative and quantitative methods and know when to use each. You turn findings into clear, actionable insights that guide decisions. *Communication and Collaboration* * You present insights in a clear and engaging way. You work closely with cross\-functional teams to make sure research findings lead to meaningful improvements. *Technical Proficiency* * You know how to select and use research tools to manage studies, analyze data, and communicate results. You apply these tools effectively in your day\-to\-day work. *Creativity* * You try new approaches when needed and adapt your communication for different audiences. You help your team explore fresh ways to apply research in their work. **Qualifications and Experience :** *Required:* * 3\-5 years of UX research experience, ideally in health tech or regulated industries * Comfortable conducting research in a fast\-paced, cross\-functional environment * Portfolio that shows past research work and deliverables * Experience with tools such as UserZoom, UserTesting, Qualtrics, Confluence, and Miro or Whiteboard * Strong communication and collaboration skills * Comfortable using AI tools as part of the research workflow * Degree in Human\-Computer Interaction, Psychology, Anthropology, or a related field * Willingness and ability to travel internationally *Preferred:* * Experience working in healthcare or medical devices * Experience conducting research on hardware/physical products **Additional information :** * At Resmed, all employees benefit from a bonus plan, the percentage of which depends on your position within the organisation. * Competitive benefits * Working from home flexibility * You also have access to a referral bonus and to Resmed's preferred shareholding programme * Internal career opportunity \- joining an international fast\-pace and massively growing company **OK, so what's next?** Joining us is more than saying “yes” to making the world a healthier place. It’s discovering a career that’s challenging, supportive and inspiring. Where a culture driven by excellence helps you not only meet your goals, but also create new ones. We focus on creating a diverse and inclusive culture, encouraging individual expression in the workplace and thrive on the innovative ideas this generates. If this sounds like the workplace for you, apply now! \#LI\-EU Joining us is more than saying “yes” to making the world a healthier place. It’s discovering a career that’s challenging, supportive and inspiring. Where a culture driven by excellence helps you not only meet your goals, but also create new ones. We focus on creating a diverse and inclusive culture, encouraging individual expression in the workplace and thrive on the innovative ideas this generates. If this sounds like the workplace for you, apply now! We commit to respond to every applicant.

title: Vendor Masterfile Associate - 10 Month Contract
description: Vendor Masterfile Associate \- Dublin \- Hybrid \- 10 month contract ICON plc is a world\-leading healthcare intelligence and clinical research organization. We’re proud to foster an inclusive environment driving innovation and excellence, and we welcome you to join us on our mission to shape the future of clinical development **The Role:** As part of the Accounts Payable team the Supplier Data Administrator/Vendor Data Associate will be responsible for liaising with suppliers and ensuring that all supplier details are up to date at all times. * Responsible for ensuring timely and accurate processing of new supplier and investigator account set ups. * Timely and accurate processing of system approvals set\-up * Resolution of issues with suppliers, investigators and approvals set\-up **To be successful in the role, you will ideally have:** * Previous Vendor Data Associate experience * Recognise, exemplify and adhere to ICON’s values which centre around our commitment to People, Clients and Performance. * As a member of staff, the employee is expected to embrace and contribute to our culture of process improvement with a focus on streamlining our processes adding value to our business and meeting client needs. * Delivery to customers: Delivers high quality standard and strives for excellence. Acts in a proactive, flexible and responsive manner to customer needs. * Building Relationships: Establishes good relationships internally and externally. * Personal Effectiveness: Operate independently and make a professional and positive impression on others. * Timely and accurate processing of creditor, and investigator set\-up’s. * Resolution of issues with suppliers, investigators and approvals set\-up. * To help with any projects, and process improvement initiatives. **What ICON can offer you:** Our success depends on the quality of our people. That’s why we’ve made it a priority to build a diverse culture that rewards high performance and nurtures talent. In addition to your competitive salary, ICON offers a range of additional benefits. Our benefits are designed to be competitive within each country and are focused on well\-being and work life balance opportunities for you and your family. Our benefits examples include: * Various annual leave entitlements * A range of health insurance offerings to suit you and your family’s needs. * Competitive retirement planning offerings to maximize savings and plan with confidence for the years ahead. * Global Employee Assistance Programme, TELUS Health, offering 24\-hour access to a global network of over 80,000 independent specialised professionals who are there to support you and your family’s well\-being. * Life assurance * Flexible country\-specific optional benefits, including childcare vouchers, bike purchase schemes, discounted gym memberships, subsidised travel passes, health assessments, among others. Visit our careers site to read more about the benefits ICON offers. At ICON, inclusion \& belonging are fundamental to our culture and values. We’re dedicated to providing an inclusive and accessible environment for all candidates. ICON is committed to providing a workplace free of discrimination and harassment. All qualified applicants will receive equal consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, disability or protected veteran status. If, because of a medical condition or disability, you need a reasonable accommodation for any part of the application process, or in order to perform the essential functions of a position, please let us know Interested in the role, but unsure if you meet all of the requirements? We would encourage you to apply regardless – there’s every chance you’re exactly what we’re looking for here at ICON whether it is for this or other roles.

title: Vice President, Accounting
description: Vice President, Accounting At BNY, our culture allows us to run our company better and enables employees’ growth and success. As a leading global financial services company at the heart of the global financial system, we influence nearly 20% of the world’s investible assets. Every day, our teams harness cutting\-edge AI and breakthrough technologies to collaborate with clients, driving transformative solutions that redefine industries and uplift communities worldwide. Recognized as a top destination for innovators, BNY is where bold ideas meet advanced technology and exceptional talent. Together, we power the future of finance – and this is what \#LifeAtBNY is all about. Join us and be part of something extraordinary. We’re seeking a future team member for the role of Vice President, Accounting to join our Finance/Accounting team. This role is located in Dublin, Ireland. In this role, you’ll make an impact in the following ways: * Lead the preparation and review of financial statements, ensuring accuracy and compliance with relevant accounting standards by demonstrating expertise in the field. * Develop and implement accounting procedures to enhance operational efficiency and ensure robust internal controls. * Provide insightful financial analysis and reporting to support strategic decision\-making and business planning initiatives. * Collaborate with cross\-functional teams to align accounting practices with organizational objectives and regulatory requirements. * Mentor and guide junior team members, fostering a culture of continuous learning and professional development. * Stay abreast of industry trends and regulatory changes to ensure compliance and inform strategic planning. At BNY, we are focused on developing AI capabilities across the firm in a responsible and controlled manner. The successful candidate will have opportunities to enhance their AI skills and apply them in support of Finance transformation * To be successful in this role, we’re seeking the following: * Bachelor’s degree in Accounting, Finance, or a related field. * ACA, ACCA, CMA or equivalent professional certification preferred. * Strong analytical and problem\-solving skills with the ability to interpret complex financial data. * Excellent communication and interpersonal skills to build strategic relationships and effectively collaborate with cross\-functional teams. Leadership skills with a focus on mentoring and developing team members. * At BNY, our culture speaks for itself, check out the latest BNY news at: BNY Newsroom BNY LinkedIn Here’s a few of our recent awards: America’s Most Innovative Companies, Fortune, 2025 World’s Most Admired Companies, Fortune 2025 “Most Just Companies”, Just Capital and CNBC, 2025 Our Benefits and Rewards: BNY offers highly competitive compensation, benefits, and wellbeing programs rooted in a strong culture of excellence and our pay\-for\-performance philosophy. We provide access to flexible global resources and tools for your life’s journey. Focus on your health, foster your personal resilience, and reach your financial goals as a valued member of our team, along with generous paid leaves, including paid volunteer time, that can support you and your family through moments that matter. BNY is an Equal Employment Opportunity/Affirmative Action Employer \- Underrepresented racial and ethnic groups/Females/Individuals with Disabilities/Protected Veterans.

title: Warehouse Operative
description: Are you a dedicated and experienced warehouse professional who thrives in a collaborative work environment and takes pride in upholding high standards? If so, an exciting opportunity awaits you at HuntOffice as a Warehouse Operative. **Main Responsibilities:** * Team Player: Play an integral role in ensuring seamless warehouse operations. * Procedural Adherence: Adhere to goods receiving and dispatch procedures. * Picking and Packing: Maintain standards in picking and packing. * Inventory Support: Assist in cycle counting and stock checks. * Performance Excellence: Meet daily SLA and KPI targets consistently. * Safety First: Adhere to health and safety policies at all times. * Efficient Handling: Safely load and unload goods. * Order Organisation: Organise orders and manifests for van routes. * Issue Reporting: Promptly report any delivery and collection issues. **Requirements:** * Punctuality and Reliability: Exhibit excellent timekeeping and attendance. * Safety and Cleanliness: Contribute to maintaining a safe and clean warehouse. * Experience: Possess 2\-3 years of warehousing experience * Communication and Organisation: Showcase strong communication and organisational skills. * Tech Proficiency: Demonstrate basic computer proficiency. * On occasion there might be a need to do a van delivery so a full clean B driver’s license would be an advantage but not essential for this role. * Counterbalance \& Reach Forklift Certificate would be an advantage but not essential for this role. **Additional Information:** * We have one shift available \- 8am to 5pm. * Competitive salary starting from €29,000 per annum depending on experience. * Conveniently located near the Luas line at Cookstown station (5 minutes walk from the warehouse). Also conveniently located near the M50\. **Working Hours** * Monday to Friday business hours. * Occasionally Saturday or extended hours during busy periods so some flexibility will be required. This is a permanent position and will be based at our Cookstown Industrial Estate, Tallaght warehouse, Dublin. Job Types: Full\-time, Permanent Pay: From €29,000\.00 per year Benefits: * Bike to work scheme * Company events * Employee discount * On\-site parking * Wellness program Work authorisation: * Ireland (required) Work Location: In person

title: Workday Functional Specialist(Time-tracking Module, Bangkok / Indian / Malaysia-based, relocation provided)
description: **About Agoda** At Agoda, we bridge the world through travel. Our story began in 2005, when two lifelong friends and entrepreneurs, driven by their passion for travel, launched Agoda to make it easier for everyone to explore the world. Today, we are part of Booking Holdings \[NASDAQ: BKNG], with a diverse team of over 7,000 people from 90 countries, working together in offices around the globe. Every day, we connect people to destinations and experiences, with our great deals across our millions of hotels and holiday properties, flights, and experiences worldwide. No two days are the same at Agoda. Data and technology are at the heart of our culture, fueling our curiosity and innovation. If you’re ready to begin your best journey and help build travel for the world, join us. **Get To Know Our Team** The Global Service Delivery (GSD) team is responsible for Agoda's office infrastructure and staff connectivity. We provide the hardware, software, and access to resources that allow staff to achieve their goals. We operate in 10 different countries, providing 24/7 support to all Agoda employees. Whether in the office, working remotely, or traveling, we enable everyone to stay connected. The GSD team uses state\-of\-the\-art cloud management technologies combined with the best on\-site services to manage the latest hardware running Windows, macOS, and Linux. **The Opportunity:** As a Workday Time Tracking Specialist, you will serve as the primary expert ensuring our Time Tracking module operates seamlessly, scales effectively, and supports accurate payroll and regulatory compliance. While Time Tracking will be your core focus, you will also contribute across other domains and additional HRIS platforms to strengthen our overall People Technology foundation. You’ll partner closely with People and IT teams to deliver scalable solutions, optimize existing processes, and explore new ways Workday and other tools can bring value to the organization. If you’re someone who enjoys solving complex problems, thrives in a collaborative environment, and is eager to expand your skills beyond Workday, this role will give you the platform to do just that. **This role can be based in Bangkok, Gurgaon or Kuala Lumpur** **In this Role, you'll get to:** * Lead configuration and maintenance of Workday Time Tracking, including time entry rules, validation logic, work schedules, time calculations, and compliance requirements. * Partner closely with cross\-functional teams to translate business requirements into scalable Workday solutions that enhance employee experience. * Own and manage reporting, calculated fields, and security configurations that provide accurate insights into time entries, exceptions, overtime, and pay\-impact data. * Own documentation and knowledge sharing, including SOPs, configuration guides, and process maps to strengthen team resiliency. * Gain opportunities to work across other Workday domains and internal tools, expanding expertise based on project needs and personal interests. * Stay informed on new Workday releases and people technology trends, identifying opportunities for enhancement and automation. * Explore and contribute to other internal tools and technologies beyond Workday, expanding your technical footprint within the People Technology ecosystem. **What you'll Need to Succeed:** * 4 \- 6 years of hands\-on Workday experience, with at least 2–3 years focused on Time Tracking configuration, rules, and reporting. * Deep expertise in Time Tracking setup, including time entry codes, calendars, work schedules, validation rules, time calculations (OT, shift premiums, holiday rules), and compliance requirements. * Strong proficiency in Advanced Reporting and Calculated Fields, with the ability to build complex, cross\-domain reports and time\-related analytics. * Solid understanding of HCM fundamentals, covering both business processes (Hire, Change Job, Termination) and configuration concepts (condition rules, orgs, hierarchies, custom objects) * Working knowledge of Workday Security, including constrained/unconstrained roles and segmented security * Proven analytical and problem\-solving skills, with an eye for process efficiency and data integrity. * Excellent communication and collaboration skills, with the ability to influence stakeholders and translate business needs into system design. * Bachelor’s degree in HR, IT, Business Systems, or a related field, or equivalent practical experience. **It's Great if you have:** * Experience with Workday Time Tracking, Absence, or Payroll integrations (EIBs, PECI, or custom interfaces). * Exposure to overtime, shift differential, or country\-specific labor rule implementations in Workday. * Familiarity with testing frameworks, release management, or regression testing for time\-related logic. * Experience working in an international or fast\-paced environment. * Workday certification(s) in Time Tracking, Payroll, or HCM. * Interest in expanding knowledge beyond Workday into other people technology platforms and tools. **What you'll get:** * Medical, dental, mental health support line, annual leave, travel discounts, sick days, and the AgodaChoice flexible spending benefit * Provident fund benefits, and life insurance * Flexibility to help you work in the way that's best for you with a work from home allowance, 30\-day work from anywhere benefit, and hybrid working options. * Unlimited online learning access, several in\-person development programs for each stage of your career, and career paths for management and individual contributors * The chance to live in Bangkok, one of the best cities in Southeast Asia and a central hub for travel. Within 3 hours you can hit any of the most popular countries like Vietnam, Singapore, Laos, Malaysia, and Hong Kong, just to name a few. * A generous relocation package to help you get here title: €15.41ph - Day Shift Security Officer - Dublin 9
description: 📍 **Dublin 9** 🕒 **Fully Flexible, Monday to Sunday, minimum 36 hours per week** 💰 **€15\.41 per hour plus €20\.00 night allowance and Sunday premium** Join Today and Build a Better Tomorrow! At Securitas Security Services we are united by our purpose of helping make your world a safer place, we are committed to ethics, sustainability, and compliance, our core values – Integrity, Vigilance and Helpfulness .We have created a culture where our people are empowered to be the best they can be. We believe in celebrating our differences and creating a supportive environment where all voices are heard. Our colleagues are empowered to think big and push the boundaries of innovation. As a purpose\-led organisation, we aspire to make a difference every day \- and we’re looking for the best and brightest to join our team. Career opportunities to fuel your future, together we can invest in your professional and personal growth. No matter where you are in your career, there are many training and development opportunities. Your career is in your hands, and we will help you maximize your potential! We are always looking to grow too! Join our team and use your talent and passion to help build a safer, healthier, and more efficient world. **Job Description** **Securitas Security Services are currently recruiting Customer Service Security Officers for day shifts only to be based in our large retail client site in Dublin 9\.** **Rate of pay is €15\.41 per hour plus €3\.44 on Sunday and night shift allowance of €20\.00** * Free onsite parking and subsidized canteen facilities and easily accessible by public transport\* **Main Purpose Of Position** To protect our customer’s property, people and/or assets by providing security services to a specific site in direct accordance with the sites published Assignment Instructions (AI’s) and any subsequent changes. **Expectations Of This Position** * Fully and satisfactorily complete all of the training that has been agreed is necessary for the particular site. * Fully and satisfactorily complete all of the tasks and duties on site as defined within the published Assignment Instructions (AI’s). * Proactively refer to and review the published Assignment Instructions, thus familiarising oneself with any changes and in order to suggest changes that can proactively improve or enhance the level of service. * Look out for, and to immediately report, any Health \& Safety hazards, or potential hazards, on site. * Stay alert to any threats or changes within the work environment in order to take the appropriate action in order to reduce the chance of disruption, loss or damage. * Use common sense and initiative by immediately involving others if a situation appears to become threatening. * Complete an accurate, detailed notebook entry as soon as possible after any incident, which can then be used as part of the Incident Report process. * Wear the uniform provided correctly at all times, ensuring all items is clean and pressed. To maintain an excellent level of personal grooming and hygiene at all times. * Maintain a helpful, interested and courteous approach to team members, customer’s staff and the general public, as applicable in order to uphold a highly professional image. **Qualifications** **Security Officer Specification:** * Good English essential * Excellent written and attention to detail skills are essential * Good level of P.C. skills * Previous experience desirable, however training will be provided * Must have excellent communication skills * Be flexible with your working hours. * PSA Licence * Strong Customer Service experience desirable **Additional Information** At Securitas, we believe in rewarding our people for their hard work. We offer a range of great benefits designed to support our people and their families. These comprehensive benefits include discounted health care scheme, company pension, bike to work scheme, access to hundreds of discounts from well\-known brands and retailers, access to hundreds of high\-quality online education courses from some of the world’s leading learning institutions, ways to save for the future, and other resources to improve health and well\-being. **Benefits** * Full\-time 36 hours a week contract of employment with the ability to work up to 48 hours per week * 24 hour EAP (Employee Assistance Programme) free confidential counselling and advice supporting our employees in a number of areas. * Employer Contribution Pension Scheme from the day you join the Company * Progression, training and development opportunities * Christmas Savings Scheme * Discounted Private Healthcare Scheme (Cash back on everyday medical costs, such as dental check\-ups, optical, GP and Emergency Department visits.) * Internal recognition schemes * Bicycle to work scheme from the day you join the Company (Save up to 52% on a bike and accessories) * Employee Refer a friend incentives * Life Assurance equal to one year’s basic pay as per Employment Regulation Order (ERO) * Overtime as per the ERO * Sick Pay Scheme as per ERO * SIPTU Representation Rights * Substantial Maternity Leave top up payment ***Securitas is an equal opportunities Employer*** **P.S.A. Licence Number : 00352** **Website:** https://www.securitas.ie/ **Available jobs:** https://www.securitas.ie/careers/available\-jobs/ **Benefits at Securitas:** https://www.securitas.ie/careers/benefits\-at\-securitas/ **Securitas Integrity Line:** https://www.securitas.ie/sustainability/securitas\-integrity\-line/
